[A01B 69/005] by an additional operator -- A01B 69/005 covers agricultural machines or implements whose steering is performed by a second person in addition to the primary operator, such as a co-driver or assistant who assists with directional control. This subclass addresses systems and methods that enable an extra operator to influence the steering function.
[A01B 69/006] derived from the steering of the tractor -- The subclass A01B 69/006 covers devices, components or control mechanisms that are derived from a tractor's steering system for use in soil-working agricultural machines or implements. It includes any feature that originates in the tractor steering arrangement to influence or improve the operation of related equipment.
[A01B 69/007] Steering or guiding of agricultural vehicles, e.g. steering of the tractor to keep the plough in the furrow -- The CPC entry A01B 69/007 covers systems for steering or guiding agricultural vehicles, such as tractors, to maintain precise alignment of implements like ploughs within the furrow. It relates to the control mechanisms that keep the machine on the intended soil-working path.
[A01B 69/008] automatic -- A01B 69/008 refers to automatic steering of agricultural machines or implements, where the equipment directs its own path without manual input. It falls under the
[A01B 69/02] Ridge-marking or like devices -- A01B 69/02 relates to ridge-marking or similar devices used in soil working, providing visual or physical guides that help steer agricultural machines and implements along the rows or ridges of a field.
[A01B 69/022] Ridge marking by deposition of a chemical substance, e.g. foam, on the surface of the soil -- This CPC subclass covers techniques for marking soil ridges by depositing a chemical substance, such as foam, on the soil surface to create visible or functional lines that aid in crop planting and field management.
[A01B 69/024] adapted to cut and form a ridge or forrow in the soil surface, e.g. with a disc -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code A01B 69/024 pertains to agricultural machinery steering systems specifically designed to cut and shape ridges or furrows in the soil surface, often utilizing discs for precision contouring.
[A01B 69/026] Checkrow wires -- Checkrow wires are field-installed guide lines used to steer planting or cultivation equipment along precise crop rows, ensuring consistent spacing and alignment. They are typically tensioned wires or cords laid in the soil that enable the machine's steering system to follow a predefined path.
[A01B 69/028] the wire being located above the ground -- A01B 69/028 relates to steering systems for agricultural machines where the control wire is positioned above the ground, such as overhead cables or guide wires used to direct implements during soil-working operations. It covers the arrangement, support and routing of these elevated conductors.
[A01B 7/00] Disc-like soil-working implements usable either as ploughs or as harrows, or the like -- A01B 7/00 covers disc-shaped soil-working tools that can serve as ploughs, harrows or similar implements. The entry pertains to rotating disc devices used to cut, turn and break up soil in agricultural or forestry operations.
[A01B 71/00] Construction or arrangement of setting or adjusting mechanisms, of implement or tool drive or of power take-off -- A01B 71/00 covers the design and layout of mechanisms that set, adjust, or control implements and tool drives, as well as power take-off systems, for equipment used in soil working within agriculture or forestry.
[A01B 71/02] Setting or adjusting mechanisms -- A01B 71/02 covers devices that position, calibrate or modify the operation of soil-working implements in agriculture or forestry, such as mechanisms that adjust depth, width, angle or power-take-off settings for ploughs, cultivators and similar tools.
[A01B 71/04] Bearings of rotating parts, e.g. for soil-working discs -- A01B 71/04 covers bearings used on rotating components of agricultural or forestry equipment, such as the discs that work the soil. It includes designs and configurations of bearings that support, guide, or transmit power to these rotating parts, ensuring proper operation and longevity.
[A01B 71/06] Special adaptations of coupling means between power take-off and transmission shaft to the implement or machine -- A01B 71/06 covers special adaptations of the coupling devices that connect a power take-off to the transmission shaft of agricultural or forestry implements. It includes design features that adjust, align, or modify the mechanical link
[A01B 71/063] for enabling transmission coupling and connection between tractor and implements to be controlled from the driver's seat -- A01B 71/063 pertains to soil-working agricultural machinery that includes mechanisms for linking the tractor's power take-off to implements and allowing the coupling, transmission and disconnection to be operated from the driver's seat, enabling the operator to control implement engagement without leaving the cab.
[A01B 71/066] for enabling pitch, roll or yaw movements of trailed implements or machines -- A01B 71/066 relates to mechanisms that permit trailed agricultural implements or machines to be angled in pitch, roll or yaw relative to the tractor, providing adjustable orientation during operation. This sub-class falls under soil-working equipment where the drive or power-take-off is arranged to enable such movements.
[A01B 71/08] Means for protecting against dust or the like, or for cleaning agricultural implements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Discs for ploughs -> A01B15/16 - Lifting or cleaning apparatus for harrows -> A01B19/10 - Discs for harrows -> A01B23/06 - Screening of rotary driven tilling tools -> A01B33/12
[A01B 73/00] Means or arrangements to facilitate transportation of agricultural machines or implements, e.g. folding frames to reduce overall width -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For example, telescopic frames or special details about implement's wheels for transportation.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements of lifting devices for soil working implements -> A01B63/00
[A01B 73/005] for endwise transportation, i.e. the direction of transport being substantially perpendicular to the direction of agricultural operation  A01B73/02  takes precedence -- A01B 73/005 covers devices that enable endwise transport of agricultural machines, meaning the equipment is moved with its operating direction at right angles to the direction of travel, such as folding frames or other arrangements that reduce width for easier road or field movement.
[A01B 73/02] Folding frames -- The A01B 73/02 subclass covers folding frames or similar structures that allow agricultural machines, tools or implements to be collapsed or reconfigured to reduce their overall width for easier transportation or storage. It includes designs such as hinged or telescoping frames that can be folded in situ, facilitating movement through narrow passages or on transport vehicles.
[A01B 73/04] foldable about a horizontal axis -- A01B 73/04 covers means or arrangements that enable agricultural machines or implements to be folded about a horizontal axis for transport, such as folding frames that reduce the overall width of the equipment. This facilitates easier movement on roads and in confined spaces.
[A01B 73/042] specially adapted for actively driven implements -- A01B 73/042 covers means or arrangements specially adapted for actively driven agricultural implements to facilitate transport, such as folding frames, detachable components or adjustable-width mechanisms that reduce the overall envelope of the machine.
[A01B 73/044] the axis being oriented in a longitudinal direction  A01B73/042  takes precedence -- This CPC subclass covers means or arrangements that orient the machine's axis in a longitudinal direction to facilitate transport of agricultural implements, such as folding frames that reduce overall width. It falls under soil working in agriculture, specifically "means or arrangements to facilitate transportation of agricultural machines or implements," and is subordinate to A01B 73/042.
[A01B 73/046] each folding frame part being foldable in itself -- A01B 73/046 relates to agricultural equipment whose transport is aided by having folding frame components that can themselves be folded, such as frames that collapse to reduce overall width. This sub-class covers designs where each part of the frame is independently foldable to facilitate easier movement of machines or implements.
[A01B 73/048] the axis being oriented in transverse direction  A01B73/042  takes precedence -- A01B73/048 covers devices that allow agricultural machines or implements to be transported more easily, such as folding frames that reduce the overall width. The claim is limited to arrangements where the axis of the implement is oriented transversely, and it is subordinate to A01B73/042.
[A01B 73/06] foldable about a vertical axis -- A01B 73/06 covers foldable agricultural implements that fold about a vertical axis to reduce overall width for transport, such as frames
[A01B 73/062] of the type comprising four vertical folding axes each disposed at a substantially fixed location of a supporting frame -- An agricultural transport device for soil-working tools that features four vertical-folding axes mounted at fixed points on a supporting frame, enabling the frame to collapse and reduce its overall width for easier movement and compact storage.
[A01B 73/065] to a position essentially forward of the axis, in relation to the direction of travel -- A01B 73/065 covers arrangements that place parts of an agricultural machine or implement in a position ahead of its longitudinal axis, relative to the direction of travel, to aid transport - for example folding frames that reduce overall width.
[A01B 73/067] to a position essentially rearward of the axis, in relation to the direction of travel -- A01B 73/067 covers arrangements located behind the machine's travel axis that
[A01B 75/00] Carriers for supporting persons when working in the field, e.g. while thinning beet -- This classification covers devices or structures that support a worker's weight or provide a platform while performing field tasks such as beet thinning, including harnesses, seats, or transport frames that enable the person to move or stay upright in the field.
[A01B 76/00] Parts, details or accessories of agricultural machines or implements, not provided for in groups -- A01B 76/00 covers components, sub-assemblies or auxiliary devices for agricultural machinery that are not listed in any other more specific subclass. This includes specialised fittings, mounting brackets, control panels or other accessories designed to enhance or modify the function of farm implements. It applies to any part not otherwise enumerated in the classification.
[A01B 77/00] Machines for lifting and treating soil -- A01B 77/00 covers machines designed to lift, turn, or otherwise treat soil in agricultural or forestry operations. These include tillage equipment, soil aerators and other devices that move or condition the ground to prepare for planting.
[A01B 79/00] Methods for working soil -- A01B 79/00 relates to methods for working soil in agricultural or forestry settings, encompassing techniques that prepare, condition, or modify the ground to enhance plant growth. It includes processes such as tillage, plowing, harrowing, subsoiling and other soil-working operations aimed at improving crop production.
[A01B 79/005] Precision agriculture -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for working soil that involve more complexity and precision than a simple tilling.  Examples are:
[A01B 79/02] combined with other agricultural processing, e.g. fertilising, planting -- A01B 79/02 covers methods that integrate soil working with subsequent agricultural operations such as fertilising or planting, allowing the field to be processed simultaneously or in sequence. It is a subclass of soil working in agriculture or forestry.
[A01B 9/00] Ploughs with rotary driven tools -- A01B 9/00 includes ploughs that employ rotary-driven tools to cut, turn or loosen soil, such as rotavators and rotary cultivators used for primary soil preparation in agriculture. The subclass covers designs where the cutting element rotates rather than being a conventional fixed share.
[A01B 9/003] with tools on horizontal shaft transverse to direction of travel -- A01B 9/003 covers ploughs equipped with tools mounted on a horizontal shaft that runs across the machine, perpendicular to its forward motion. The shaft carries rotary-driven implements such as discs or hoes, enabling soil working while the plough advances.
[A01B 9/006] with tools on horizontal shaft parallel to direction of travel -- A01B 9/006 refers to ploughs equipped with rotary-driven tools that are mounted on a horizontal shaft aligned parallel to the direction of travel, allowing the implement to cultivate soil continuously as the machine moves forward.
[A01C] PLANTING -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Parts, details or accessories of agricultural machines or implements, in general -> A01B51/00 - A01B75/00  **Glossary:** - Plant: Any of a kingdom (Plantae) of multicellular eukaryotic mostly photosynthetic organisms typically lacking locomotive movement or obvious nervous or sensory organs and possessing cellulose cell walls. - Planting: Placement of seed, bulbs or plants into the ground for growth. - Sowing: Placement of seed into or onto the ground for growth. - Fertilising: Application of fertiliser into or onto the ground near seed, bulb or plants to stimulate growth.
[A01C 1/00] Apparatus, or methods of use thereof, for testing or treating seed, roots, or the like, prior to sowing or planting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General methods and apparatuses for treating seeds, involving mechanical means.
[A01C 1/005] Potato seed cutters -- A01C 1/005 covers devices for cutting potato seed pieces, typically tuber sections, to produce uniform planting material. The apparatus is used before sowing to improve handling and emergence of potatoes.
[A01C 1/02] Germinating apparatus -- A01C 1/02 pertains to germinating apparatus designed to test or treat seeds, roots or similar plant material before sowing. The devices provide controlled conditions such as moisture, temperature and light to assess viability or apply pre-plant treatments.
[A01C 1/025] Testing seeds for determining their viability or germination capacity -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Testing, but not obtaining samples for testing, whereby the testing for viability is non-destructive.
[A01C 1/04] Arranging seed on carriers, e.g. on tapes, on cords -- A01C 1/04 relates to arranging seeds on carriers such as tapes or cords to aid planting. The subclass covers methods and apparatus that place seeds on a supporting medium before sowing, improving handling, uniformity and seed-to-soil contact.
[A01C 1/042] Tapes, bands or cords -- This subclass covers the use of tapes
[A01C 1/044] Sheets, multiple sheets or mats -- The CPC subclass A01C 1/044 covers sheets, multiple sheets or mats that are employed to test, treat or protect seeds, roots or similar plant material before sowing. These flat or layered items are used as carriers, filters or protective layers in seed-handling and planting operations.
[A01C 1/046] Carrier compositions -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** This place does not cover: No chemical compositions or formulas classified in this group. Only the composition in respect of disposition of layers and similar, or materials used.
[A01C 1/06] Coating or dressing seed -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical means/machines, e.g. nozzles, used for coating seeds or mechanical aspects of the coating.
[A01C 1/08] Immunising seed -- A01C 1/08 covers apparatus and methods used to immunise seed, roots or similar propagative material before sowing, including treatments that protect against pathogens or pests by inoculation, coating, or other preventive measures. The subclass is part of the planting group within agriculture under human necessities.
[A01C 11/00] Transplanting machines -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** This place does not cover: Machines or means for transplanting of plants or seedlings into the field, not into pots. Planting of elongated parts of plants (like sugar cane buds).
[A01C 11/003] for aquatic plants; for planting underwater, e.g. rice -- The CPC subclass A01C 11/003 covers transplanting machines designed specifically for aquatic plants, enabling planting directly under water such as rice seedlings. It falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture, planting, and transplanting machines, focusing on equipment that operates in submerged conditions.
[A01C 11/006] Other parts or details or planting machines -- This subclass groups any additional components, accessories or specific details of planting machines that are not covered by more specific CPC codes. It includes items such as frames, seed-metering units, adjustment mechanisms and other auxiliary parts used in machines that sow or transplant seeds.
[A01C 11/02] for seedlings -- A01C 11/02 covers equipment designed specifically for transplanting seedlings, including mechanisms that lift young plants from trays or containers and place them into soil with minimal disturbance. The classification falls within the planting subgroup of agricultural technologies in the CPC system.
[A01C 11/025] Transplanting machines using seedling trays; Devices for removing the seedlings from the trays -- A01C 11/025 refers to transplanting machines that use seedling trays and include mechanisms for lifting or removing seedlings from those trays. Such devices automate the transfer of young plants from propagation containers to the field, improving planting efficiency.
[A01C 11/04] for deeper setting or shifting of plants -- A01C 11/04 refers to agricultural machines that place transplanted plants at a deeper depth or shift them after initial planting, enabling more precise positioning of seedlings or cuttings in the soil. This subclass falls under planting equipment within the broader human necessities and agriculture categories.
[A01C 13/00] Machines or apparatus for consolidating soil around plants -- A01C 13/00 includes machines that compact or pack the soil surrounding planted crops to improve root contact, stability and moisture retention. The category covers rollers, plates, tampers and similar mechanical devices used during sowing or transplanting.
[A01C 14/00] Methods or apparatus for planting not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- A01C 14/00 includes methods and apparatus for planting that are not covered by the more specific groups of the A01C subclass, such as unconventional seed placement, timing, spacing or delivery techniques. It is used for novel or atypical planting operations that fall outside existing categories.
[A01C 15/00] Fertiliser distributors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fertiliser distributors combined with seeders -> A01C7/06
[A01C 15/001] with slowly turning wheels -- The subclass A01C 15/001 covers fertilizer distribution apparatus that uses slowly rotating wheels to spread material over the ground. These devices are employed in planting operations to provide a controlled, even application of fertilizer as the wheel turns at a low speed.
[A01C 15/002] with oscillating distributing spouts -- This subclass covers fertiliser distribution devices that include spouts which move back-and-forth to spread material over a field. The oscillating action helps achieve a more uniform application compared with fixed nozzles. Such systems are used in agricultural planting equipment.
[A01C 15/003] Bulk fertiliser or grain handling in the field or on the farm -- A01C 15/003 refers to devices or processes for handling bulk fertiliser or grain directly in the field or on a farm, including storage, transport, distribution and spreading equipment used during planting operations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, planting, and fertilizer distributors.
[A01C 15/005] Undercarriages, tanks, hoppers, stirrers specially adapted for seeders or fertiliser distributors -- The CPC subclass A01C 15/005 covers equipment such as undercarriages, tanks, hoppers and stirrers that are specially designed for use on seeders or fertilizer distributors, enabling efficient transport, storage and mixing of seeds or fertiliser during planting operations.
[A01C 15/006] Hoppers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Both this group and A01C15/007 cover also hoppers for seed.
[A01C 15/007] with agitators in the hopper -- A01C 15/007 relates to fertilizer dispensing apparatus that incorporate agitators inside the hopper to keep the material flowing and prevent clumping, a feature used in agricultural planting systems.
[A01C 15/008] Aprons; Deflecting plates; Band-spreading attachments -- The CPC subclass A01C 15/008 covers components that guide or shape material flow in fertilizer-distribution equipment, specifically aprons, deflecting plates and band-spreading attachments used on spreaders to spread, direct or contain granules during application.
[A01C 15/02] for hand use -- A01C 15/02 covers hand-operated implements for spreading or applying fertilizer, such as portable spreaders, hand-dressed seeders and other devices that allow a user to distribute fertiliser manually over crops or soil. These tools are designed for precise, small-scale application in planting operations.
[A01C 15/04] using blowers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only powder fertilizers, or specific aspects different or not suitable for seeds.
[A01C 15/06] with distributing slots -- A01C 15/06 covers apparatus for dispensing fertilizer that incorporates distributing slots, allowing the material to be released in a controlled pattern onto the soil. The design focuses on devices used by fertilizer distributors to spread granular or powdered products evenly during planting operations. This sub-class fits within the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, planting, and fertilizer distribution.
[A01C 15/08] with pushers or stirrers in the slots -- A01C 15/08 covers fertilizer distributors that incorporate pushers or stirrers positioned in the delivery slots to propel or mix the material as it is released onto the soil, improving uniform distribution during planting operations.
[A01C 15/10] with reciprocating pushers -- A01C 15/10 covers fertilizer-distribution devices that employ reciprocating pushers to move material from a storage unit to the planting zone. The classification includes mechanisms where pushers slide forward and back to dispense measured amounts of fertilizer during sowing operations.
[A01C 15/12] with movable parts of the receptacle -- This subclass concerns fertiliser distributors that incorporate movable parts in the receptacle, such as adjustable or pivoting chambers, enabling variable flow or positioning during application. The design allows more precise and adaptable distribution of fertiliser in planting operations.
[A01C 15/122] with moving floor parts -- A01C 15/122 covers fertilizer-distribution devices that incorporate moving floor parts to convey material across the machine, assisting uniform spreading during planting operations. The moving floor sections transport fertilizer from the hopper to the discharge area, often synchronised with other planting mechanisms. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Planting → Fertiliser distributors.
[A01C 15/124] with adjustable shutters or slits -- The A01C 15/124 subclass covers fertilizer distribution devices that incorporate adjustable shutters or slits to regulate the amount of material released. These mechanisms are used in planting equipment to fine-tune the flow of fertilizer onto crops.
[A01C 15/126] with pushers or stirrers in the slits -- A01C 15/126 covers fertilizer distribution devices that incorporate pushers or stirrers within the delivery slits to move material forward or keep it uniformly mixed. The feature is used in planting equipment to improve the flow and placement of granular or powdered fertilizers as they are applied.
[A01C 15/128] with pushers or stirrers in front of the slits  with endless chains  A01C15/18 -- A01C 15/128 covers fertiliser distributors used in planting that employ pushers or stirrers positioned ahead of the discharge slits, driven by endless chains to evenly spread material. The design ensures continuous, controlled delivery of granular or powdered fertiliser as the implement moves across the field. It falls within the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, planting, and fertiliser distribution.
[A01C 15/14] with means for lifting out the fertiliser -- A01C 15/14 relates to fertilizer distributors equipped with mechanisms for lifting out the fertilizer, such as extraction or elevating devices. It is part of the CPC subgroup for planting tools within the agriculture sector of human necessities.
[A01C 15/16] with means for pushing out the fertiliser, e.g. by a roller -- A01C 15/16 covers fertilizer distribution devices that actively expel the material, typically using a roller or similar pushing mechanism. These systems are mounted on planting equipment and ensure uniform fertilizer placement ahead of or behind the seed rows.
[A01C 15/18] with endless chains -- The A01C 15/18 subclass covers fertiliser-distribution devices that employ an endless chain to move or dispense material. It includes chain-driven spreaders and applicators used in planting operations, defining mechanisms where the chain continuously circulates to transport fertiliser across the field.
[A01C 17/00] Fertilisers or seeders with centrifugal wheels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices using the outward force of a spinning distributor to spread fertiliser or seed across a generally broad area of ground.
[A01C 17/001] Centrifugal throwing devices with a vertical axis -- A01C 17/001 covers centrifugal throwing devices that operate around a vertical axis, used in fertiliser or seeder implements to distribute material outward by rotation. These mechanisms are part of planting equipment that employ centrifugal wheels to spread seeds, fertilizers or other substances over the soil.
[A01C 17/003] Centrifugal throwing devices with a horizontal axis -- A01C 17/003 covers agricultural implements that use centrifugal wheels rotating around a horizontal axis to launch seeds, fertilizer or other particulate material onto the soil. The devices throw the material outward by means of a spinning wheel, providing a controlled spread across a field. This class falls under planting equipment within the broader human necessities sector.
[A01C 17/005] Driving mechanisms for the throwing devices -- A01C 17/005 covers the power-transmission and control systems that drive the throwing mechanisms of centrifugal-wheel seeders and fertiliser spreaders. It includes gears, belts, shafts, motors and related components that move the wheel-mounted disks or discs that launch material onto the field.
[A01C 17/006] Regulating or dosing devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Dosing or regulation of the fertiliser or seed before reaching the rotating disks, i.e. dosing shutters and devices controlling the quantity and location of fertiliser or seed falling onto the rotating disks.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[A01C 17/008] Devices controlling the quantity or the distribution pattern -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Regulation of the fertiliser or seed on or after the rotating disks, i.e. blades, chutes, plates for directing the flux of fertiliser or seed to the ground in a particular quantity or pattern.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.

[A01C 2001/048] Machines -- A01C 2001/048 covers machines used in agriculture and horticulture for soil preparation, planting or harvesting. It includes tractors, cultivators, seeders and other powered equipment that performs field operations. The group groups together all mechanical devices that perform the primary work of cultivating or handling crops.
[A01C 21/00] Methods of fertilising -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Special methods for fertilizing or sowing (like planting patterns or designs), as well as the methods for calculating fertilization requirements.
[A01C 21/002] Apparatus for sowing fertiliser; Fertiliser drill -- A01C 21/002 covers devices designed to place fertilizer into the soil, typically as part of a combined seed-and-fertilizer drill. The class includes mechanisms that meter, distribute and deposit fertilizer at a controlled depth and spacing, often integrated with sowing equipment to improve crop nutrition and yield.
[A01C 21/005] Following a specific plan, e.g. pattern -- A01C 21/005 covers fertilising techniques where the operator follows a predetermined plan or pattern, such as a grid, rows, or other systematic layout, to apply fertilizer to crops. The method dictates the sequence, spacing and timing of application according to that specific scheme.
[A01C 21/007] Determining fertilization requirements -- A01C 21/007 covers methods for assessing the nutrient needs of crops, including soil and plant testing, sensor-based measurements, and analytical models that determine the appropriate type and amount of fertilizer to apply. The aim is to optimize fertiliser use for plant growth while minimizing waste and environmental impact.
[A01C 23/00] Distributing devices specially adapted for liquid manure or other fertilising liquid, including ammonia, e.g. transport tanks or sprinkling wagons -- A01C 23/00 covers devices specially adapted for distributing liquid manure or other fertilising liquids in agricultural planting, including transport tanks and sprinkling wagons designed to handle substances such as ammonia.
[A01C 23/001] Sludge spreaders, e.g. liquid manure spreaders -- A01C 23/001 covers sludge spreaders such as liquid manure spreaders, including transport tanks and sprinkling wagons, used to distribute liquid fertilising substances, including ammonia, across agricultural fields.
[A01C 23/002] provided with auxiliary arrangements, e.g. pumps, agitators, cutters -- A01C 23/002 covers agricultural devices for distributing liquid manure, fertilizer or ammonia that incorporate auxiliary elements such as pumps, agitators or cutters to aid flow, mixing or application, typically found on transport tanks, sprinkling wagons or similar planting equipment.
[A01C 23/003] Distributing devices, e.g. for rotating, throwing -- A01C 23/003 covers devices that spread liquid fertilisers such as manure or ammonia, using mechanisms that rotate or throw the liquid onto fields. Typical examples include transport tanks equipped with spray or sprinkler systems and wagons that disperse the liquids while moving across crops.
[A01C 23/005] Nozzles, valves, splash plates -- A01C 23/005 covers devices used in agriculture for applying liquid manure or other fertilising liquids, such as transport tanks, sprinkling wagons, and the associated nozzles, valves and splash plates that control and disperse the flow. These components are designed to deliver the liquid uniformly and efficiently onto fields.
[A01C 23/006] Distribution of liquid fertilisers by gravity flow -- A01C 23/006 covers devices specially adapted to distribute liquid fertilisers-including manure, ammonia or other fertilising liquids-by gravity flow, such as transport tanks and sprinkling wagons used in planting operations.
[A01C 23/007] Metering or regulating systems -- The CPC subclass A01C 23/007 relates to metering or regulating systems used to distribute liquid manure or other fertilising liquids, including ammonia, through specially adapted devices such as transport tanks or sprinkling wagons.
[A01C 23/008] Tanks, chassis or related parts -- A01C 23/008 covers equipment used to distribute liquid manure or other fertilising liquids, such as transport tanks and sprinkling wagons, together with their supporting chassis or related parts. The subclass includes devices specially adapted for handling ammonia or similar liquids in agricultural applications.
[A01C 23/02] Special arrangements for delivering the liquid directly into the soil -- A01C 23/02 covers devices and systems designed to place liquid manure, fertilising liquids or ammonia directly into the soil, such as injection rigs, transport tanks or wagons equipped with specialised delivery mechanisms that feed the liquid beneath the surface rather than merely sprinkling it above.
[A01C 23/021] Sludge injectors, i.e. liquid manure injectors -- The CPC entry A01C 23/021 covers sludge injectors, also known as liquid-manure injectors, which are devices used to distribute fertilising liquids such as liquid manure or ammonia directly into the soil during planting operations. These systems include transport tanks,
[A01C 23/022] Continuous injection tools -- A01C 23/022, titled Continuous injection tools, covers apparatus for continuously delivering liquid manure or other fertilising liquids during planting operations, such as transport tanks, sprinkling wagons or similar devices that inject the fluid while moving along the field.
[A01C 23/023] for liquid or gas fertilisers -- Devices for applying liquid or gaseous fertilisers, such as transport tanks, sprinkling wagons or other equipment specially adapted to distribute manure, ammonia or similar fertilising liquids, are covered by A01C 23/023. These apparatuses are used in planting operations to spread liquid or gas fertilisers.
[A01C 23/024] for ammonia -- A01C 23/024 covers devices for distributing liquid fertilisers, specifically ammonia, in agricultural planting operations, such as transport tanks, sprinkling wagons or similar apparatuses adapted to handle and apply ammonia as a fertilising liquid.
[A01C 23/025] Continuous injection tools -- The A01C 23/025 subclass covers apparatus that continuously inject fertilising liquids, such as liquid manure or ammonia, onto crops during planting. It includes transport tanks, sprinkling wagons and other devices specially adapted to distribute the liquid directly onto the soil.
[A01C 23/026] Localised non-continuous injection tools, e.g. pal injectors, spike wheels -- The subclass A01C 23/026 covers devices that apply fertilising liquids at discrete points rather than continuously, such as PAL injectors and spike wheels used for localized injection of liquid manure, ammonia or other liquid fertilizers. These tools are designed for precise distribution of nutrients in agricultural planting operations.
[A01C 23/027] Other methods for delivering fertiliser into the soil -- A01C 23/027 covers equipment and techniques for applying liquid fertilisers such as manure or ammonia to soil, including transport tanks and sprinkling wagons, that are not covered by more specific categories. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Human Necessities → Agriculture → Planting.
[A01C 23/028] Contactless injection into the soil -- This CPC subclass A01C 23/028 concerns contactless injection of liquid manure or other fertilising liquids into the soil, using pneumatic or pressure-driven nozzles that deliver the fluid without direct contact with the ground. It falls under distributing devices specially adapted for liquid fertiliser in planting operations.
[A01C 23/04] Distributing under pressure -- This CPC subclass deals with devices that distribute liquid manure or other fertilising liquids under pressure, such as transport tanks, sprinkling wagons and related equipment used for applying these liquids to crops. It includes mechanisms for pressurised spraying and delivery of fertiliser solutions in agricultural settings.
[A01C 23/042] Adding fertiliser to watering systems -- A01C 23/042 relates to devices and methods that add fertiliser to watering or irrigation systems, including tanks, injectors, mixers or sprinkling wagons that combine liquid fertiliser with water for distribution to crops.
[A01C 23/045] Filling devices for liquid manure or slurry tanks -- The CPC entry A01C 23/045 covers devices that fill or load liquid manure, slurry or other fertilising liquids into storage tanks, including transport tanks and sprinkling wagons. These filling mechanisms are designed specifically for handling high-volume, often corrosive liquids used in agricultural fertilisation.
[A01C 23/047] Spraying of liquid fertilisers -- A01C 23/047 covers equipment designed to apply liquid fertilisers, including manure or ammonia, by spraying. It includes transport tanks, sprinkling wagons and similar devices that distribute fertilising liquids onto crops.
[A01C 3/00] Treating manure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  General methods for treating manure for its specific use as an agricultural input.
[A01C 3/02] Storage places for manure, e.g. cisterns for liquid manure -- A01C 3/02 concerns structures or containers for holding animal waste, especially liquid manure, including tanks, pits and cisterns, designed to store the material safely and allow controlled handling and application in agricultural settings.
[A01C 3/021] for composted manure -- A01C 3/021 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the European and United States patent offices, specifies methods or apparatus for treating composted manure, falling under the broader categories of agriculture and planting. This classification focuses on techniques aimed at enhancing or managing the composting process of manure for agricultural purposes.
[A01C 3/023] Digesters -- A01C 3/023 - Digesters relates to equipment that treats animal manure by processing it in anaerobic or aerobic vessels, converting waste into biogas, stabilized fertilizer, or other useful products, and is used in agricultural planting operations for manure management.
[A01C 3/025] with heat recuperation means -- A01C 3/025 refers to agricultural manure-treatment apparatus that recovers heat generated during the process. The classification covers systems which integrate heat-recuperation means, such as heat exchangers or condensers, to capture and reuse thermal energy while composting or otherwise treating animal waste. This improves energy efficiency and can provide auxiliary heating for farm operations.
[A01C 3/026] with mixing or agitating devices -- A01C 3/026 covers methods for treating manure in agricultural planting that employ mixing or agitating devices, such as paddles, rotors or conveyors, to homogenise, aerate or accelerate decomposition, thereby improving nutrient distribution and handling efficiency.
[A01C 3/028] Covers, roofs or other structures for covering manure storage places -- A01C 3/028 relates to structures that protect manure storage, such as covers, roofs or other overhead enclosures. These devices shield the stored waste from weather, contain odors and limit runoff, helping to manage manure safely in agricultural settings.
[A01C 3/04] Manure loaders -- This subclass covers devices that lift, transport and spread animal manure for agricultural use. It includes mechanical and hydraulic loaders, conveyors and similar equipment for moving manure from storage to fields. The focus is on apparatus that handles manure during planting operations.
[A01C 3/06] Manure distributors, e.g. dung distributors -- A01C 3/06 covers devices that spread
[A01C 3/063] Side-spreaders -- The A01C 3/063 subclass covers side-spreaders, devices that distribute manure laterally from a tractor or implement to achieve uniform field coverage. These spreaders eject the material outward from the centre line, improving distribution while reducing soil compaction.
[A01C 3/066] Spreading by using worm or screw conveyors -- A01C 3/066 relates to devices for distributing manure in fields by means of worm or screw conveyors. The classification covers agricultural spreading equipment that transports and deposits organic fertilizer using helical or auger mechanisms, facilitating
[A01C 3/08] for manure already laying on the soil -- A01C 3/08 covers techniques for handling manure that is already deposited on the ground, such as spreading, mixing or incorporating it into the soil to improve fertilisation, reduce odour or accelerate decomposition. The class is applied to agricultural equipment and methods that treat existing field manure rather than fresh or stored material.
[A01C 5/00] Making or covering furrows or holes for sowing, planting or manuring -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ploughs for making ridges -> A01B13/02
[A01C 5/02] Hand tools for making holes for sowing, planting or manuring -- A01C 5/02 covers hand-operated implements designed to create holes or furrows in the soil for sowing seeds, planting seedlings or applying manure, such as dibbers, sowing spikes and similar tools.
[A01C 5/04] Machines for making or covering holes for sowing or planting -- A01C 5/04 classifies agricultural machines designed to create or seal furrows, holes or slots for sowing seeds, planting seedlings or applying manure. It includes equipment that forms the planting openings and devices that cover them, covering both primary soil preparation and later protection of the placed material.
[A01C 5/045] with piercing buckets -- A01C 5/045 concerns agricultural tools that create furrows or holes for sowing, planting or manuring and incorporate piercing buckets that puncture the soil to drop seeds, fertilizer or other material directly into the opening. It covers equipment combining trench formation with a bucket-type inserter.
[A01C 5/06] Machines for making or covering drills or furrows for sowing or planting -- Machines classified under A01C 5/06 are agricultural implements designed to cut or form narrow channels-drills or furrows-in the soil for sowing or planting, and to cover those openings after seed placement. These devices may be towed or self-propelled and often include mechanisms for seed delivery, soil displacement, and subsequent backfilling.
[A01C 5/062] Devices for making drills or furrows -- A01C 5/062 covers equipment designed to create drills or furrows in the soil for sowing, planting or applying manure. It includes machines and tools that form the narrow channels or holes needed to place seeds or fertilizer accurately, and falls under the broader categories of agriculture, planting, and making or covering furrows or holes for sowing.
[A01C 5/064] with rotating tools -- This CPC subclass covers agricultural implements that create or cover furrows or holes for sowing, planting or applying manure using rotating tools. It includes rotary tillage, auger-type seeders and similar equipment designed to turn soil and form rows.
[A01C 5/066] Devices for covering drills or furrows -- A01C 5/066 covers apparatus designed to conceal or protect drilled or furrowed soil after sowing, such as rollers, blankets or other covers that press soil over seeds to improve germination and protect against erosion. This falls under the planting sub-class of agriculture within human necessities.
[A01C 5/068] Furrow packing devices, e.g. press wheels -- A01C 5/068 covers implements that compact or pack the soil in a furrow after sowing, planting or manuring, such as press wheels, rollers or other devices that press the seed-bearing soil to improve seed-soil contact and reduce erosion. These tools are used to improve germination and field conditions by applying pressure to the freshly formed furrow.
[A01C 5/08] Machines for both manuring and sowing or both manuring and planting -- The subclass A01C 5/08 covers agricultural machines that perform fertiliser application together with sowing or planting, allowing manure to be spread while seed or seedlings are placed in the same operation. These devices may be integrated tools or attachments that form furrows, deposit seed and dispense manure simultaneously, improving field-working efficiency.
[A01C 7/00] Sowing -- A01C 7/00 covers the technical features of sowing, including devices and methods for placing seeds or propagules in the soil, the spacing and depth control, and mechanisms that deliver seed material in rows or broadcast patterns. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Planting, and addresses the practical aspects of seed distribution for crop establishment.
[A01C 7/002] Dibble seeders -- A01C 7/002 covers dibble seeders, devices that create a series of holes (dibbles) in
[A01C 7/004] using liquid seeding techniques -- A01C 7/004 covers methods that apply seeds using liquid carriers, such as slurries or suspensions, to improve seed placement and germination efficiency. This includes equipment and processes that mix seeds with water, nutrients or adhesives before sowing.
[A01C 7/006] Minimum till seeding -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tool or combinations of tools minimum till sowing techniques.
[A01C 7/008] Sod or grassland seeding  A01C7/08  takes precedence -- A01C 7/008 covers methods and devices for establishing sod or grassland by seeding, including the preparation, placement and establishment of turf, lawns, pastures or other grass surfaces. It pertains to planting techniques that create continuous grass cover rather than individual seed sowing, and it is used in agricultural applications for landscaping, sports fields and land reclamation.
[A01C 7/02] Hand sowing implements -- A01C 7/02 covers hand-sowing implements
[A01C 7/04] Single-grain seeders with or without suction devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Seeders for planting single seeds at equal intervals typically in laterally spaced rows.  Suction is created by pneumatic means.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.
[A01C 7/042] using pneumatic means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Seed singulators operated under pressure of gas or air.
[A01C 7/044] Pneumatic seed wheels -- A01C 7/044 relates to pneumatic seed wheels, which are sowing devices that use air pressure to convey and dispense seeds from an implement, providing uniform distribution and spacing during planting.
[A01C 7/0443] Seed singulators -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for knocking multiples of seeds from an aperture back into the seed pool such that a single seed remains in the aperture for immediate sowing.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Singulator = 44
[A01C 7/0445] Seed ejectors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for ejecting seeds from apertures for immediate sowing. This place also includes ejectors for forcefully ejecting seed or debris stuck in apertures.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Ejector = 1
[A01C 7/046] with perforated seeding discs -- The A01C 7/046 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers sowing devices that use perforated seeding discs. These discs are machined with holes to regulate the release of seed as the disc rotates, improving distribution uniformity and reducing seed bounce during planting.
[A01C 7/048] Seed tape planters -- A01C 7/048 covers seed-tape planters, devices that dispense seeds arranged on a continuous tape to achieve precise spacing and depth during sowing. These machines automate planting by feeding the tape through the field, allowing efficient, uniform seed placement for crops.
[A01C 7/06] Seeders combined with fertilising apparatus -- A01C 7/06 covers agricultural machines that sow seeds while simultaneously applying fertilizer, integrating seeding and fertilising functions in a single apparatus. These combined seeders are designed to improve efficiency and uniformity of crop establishment.
[A01C 7/08] Broadcast seeders -- Broadcast seeders are devices for sowing seeds by scattering them uniformly over the soil surface rather than placing them in rows. They fall under the CPC subclass A01C 7/08, which is part of the hierarchy Human necessities → Agriculture → Planting → Sowing, and cover mechanisms that distribute seed in a broadcast pattern for crop establishment.
[A01C 7/081] Seeders depositing seeds in rows using pneumatic means -- A01C 7/081 pertains to agricultural seeders that lay seeds in distinct rows by propelling them with air pressure. The pneumatic system delivers seed continuously as the implement advances, providing precise spacing while minimizing mechanical contact with the soil.
[A01C 7/082] Ducts, distribution pipes or details thereof for pneumatic seeders -- A01C 7/082 pertains to the ducts, distribution pipes and related details used in pneumatic seeders for transporting and spreading seed during sowing. These components are designed to provide a uniform flow and precise placement of seed material in agricultural planting equipment.
[A01C 7/084] Pneumatic distribution heads for seeders -- A01C 7/084 covers devices that use air pressure to convey and evenly distribute seeds from a seeder to the soil, typically involving rotating heads or nozzles that control seed flow. These pneumatic distribution heads are components of seed-placement systems used in planting machinery.
[A01C 7/085] Broadcast seeders -- Broadcast seeders are agricultural machines that disperse seed over a field without forming rows, spreading the material uniformly by a rotating disc, fan or fluted drum. They are used for sowing grasses, cereals and other crops where uniform coverage is required and often include seed-metering and soil-contact mechanisms.
[A01C 7/087] Tramlining -- Tramlining refers to the method of guiding planting equipment to lay seeds in precisely straight rows, typically by using fixed markers, guide spikes or visual references on the field. This technique improves uniform plant spacing and enables efficient use of machinery during sowing. It is a specific planting operation within the broader CPC category for agricultural methods.
[A01C 7/088] Sectional seeding -- A01C 7/088 falls under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Planting → Sowing and refers to sectional seeding, a technique in which seed is placed in separate rows or zones of a field to create varied plant densities or mixed species, often using equipment that can adjust spacing and distribution.
[A01C 7/10] Devices for adjusting the seed-box -- Devices for adjusting the seed-box are mechanisms that modify the position, opening or volume of the seed compartment in a sowing implement to regulate seed rate and distribution. Examples include adjustable plates, sliding doors, movable flutes and rakes that can be set to different positions during operation.
[A01C 7/102] Regulating or controlling the seed rate -- A01C 7/102 covers apparatus, systems or methods that adjust the amount of seed delivered during sowing, ensuring a consistent seed rate across a field. It includes mechanisms for monitoring, measuring and varying seed flow in real time to achieve uniform plant spacing and optimal crop density.
[A01C 7/105] Seed sensors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sensors for counting or detecting the passage of seeds.
[A01C 7/107] Calibration of the seed rate -- A01C 7/107 relates to the calibration of the seed rate, covering methods and devices that set and verify the amount of seed delivered per unit area during sowing. It includes adjustments of seed meters, test runs and measurement techniques to ensure uniform plant spacing and optimal yields.
[A01C 7/12] Seeders with feeding wheels -- A01C 7/12 designates agricultural seeders equipped with feeding wheels that transport seed from a hopper to the soil during sowing. The rotating wheels advance the seed into the furrow, providing uniform spacing and depth as the machine moves forward.
[A01C 7/121] for sowing seeds of different sizes -- This subclass covers agricultural implements or methods that sow seeds of different sizes in the same operation, providing separate chambers, openings or adjustable spacing so each seed size can be placed appropriately during sowing. It is used for mixed-crop or varied-size seed planting.
[A01C 7/122] using more than one feeding wheel; using double-run feeding wheels -- A01C 7/122 relates to sowing devices that employ more than one feeding wheel or double-run feeding wheels to move seed forward. The arrangement enhances uniform seed distribution and allows handling of larger seed volumes compared with single-wheel systems.
[A01C 7/123] Housings for feed rollers or wheels -- A01C 7/123 pertains to housings that enclose the feed rollers or wheels of sowing equipment, protecting the components from dirt, debris and weather while guiding seed flow. These protective casings are used in agricultural planting machinery to ensure reliable operation and seed delivery.
[A01C 7/124] with top delivery of the seeds -- A01C 7/124 covers sowing equipment that delivers seeds from a top-mounted dispenser. The class includes seed drills, spreaders or similar machines where seeds are released onto the soil by gravity or pressure from a hopper placed above the furrow or planting area.
[A01C 7/125] with bottom delivery of the seeds -- A01C 7/125 covers sowing equipment that releases seeds through a bottom-oriented delivery system, where the seed flow exits from the lower side of a hopper or tube rather than from the top or sides. This arrangement improves seed placement accuracy and reduces clogging during planting operations.
[A01C 7/126] Stubbed rollers or wheels -- The entry covers sowing devices that use rollers or wheels fitted with short protruding stubs to form furrows or press the seed bed, improving seed placement and soil contact during planting.
[A01C 7/127] Cell rollers, wheels, discs or belts -- A01C 7/127 covers equipment such as rollers, wheels, discs or belts used in sowing to press seed and soil together, create furrows, or convey seed material during planting.
[A01C 7/128] Cell discs -- A01C 7/128 - Cell discs relates to sowing devices in which seeds are arranged on a rotating disc or similar cell-disc structure for uniform distribution. The classification covers apparatus, methods and accessories that use disc-based seed placement to achieve precise planting spacing and depth.
[A01C 7/14] Seeders with spoon or bucket wheels -- A01C 7/14 covers sowing machines that use rotating spoon- or bucket-wheel mechanisms to discharge and place seed material into the soil. These devices belong to the broader class of seeders within agricultural planting equipment and are designed for controlled, even distribution of seed rows.
[A01C 7/16] Seeders with other distributing devices, e.g. brushes, discs, screws or slides -- A01C 7/16 covers seeders that incorporate auxiliary dispensing mechanisms such as brushes, discs, screws
[A01C 7/163] Gravity distributors -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Seeders in which the seeds are downwardly driven only by gravity to the ground.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Deflectors = 70
[A01C 7/18] Machines for depositing quantities of seed at intervals -- A01C 7/18 covers machines that release measured amounts of seed at set distances along a row, ensuring uniform spacing during sowing. These devices typically include mechanisms for metering seed flow and timing the discharge to match the forward travel of the implement. The classification is used for equipment that automates seed placement in agricultural planting operations.
[A01C 7/185] Foot valves -- A01C 7/185 refers to foot valves, devices installed at the lower end of irrigation or planting pipelines that automatically close when water flow stops, preventing backflow and helping maintain consistent sowing conditions.
[A01C 7/20] Parts of seeders for conducting and depositing seed -- A01C 7/20 covers components of seed-drilling machines that guide and release seeds into the soil, such as seed tubes, ducts, distributors, or other conduit parts that conduct seed from the hopper to the point of deposition during sowing operations. These elements are integral to the precise placement of seed in agricultural planting equipment.
[A01C 7/201] Mounting of the seeding tools -- This subclass covers the way seeding implements are attached to planting machines, including brackets, mounts, couplings and other fixation mechanisms. It addresses the arrangement of seed drills, air-seeders or similar seed-delivery tools on frames, shafts or bodies of agricultural equipment.
[A01C 7/203] comprising depth regulation means -- A01C 7/203 covers sowing equipment that includes means for regulating seed-placement depth, such as adjustable plates, rollers, or hydraulic devices that set and maintain a prescribed planting depth during operation. This subclass is used for machines that need precise control of seed depth to ensure uniform germination and crop performance.
[A01C 7/205] comprising pressure regulation means -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for adjusting or maintaining a uniform pressure, even if they have an effect on the depth regulation.
[A01C 7/206] Seed pipes -- A01C 7/206 covers seed pipes, devices that channel seeds from a reservoir to the soil during sowing, often tubular or hollow tools
[A01C 7/208] Chassis; Coupling means to a tractor or the like; Lifting means; Side markers -- The CPC subgroup A01C 7/208 covers features of sowing equipment related to the chassis and to devices that connect the implement to a tractor or similar vehicle, including lifting mechanisms and side-mounted markers. It addresses the structural and coupling arrangements that enable the implement to be mounted, raised, lowered and identified while in operation.
[A01C 9/00] Potato planters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Planting of all types of tubers, bulbs and similar big, round shaped reproductive material.
[A01C 9/02] with conveyor belts -- A01C 9/02 covers potato planters that incorporate conveyor belts to move seed potatoes or tubers through the machine, providing continuous feeding and positioning for planting. The conveyor system facilitates handling of the crop and improves planting efficiency.
[A01C 9/04] with bucket wheels -- A01C 9/04 covers potato planting machines that use a bucket-wheel mechanism to lift and deposit seed tubers. This sub-class falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, planting, and specifically potato planters, defining the technical features of the wheel-type seeding device.
[A01C 9/06] with piercing or grasping devices -- A01C 9/06 covers planting implements, specifically potato planters, that incorporate piercing or grasping mechanisms to position seed tubers. The devices use spikes, hooks or similar elements to penetrate the soil and hold the potatoes in place during planting, improving placement accuracy and speed. This subclass falls under the broader categories of agriculture, planting, and human necessities.
[A01C 9/08] with other distributing devices, e.g. flaps, screws, horizontal turning plates -- A01C 9/08 covers potato planters that incorporate supplementary distributing devices such as flaps, screws or horizontal turning plates to spread seed tubers evenly as they are placed in the soil. These mechanisms aid in uniform planting and improve crop emergence.
[A01D] HARVESTING -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods, apparatus or hand tools for  Separation of useful parts of plants (e.g. seeds, fruits, roots, stalks or leaves) from the remainder of the plants in the place where the plants are grown;  Mowing of plants (e.g. grass or cereals), regardless of whether the intention is to make use of the mown plants or not;  Gathering of whole plants (e.g. root crops or onions) or their useful parts (e.g. fallen fruit) from where the plants grow or from the ground;  Haymaking, i.e. working on mown forage crop lying on the ground with the intention to make hay.  Hand rakes.  Forks  Non-abrasive sharpening devices for scythes, sickles or the like.  Containers for whetstones for use during harvesting.  Machines specially adapted for binding of harvested hay, straw or similar crops in the field.  Arrangements for making or setting of stacks, used in connection with harvesting.  Vehicles specially adapted for carrying harvested crops with means for self-loading or unloading.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Soil working tools or machines for weeding or thinning -> A01B , A01B1/16 , A01B39/18 , A01B41/00 - Transplanting -> A01C11/00 , A01G23/04 - Cutting implements specially adapted for horticultural purposes, e.g. flower shears, fruit shears, secateurs or hedge shears -> A01G3/00 - Delimbing of standing trees -> A01G3/00 - Machines for cutting sods or turf from lawns or meadows -> A01G20/12 - Uprooting or felling of trees -> A01G23/02 - Tapping of tree juices -> A01G23/10 - Apparatus for destruction of unwanted vegetation -> A01M21/00  **Glossary:** - Combine (noun): Harvester or mower combined with a threshing device. - Fork: Hand tool having two or more tines that are used for lifting, digging or pitching. - Harvest (verb): Perform one of the methods defined in the definition statement in order to make use of the separated, mown or gathered plant material. - Hay: Mown forage crop in a dry or partially dry state. - Haymaking: Working on mown forage crop lying on the ground with the intention to make hay. - Mow (verb): Cut down grass, cereals or other standing plants. - Thresh (verb): Separate grains or seeds from harvested plants or plant parts (e.g. cereals, maize/corn, peas, beans or pine cones) by mechanical action (e.g. shaking, stamping, rubbing, beating or applying intermittent pressure). - Plant: Any of a kingdom (Plantae) of multicellular eukaryotic mostly photosynthetic organisms typically lacking locomotive movement or obvious nervous or sensory organs and possessing cellulose cell walls.
[A01D 1/00] Hand implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Human-held tools for harvesting.
[A01D 1/02] Scythes -- A01D 1/02 covers scythes, hand-held cutting tools used in harvesting crops by swinging a curved blade to cut stalks close to the ground. The classification includes variations in blade shape, mounting, and handle design for agricultural harvesting.
[A01D 1/04] Sickles -- A01D 1/04 covers sickles, hand-held tools used for cutting and gathering crops during harvest. The classification falls within the hand implements subgroup of harvesting in the agriculture section of the human necessities field.
[A01D 1/06] Knives -- A01D 1/06 concerns hand-held knives employed in agricultural harvesting. The class covers tools designed for cutting, trimming or processing plant material, such as stalks, fruits, vegetables or other harvested products, and includes various blade shapes and attachment methods used by farmers and orchard workers.
[A01D 1/08] Attaching means for blades -- A01D 1/08 covers the mechanisms used to secure cutting blades to hand-held harvesting tools such as sickles, scythes and similar implements. It includes clamps, screws, pins, bolts, brackets and other fastening devices that attach, adjust or replace the blade on the tool's handle or frame. The class is applied to any attachment system that enables the blade to be mounted, locked in place or easily removed for sharpening or replacement.
[A01D 1/10] with fastening by means of eccentric levers -- This subclass covers hand-operated harvesting tools that are secured to the workpiece using a fastening system that employs eccentric levers. The eccentric lever provides a toggle-like clamping action that can be released and re-engaged quickly.
[A01D 11/00] Other hand implements -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hand implements for harvesting maize -> A01D45/026 - Hand implements for picking of cotton -> A01D46/087
[A01D 11/02] for lifting or cropping beet, potatoes or other root crops -- A01D 11/02 covers hand tools designed to lift or crop beet, potatoes and other root crops. It is classified under other hand implements within the harvesting subgroup of agricultural technology.
[A01D 11/025] Knives for topping beets -- A01D 11/025 covers knives specifically designed for topping beets during harvest, belonging to the "other hand implements" group within agricultural harvesting tools. The subclass addresses the shape, cutting edge and ergonomics needed to remove beet foliage efficiently.
[A01D 11/04] for handling root crops, e.g. shovels, fork-like shovels -- A01D 11/04 covers hand tools designed to handle root crops such as carrots, potatoes and beets. It includes shovels and fork-like shovels used for lifting, moving or turning these crops during harvesting.
[A01D 11/06] Combined implements, e.g. fork and rake -- A01D 11/06 pertains to agricultural hand tools that merge two or more functions into a single device, such as a fork combined with a rake. These combined implements are used in harvesting and crop handling, allowing multiple operations with one tool.
[A01D 1/12] with blades adjustable in several ways -- This group covers hand-held harvesting tools whose cutting blades can be set to different angles, widths, or positions by the user. It includes sickles, scythes, and
[A01D 1/14] Handles -- A01D 1/14 relates to the design and arrangement of handles on hand implements used for harvesting and other agricultural tasks, covering various shapes, sizes and attachment methods that enable the user to grip and operate the tool effectively.
[A01D 13/00] Machines for harvesting root crops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  the plough or blade itself which penetrates into the ground for loosening the root crops.  Example of a potato plough (from US2519761 by John R. Hobbs)
[A01D 15/00] Digging machines with sieve graters but without conveying mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example of digging machine with sieve graters but without conveying mechanisms from US2932354 by Bucher Guyer AG.Masch.
[A01D 15/02] with rigid grates -- These are agricultural machines that excavate soil and pass it through rigid sieve gratings to separate material, but they lack any built-in conveyance system to transport the screened output onward. The rigid grates ensure consistent size classification of the harvested product, requiring manual removal or external handling of the separated material.
[A01D 15/04] with moving or vibrating grates -- A digging machine used in agriculture that includes a sieve-grater to separate soil or crop material, where the grate is designed to move or vibrate for improved screening, and the device does not contain any built-in conveying system to transport the separated material elsewhere. This class falls under harvesting equipment that combines digging and screening functions without material handling.
[A01D 17/00] Digging machines with sieving and conveying mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The sieving mechanism acts as conveying mechanism as well.
[A01D 17/02] with conveyors arranged above the sieving device -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example of conveyor above a sieving device from US1876137 by Carls Engelen.
[A01D 17/04] with conveyors arranged below the sieving device -- A01D 17/04 covers digging machines that incorporate a sieve and a conveyor system, where the conveyors are positioned beneath the sieving device to transport screened material away from the machine. This sub-group falls under harvesting equipment in the CPC hierarchy.
[A01D 17/06] with rollers or disc screens -- A01D 17/06 covers digging machines that incorporate a sieving or separating stage using rollers or disc screens to sort material after excavation. It applies to agricultural harvesters where the conveyed load passes over rotating or disc-shaped members that filter out unwanted particles before further handling.
[A01D 17/08] with shaker type screens -- A01D 17/08 pertains to digging or harvesting machines that incorporate shaker-type screens within their sieving and conveying systems, enabling continuous shaking of the screen to separate soil, debris or harvested material for collection or further processing.
[A01D 17/10] with smooth conveyor belts, lath bands or rake bands -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example of a lath band from EP0212146 by Franz Grimme Landmaschf.
[A01D 17/101] with two superposed conveyor belts -- This subclass covers agricultural digging machines that incorporate a sieving section and a conveying system formed by two conveyor belts arranged one above the other, allowing material to be screened and transferred continuously during harvesting operations.
[A01D 17/12] with bucket conveyors -- A01D 17/12 covers digging machines equipped with bucket-type conveyors that transport excavated material from the digging point to a discharge location. The subclass applies to agricultural harvesters and other earth-moving equipment where a series of buckets moves soil, crops or other bulk material along a conveyor path.
[A01D 17/14] with cylindrical screens -- A01D 17/14 covers digging machines that incorporate cylindrical screens for separating material during or after excavation. The rotating cylindrical screens screen soil, stones, roots and other debris, allowing refined material to be conveyed continuously.
[A01D 17/16] with a conveyor spiral -- A01D 17/16 covers agricultural digging machines that incorporate a spiral conveyor to move material from the digging or sieving area to a collection point. The spiral conveys soil, crop residues, or other harvested material continuously while the machine operates, improving handling efficiency in harvesting operations. This subclass falls under digging machines with sieving and conveying mechanisms.
[A01D 17/18] without a conveyor spiral -- This subclass covers agricultural digging implements that incorporate a sieve or screen to separate material and a conveying system, but where the material is moved without a helical (conveyor) screw. It includes machines that use other methods such as belts, paddles or air flow to transport the screened material. Used for harvesting tasks where a spiral conveyor is not employed.
[A01D 17/20] with several co-operating screens -- A01D 17/20 covers digging or soil-working machines that incorporate multiple cooperating screens for separating harvested material from soil. The screens work together to sieve and convey the product, improving efficiency in root-crop or tuber harvesting operations. This subclass falls under harvesting equipment that combines digging, sieving and conveying functions.
[A01D 17/22] with several co-operating sifter bands -- A01D 17/22 covers digging machines that incorporate multiple cooperating sifter bands to separate material while excavating. The sifter bands work together to sieve and convey soil, roots, or harvest residues, improving separation efficiency in agricultural harvesting operations.
[A01D 19/00] Digging machines with centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Digging machines comprising centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners in order to clean and convey the root crop after having been lifted from the ground.  Example of centrifugal wheels from EP0418985 by Hack
[A01D 19/02] with working tools rotating around a horizontal axis arranged transverse to the direction of travel -- A01D 19/02 classifies digging machines equipped with tools that rotate around a horizontal axis perpendicular to the machine's direction of travel, designed for agricultural harvesting tasks.
[A01D 19/04] with working tools rotating around a horizontal axis arranged parallel to the direction of travel -- The A01D 19/04 subclass covers digging machines that use centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners as working tools, where those tools rotate about a horizontal axis that is aligned parallel to the machine's direction of travel. This includes devices such as rotary cultivators or soil-working implements that employ rotating drums to turn or loosen soil while moving forward.
[A01D 19/06] with scoop wheels or drums -- A01D 19/06 refers to agricultural digging machines that use rotating scoop wheels or drums to lift, move, or discharge soil or crop material. The classification covers equipment where the wheel or drum acts as a continuous scooping element, typically employed for harvesting, soil preparation or earth-moving tasks.
[A01D 19/08] with scoop tine chains -- A01D 19/08 covers harvesting machines that use a centrifugal wheel, drum or spinner equipped with scoop-tine chains to loosen, lift and convey soil or crop material. The chains, arranged like a continuous scoop, enhance digging efficiency and are typical in root-crop and tuber harvesters.
[A01D 19/10] with receiving screens -- Digging machines equipped with centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners that incorporate receiving screens for separating soil, roots or debris from the harvested material. The screens collect the useful portion while allowing unwanted matter to pass through, improving efficiency in agricultural harvesting operations. This subclass covers machines designed for extracting crops such as tubers or root vegetables using rotary mechanisms with integrated screening.
[A01D 19/12] with working tools arranged on an approximately vertical axis -- This subclass covers digging machines that employ centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners whose working tools are arranged around an approximately vertical axis, rotating to break up soil, form furrows or prepare seedbeds for agricultural use.
[A01D 19/14] with one screening wheel -- A01D 19/14 covers digging or cultivating machines that use a centrifugal wheel, drum or spinner fitted with a single screening wheel for separating soil, roots, and other plant material, allowing larger debris to be removed while the machine continues operation.
[A01D 19/16] with several screening wheels -- A01D 19/16 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers digging machines equipped with multiple screening wheels, which are used for separating soil and other materials during agricultural harvesting processes. This classification is specific to machinery that employs centrifugal force for its operation.
[A01D 19/18] with screen tine chains -- A01D 19/18 covers harvesting equipment in the form of digging machines that use centrifugal wheels, drums or spinners and are fitted with screen-tine chains. The chains act as a moving screen to separate soil, residues or crops while the wheel or drum rotates, improving material handling during harvest.
[A01D 2017/103] Constructional details of conveyor belts -- This subclass covers the structural features of conveyor belts, such as the materials and reinforcement layers used, joint and splice designs, drive and tensioning arrangements, and methods for protecting or repairing the belt in agricultural or related handling equipment.
[A01D 2017/105] Driving means for the belt -- A01D 2017/105 covers the driving means for the belt in agricultural machines, including arrangements that transmit power to a belt such as gear, chain, hydraulic or electric drives, and mechanisms for tensioning or controlling belt speed in devices like harvesters or balers.
[A01D 2017/106] Belts with fingers mounted on them -- A01D 2017/106 covers apparatus in which a moving belt is equipped with finger-like protrusions that grip, hold or transport plant material. The fingers are mounted on the belt to maintain spacing and orientation of seeds, seedlings or cuttings during sowing, planting or transplanting operations. This technology is used in agricultural or horticultural machines for precise handling of propagules.
[A01D 2017/108] Belts with paddles mounted on them -- It designates belts that have rigid paddles fixed to them and are used in agricultural or gardening equipment to convey material such as soil, crop residues, or seedlings. The paddles lift and move the material as the belt rotates, providing a simple transport mechanism for soil-working or handling operations.
[A01D 2023/022] Rotatable cleaning devices, e.g. brushes, for removing the rests of vegetal material on the root-crops not yet harvested  with cutters  A01D2023/026 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code A01D 2023/022 pertains to rotatable cleaning devices, such as brushes, designed to remove vegetal material from root crops that have not yet been harvested. This classification is often used in conjunction with cutters, as indicated by the related code A01D 2023/026.
[A01D 2023/024] Devices with fix blades for topping -- A01D 2023/024 covers agricultural implements that use non-rotating, fixed blades to top or trim the upper portions of crops. The devices are typically mounted on tractors or harvesters and are designed to cut stems or foliage at a set height for forage or weed control.
[A01D 2023/026] Devices with movable knives for topping, e.g. rotatable cutting disks -- The CPC class A01D 2023/026 covers agricultural or horticultural devices that use moving knives for topping, such as rotatable cutting disks that trim the tops of plants, fruits, vegetables or similar produce. These mechanisms are designed to shear off the upper parts efficiently during harvesting or processing.
[A01D 2023/028] Cutters rotating around a shaft forming a cutting rotor -- The code A01D 2023/028 covers agricultural or horticultural implements that have a set of cutting blades mounted on a shaft that rotates to form a cutting rotor. The rotating cutter assembly is designed to slice or shear plant material as the device moves across the ground.
[A01D 2033/005] Yield crop determination mechanisms for root-crop harvesters -- A01D 2033/005 covers devices and methods that assess the yield of root crops while harvesting, including sensors, weighing systems, imaging or other measurement techniques integrated into the harvester to determine the amount of crop collected in real time.
[A01D 2034/645] Lifting means for the cutter of the lawnmower mounted at the front of the vehicle for inspection and maintenance -- The code covers devices that raise the cutting unit of a front-mounted lawn-mower on a vehicle so the blade can be lifted away from the ground for inspection, cleaning or repair, facilitating maintenance while the mower remains on the vehicle.
[A01D 2034/6825] being tyre driven -- A01D 2034/6825 classifies agricultural machines whose propulsion is provided by tyre-driven wheels rather than tracks or other means. It applies to tractors, harvesters and related implements that move using rubber-tired wheels. This code is used to differentiate such tyre-propelled equipment within the broader soil-working and cultivation category.
[A01D 2034/6831] being fluid driven -- A01D 2034/6831 classifies agricultural implements whose operation is powered by fluid pressure, such as hydraulic or pneumatic systems, rather than direct mechanical drive. It includes devices in which fluid-driven mechanisms perform tasks like soil working, planting or crop care.
[A01D 2034/6837] Drive connections -- A01D 2034/6837 covers the means that link a power source to agricultural implements, including shafts, couplings, universal joints and other mechanical connections transmitting drive from tractors or machines to attached tools. It addresses the design and arrangement of these drive connections for efficient power transfer in farming equipment.
[A01D 2034/6843] Control levers on the handle of the mower -- A01D 2034/6843 covers agricultural or garden mowers that have control levers mounted on the handle, enabling the operator to adjust functions such as cutting height, blade rotation or engine speed while gripping the machine.
