The classification applies to designs where the levers are integrated into or attached to the handle.
[A01D 2034/907] Control levers for portable mowing machines -- A01D 2034/907 covers portable mowing machines and the control levers that an operator manipulates to start, stop, adjust cutting height or direct the cutting unit. The subclass includes lever mechanisms, ergonomic designs and integrated control systems for hand-held or walk-behind mowers.
[A01D 2046/262] Collecting surfaces extended around a tree in order to catch the fruit -- This subclass covers apparatus or methods that employ a collecting surface arranged to encircle a tree and intercept falling fruit, such as nets, belts or catch frames positioned around the trunk or canopy. It includes designs that extend the surface around the tree to improve fruit recovery and reduce loss.
[A01D 2046/266] Portable devices to shake branches -- A01D 2046/266 pertains to portable apparatus designed to vibrate or shake tree branches to facilitate the detachment and collection of produce such as fruits, nuts, or flowers. These devices are typically hand-held or lightweight and may use mechanical, pneumatic or electric means to generate the shaking motion.
[A01D 2046/268] Devices to shake the branches pneumatically powered -- A01D 2046/268 covers pneumatic devices that shake tree branches to aid harvesting, pest control or fruit thinning. These machines use compressed air to create oscillating forces that dislodge foliage or fruit without direct mechanical contact.
[A01D 2075/203] by stopping driving when lifting frames, e.g. for haymakers -- A01D 2075/203 covers agricultural machines that automatically halt forward motion when a lifting frame is raised, a safety feature used in equipment such as haymakers to prevent the vehicle from moving while the cutting or gathering frame is being lifted.
[A01D 2085/007] Machines directly attached to a baler -- The A01D 2085/007 subclass covers machines that are directly mounted on or integrated with a baler, such as feed devices, conveyors, or pre-press units that supply material to the baling chamber without separate handling. These configurations are intended to streamline the collection and compaction of agricultural or waste material by coupling the feeding mechanism to the baler itself.
[A01D 2085/008] Making groups of bales just at horizontal level for leaving them on the field -- A01D 2085/008 covers methods and devices for arranging harvested bales in horizontal rows on the field after baling, allowing them to be left in place for later collection or processing. The classification includes mechanisms that form groups of bales at the same height without vertical stacking.
[A01D 2087/123] Manual trolleys for bales -- A01D 2087/123 covers manual trolleys designed for transporting bales of hay, straw, silage or other harvested crops. The devices are non-motorised carts or frames that can be pushed or pulled to move bundled material within farms, storage areas or during loading onto larger transport equipment.
[A01D 2087/128] Devices for unrolling or breaking round bales -- A01D 2087/128 covers agricultural equipment designed to unroll or break round bales, such as hay or straw, facilitating their handling, spreading, or further processing. The classification includes mechanisms that unwind the bale or split it into smaller portions for easier use in feeding, sowing or composting.
[A01D 2090/005] Vehicles for transporting bunches of bananas -- A01D 2090/005 refers to vehicles designed specifically for transporting banana bunches, covering equipment for loading, unloading and protecting the fruit during movement from plantations to processing or markets. The classification includes both motorized and non-motorized carriers equipped with features to secure and ventilate banana loads.
[A01D 2090/086] Taking loose material, i.e. before having been baled, into the vehicle -- A01D 2090/086 covers machinery or methods for loading unbaled, loose agricultural material-such as harvested crops, straw, or green waste-directly into a transport vehicle. It includes devices that pick up, gather, or convey the material from the field or storage area without prior compaction or baling, facilitating its movement to a trailer, truck, or other conveyance. This classification pertains to the handling stage before any bundling or baling occurs.
[A01D 21/00] Digging machines with potato-picking implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Digging machines in which the root crop is picked up by a conveying element immediately after having been loosened by a very short plough.  Example from EP0719495 by Amac BV.
[A01D 2101/00] Lawn-mowers -- A01D 2101/00 lawn-mowers are devices for cutting and trimming grass in gardens, parks, sports fields and similar areas. The class covers motor-driven, manual and robotic mowers as well as related accessories for grass cutting, trimming or mulching, and is situated in the harvesting subgroup of agricultural equipment.
[A01D 21/02] with picking tools moved up-and-down -- The code A01D 21/02 designates agricultural digging machines that incorporate implements for picking potatoes or similar tubers, where the picking tools are driven to move vertically. It covers machines that combine soil-disruption functions with up-and-down motion of the pickers to extract the crops.
[A01D 21/04] with rotating picking tools -- A01D 21/04 covers harvesting equipment that incorporates rotating picking tools on digging machines for potato collection. The classification applies to devices where the picking implements turn as they advance through the soil to lift and convey potatoes for further processing.
[A01D 23/00] Topping machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Removing the top part of root crops (e.g. sugar beet).  Example from US2587258 by Wilkinson Read A.
[A01D 23/02] cutting the tops before being lifted -- The code A01D 23/02 covers topping machines that cut the tops of crop plants, such as cereal heads, before the material is lifted for transport. It includes apparatus and methods for removing the upper portions of harvested crops to ease handling and reduce losses.
[A01D 23/04] cutting the tops after being lifted -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Removing the top part of root crops after the root crop has been lifted (i.e. extracted from the soil).
[A01D 23/06] with collecting and depositing devices for the tops -- A01D 23/06 classifies topping machines that not only cut plant tops but also incorporate means for collecting the cut material and depositing it elsewhere, such as bins, conveyors or other storage units, to streamline harvest handling.
[A01D 25/00] Lifters for beet or like crops -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices which lift the root crop from ground level upwards.  Example from US1331957 by Utah Idaho Sugar Co.
[A01D 25/005] Auxiliary devices for the lifters -- A01D 25/005 covers auxiliary devices for lifters used in harvesting beet or similar root crops. These accessories aid the primary lifting mechanism by improving handling, positioning or transport of the harvested material.
[A01D 25/02] Machines with rigid tools -- The A01D 25/02 subclass covers harvesting machines that employ rigid tools, such as fixed blades, forks or tines, to lift beet or similar root crops from the soil. These devices are designed to raise the crops without flexible or articulated elements, facilitating subsequent handling or transport.
[A01D 25/04] Machines with moving or rotating tools -- A01D 25/04 covers harvesting machines that use moving or rotating tools to lift beet or similar root crops. The subclass includes equipment where rotating arms, drums, or conveyors engage the plant and raise it from the ground for collection. It is part of the broader category of lifters for beet-type crops within agricultural harvesting technologies.
[A01D 25/042] with driven tools -- A01D 25/042 covers harvesting devices for beet or similar crops that incorporate powered or mechanically driven tools to lift the plants. These lifters are part of agricultural equipment designed to facilitate removal of root crops during harvest.
[A01D 25/044] with driven rotating tools -- A01D 25/044 covers agricultural harvesting equipment for lifting beet or similar root crops that employs powered rotating tools. The driven rotors are used to detach or elevate the crops from the soil during harvest.
[A01D 25/046] with oscillating tools -- A01D 25/046 covers agricultural harvesters that lift beets or similar root crops using tools that oscillate during operation. The classification includes machines with reciprocating or vibrating mechanisms designed to detach the crops from the soil.
[A01D 25/048] with endless chains -- This subclass covers agricultural harvesting equipment that lifts beet or similar root crops using a continuous chain mechanism. The endless chain drives the lifter bars or carriers to raise the crops from the soil for collection. It falls under the A01D 25 series dealing with lifters for beet or like crops.
[A01D 27/00] Machines with both topping and lifting mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines comprising means for both lifting the root crop from ground level upwards and removing the top part of said crop (simultaneously or not).
[A01D 27/02] with rigid tools -- This subclass covers harvesting machines that combine topping (cutting) and lifting actions and employ rigid tools such as straight blades, forks or tines rather than flexible or rotary implements. It applies to equipment used for crops where a fixed cutting element is needed to sever stems before the material is lifted for collection.
[A01D 27/04] with moving or rotating tools -- A01D 27/04 covers harvesting machines that combine topping and lifting functions and employ moving or rotating tools to detach crops from the plant. The moving or rotating implements, such as rotating blades or oscillating cutters, enable efficient separation of produce while the lifting mechanism raises the material for collection or further processing. This subclass falls under the broader category of agricultural harvesting equipment.
[A01D 29/00] Harvesters for peanuts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Machines explicitly intended for harvesting peanuts
[A01D 3/00] Non-abrasive sharpening devices for scythes, sickles or the like -- This subclass covers devices used to sharpen scythes, sickles and similar cutting tools without employing abrasive materials. It includes mechanisms that hold or guide the implement for honing, polishing or edge refinement by means such as stones, rollers or other non-abrasive means.
[A01D 3/02] with percussive tools -- A01D 3/02 designates non-abrasive sharpening devices for scythes, sickles or similar harvesting tools that employ a percussive action, such as hammer-type or vibrating sharpeners, to restore cutting edges without grinding.
[A01D 3/04] with pressing jaws -- The CPC subclass A01D 3/04 covers non-abrasive sharpening devices for scythes, sickles or similar tools that employ pressing jaws to hold and reshape the cutting edge, rather than using abrasive surfaces. It is situated in the harvesting category of agriculture under human necessities.
[A01D 3/06] with pressing rollers or pressing discs -- This CPC subclass covers devices that sharpen scythes, sickles or similar tools without abrasion, using pressing rollers or discs to reshape the cutting edge. It falls under A01D 3/06 within the harvesting group of the human necessities section.
[A01D 3/08] Details, e.g. scythe anvils, scythe guides -- A01D 3/08 covers non-abrasive sharpening devices for scythes, sickles and similar harvesting tools, including components such as anvils, guides and other details that aid in setting or maintaining a proper cutting edge without using abrasive materials. These elements are used to improve the efficiency and precision of manual harvesting equipment.
[A01D 31/00] Other digging harvesters -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Harvesting asparagus -> A01D45/007
[A01D 31/02] combined with other agricultural machines, e.g. drilling, planting, hay-harvesting machines -- A01D 31/02 covers digging harvesters that are integrated with other farm equipment such as seed drills, planters or hay-harvesting machines, allowing combined functions to be performed by a single implement.
[A01D 33/00] Accessories for digging harvesters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for digging harvesters, i.e. devices which do not form part of the digging and topping arrangements covered by the previous groups ( A01D13/00 - A01D31/00 ), but which also form part of a root crop harvester (e.g. special cleaning devices, drives, etc). See subgroups for some particular accessories which should be classified in one of those and not in this head group.
[A01D 33/02] Foliage-separating mechanisms -- A01D 33/02 relates to mechanisms that separate foliage from harvested material in digging harvesters. These devices remove leaves, stems or other plant parts from the crop after it is lifted, improving product quality and reducing waste.
[A01D 33/04] Stone-separating mechanisms -- Stone-separating mechanisms are attachments for digging harvesters that separate stones and rocks from harvested material, protecting the crop and the equipment. They improve product quality and reduce wear on harvesting machinery.
[A01D 33/06] Haulm-cutting mechanisms -- The CPC code A01D 33/06 covers mechanisms that cut the haulm-the stalks or stems-of harvested crops, typically as part of accessories for digging harvesters. These devices are designed to sever plant material during the gathering process, improving handling and processing efficiency.
[A01D 33/08] Special sorting and cleaning mechanisms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Sorting and cleaning in general (e.g. for removing attached earth).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Devices for removing foliage -> A01D33/02 - Devices for removing stones -> A01D33/04
[A01D 33/10] Crop collecting devices, with or without weighing apparatus -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Collecting devices for root crops which have been lifted from the ground and left on the field.  Tanks and containers for root crop harvesters.
[A01D 33/105] Devices for gathering the crop in a row -- A01D 33/105 covers devices that collect crops arranged in rows, typically mounted on or used with digging harvesters. The devices include mechanisms that lift, convey, or bundle the harvested material while maintaining row alignment, facilitating efficient field harvesting.
[A01D 33/12] Driving mechanisms, with or without motor -- A01D 33/12 relates to driving mechanisms used on accessories for digging harvesters, encompassing gear, chain, hydraulic or other power-transmission systems that move or steer the harvester's digging elements, irrespective of whether a dedicated motor is incorporated.
[A01D 33/14] Lifting or lowering mechanisms for the tools -- A01D 33/14 covers the lifting or lowering mechanisms used on tools attached to digging harvesters, including hydraulic, mechanical or pneumatic devices that raise, lower or adjust the position of harvesting implements. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Harvesting → Accessories for digging harvesters.
[A01D 34/00] Harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals, or other crops -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined with apparatus performing additional operations while mowing -> A01D37/00 , A01D41/00 , A01D43/00
[A01D 34/001] Accessories not otherwise provided for -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Carrying and storage means
[A01D 34/003] Means for cleaning the machine -- A01D 34/003 refers to the means for cleaning harvesters or mowers, encompassing devices that remove plant or soil residues from the cutting and gathering equipment during or after operation. This subclass falls under the broader category of harvesting tools for grass, cereals and other crops.
[A01D 34/005] Mulching means -- A01D 34/005 covers mulching methods used with harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals, or other crops. It includes devices and techniques that spread organic or inorganic material over the soil to conserve moisture, suppress weeds, and protect the seedbed after cutting. The classification falls under the harvesting group of the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[A01D 34/006] Control or measuring arrangements -- This CPC subgroup deals with devices and systems that monitor or regulate the operation of agricultural harvesters and mowers, such as sensors, electronic controllers and feedback mechanisms that adjust cutting speed, cutting height or other performance parameters. It falls under A01D for harvesting machinery.
[A01D 34/008] for automated or remotely controlled operation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Robotic devices (working independently or autonomous)
[A01D 34/01] characterised by features relating to the type of cutting apparatus -- A01D 34/01 covers harvesters or mowing machines whose design is defined primarily by the kind of cutting mechanism employed. It includes patents that specify blade, scissor, rotary or other specific cutting device types used for grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/015] Cutting mechanisms not covered by another group -- A01D 34/015 covers cutting mechanisms for harvesters or mowers that are not classified elsewhere, including unconventional blade shapes, flexible or brush-type cutters, and cutting elements that use fluids or other atypical means. These devices are intended for cutting grass, cereals or other crops but fall outside standard rotary, reciprocating or sickle-bar categories.
[A01D 34/02] having reciprocating cutters -- A01D 34/02 pertains to agricultural harvesters or mowers equipped with reciprocating cutters that move back-and-forth to cut grass, cereals or other crops. This subclass is used for machines where the cutting elements oscillate rather than rotate, typical of certain combine-harvester heads.
[A01D 34/03] mounted on a vehicle, e.g. a tractor, or drawn by an animal or a vehicle -- A01D 34/03 covers harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops that are installed on a vehicle such as a tractor, or that are pulled by an animal or another vehicle, enabling mobile field cutting operations.
[A01D 34/032] drawn by an animal or a vehicle -- A01D 34/032 covers harvesters or mowers that are pulled or driven by an animal or a motor vehicle, used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Harvesting.
[A01D 34/035] with cutter bars driven by a power take-off from the vehicle -- A01D 34/035 covers harvesters or mowers that employ cutter bars driven by a power take-off from the vehicle to which they are attached. The code applies to equipment used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops where the drive is taken directly from the tractor or similar machine.
[A01D 34/037] with additional power-supply -- A01D 34/037 covers harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops that incorporate an auxiliary-power source, such as an additional engine or battery, to drive the cutting or gathering mechanism alongside the main propulsion.
[A01D 34/04] with cutters at the front -- A01D 34/04 refers to harvesters or mowers that employ cutting tools positioned at the front of the machine. The classification covers devices such as rotary or reciprocating cutters mounted ahead of the gathering mechanisms, used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops before they are collected.
[A01D 34/043] for cutting at the side -- A01D 34/043 covers agricultural harvesters or mowers that cut crops from the side of the machine rather than ahead of it. It applies to devices designed for grass, cereals or other field crops where side-cutting blades or discs are used to trim or gather material along the edges of the cutting path. This subclass refines the broader category of harvesters and mowers within the human necessities-agriculture-harvesting hierarchy.
[A01D 34/046] drawn by an animal or a vehicle -- A01D 34/046 designates harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops that are drawn by an animal or a vehicle. The entry covers machine-driven cutting equipment powered by non-self-propelled traction, fitting within the harvesting subgroup of agricultural tools. It is part of the broader human necessities category.
[A01D 34/06] with cutters at the side -- This subclass covers agricultural harvesting machines that incorporate cutting elements mounted on the sides of the main body, enabling sideward cutting of crops such
[A01D 34/065] drawn by an animal or a vehicle -- A01D 34/065 covers harvesters or mowers that are propelled by an animal or a vehicle, rather than by a self-propelled engine. It includes designs where the cutting or gathering equipment is attached to a draft animal, a tractor, or other non-motorised pull device for cutting grass, cereals or similar crops.
[A01D 34/07] with cutters at the back -- A01D 34/07 classifies harvesters or mowers that feature cutting elements mounted at the rear of the machine, so the crop is cut by cutters positioned behind the main frame or transport carriage. This subcategory applies to equipment used for gathering grass, cereals or other crops where the cutting action occurs at the back of the device.
[A01D 34/08] hand-guided by a walking operator -- Hand-guided harvesters or mowers are manually operated devices that a walking operator pushes or pulls to cut grass, cereals or other crops. They provide portable, low-power cutting capability for small-scale or irregular fields where larger machinery is impractical.
[A01D 34/10] with motor driven cutters or wheels -- A01D 34/10 covers agricultural harvesters or mowers that employ motor-driven cutters or rotating wheels to cut grass, cereals or other crops, specifying machines where the cutting action is powered by an engine rather than manual operation.
[A01D 34/125] with means for discharging mown material -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Protection devices -> A01D34/4167 - Combined with means for gathering or loading mown material -> A01D43/06 - Delivering mechanisms for harvesters or mowers -> A01D57/26 - A01D57/30
[A01D 34/13] Cutting apparatus -- A01D 34/13 covers cutting apparatus used on harvesters or mowers for harvesting grass, cereals and other crops. The classification details mechanical devices that trim or shear plant material, including blades, rotary cutters and feed mechanisms that enable efficient field harvesting.
[A01D 34/135] having oppositely movable cooperating knife-bars -- A01D 34/135 refers to harvesting or mowing machines that cut grass, cereals or other crops using oppositely movable cooperating knife-bars, where the cutting elements move toward each other to shear the material.
[A01D 34/14] Knife-bars -- Knife-bars are rows of sharp knives mounted on a supporting bar that serve as the cutting element in harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals and other crops. The knives slice the vegetation as the machine moves forward, and the bar can be adjusted or replaced to suit different crop types or cutting conditions. This component falls under the A01D 34/14 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01D 34/145] Devices for connecting the knife-bars to the driving mechanism -- A device that links the knife-bars of a grass, cereal or other crop harvester to its drive system, ensuring power transmission from the engine to the cutting elements. It typically includes brackets, joints or couplings designed for secure, adjustable connection during operation.
[A01D 34/16] Guides for the knife-bar in the ledger-plate -- A01D 34/16 covers devices that guide the knife-bar within the ledger-plate of harvesters or mowers used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops. These guides ensure correct positioning and alignment of the cutting knives during operation, improving cutting efficiency and safety.
[A01D 34/17] Holding-down devices for the knife -- A01D 34/17 covers mechanisms that secure the cutting knife of a harvester or mower in place during operation. The devices may include clamps, brackets, or other fastening systems that prevent knife displacement caused by crop impact or vibration, ensuring consistent cutting performance and safety. This subclass falls under harvesting equipment for grasses, cereals and other crops.
[A01D 34/18] Guard fingers -- Guard fingers are protective protrusions attached to the front or side of grass, cereal or crop harvesters and mowers. They guide harvested material away from the cutting unit and shield the operator and machine components from striking debris, reducing damage and improving safety during harvesting operations.
[A01D 34/20] Ledger-plate bars -- Ledger-plate bars are rigid metal strips attached to the frame of harvesters or mowers to support and reinforce the cutting or gathering mechanisms, helping maintain alignment and absorb stresses during operation on grass, cereals or other crops. They are commonly used in agricultural machinery to improve durability and performance.
[A01D 34/22] Shoes therefor -- A01D 34/22 covers shoes designed for harvesters or mowers used in grass, cereal or other crop harvesting. These shoes are auxiliary accessories fitted to the cutting unit to protect the ground, reduce soil compaction or guide the machine. They fall under the harvesting subgroup of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01D 34/23] with additional vertical cutter-bar -- A01D 34/23 refers to harvesters or mowers equipped with an additional vertical cutter-bar, used to cut grass, cereals or other crops. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of HUMAN NECESSITIES → AGRICULTURE → HARVESTING.
[A01D 34/24] Lifting devices for the cutter-bar -- A01D 34/24 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers lifting mechanisms specifically designed for the cutter-bar of harvesters or mowers used in agriculture. This classification is part of a detailed system that organizes inventions related to human necessities, particularly in agricultural machinery.
[A01D 34/243] Mechanical lifting devices  A01D34/26  takes precedence -- A01D 34/243 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers mechanical lifting devices used on harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals, or other crops, with A01D 34/26 taking precedence when applicable.
[A01D 34/246] Hydraulic lifting devices -- Hydraulic lifting devices are mechanisms used on harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals, and other crops to raise and lower cutting or gathering components. They typically employ fluid power to adjust the height of the working tools, improving adaptability to varying crop conditions and improving operator control.
[A01D 34/26] manually actuated -- A01D 34/26 covers harvesters or mowers that are powered by direct human effort rather than a motor, such as hand-cranked or foot-pedal devices for cutting grass, cereals or other crops. The classification includes simple, manually actuated mechanisms that convert the operator's motion into cutting action, typically used in small-scale or low-technology farming where electricity or fuel is unavailable.
[A01D 34/27] Devices for disengaging the knife-driving mechanisms during the lifting of the cutter-bar -- Devices that automatically disconnect the knife-driving mechanism when the cutter-bar of a grass, cereal or other crop harvester is lifted, preventing damage or injury during raising or transport. These safety systems are used in harvesters and mowers to halt blade motion as the cutting unit is disengaged.
[A01D 34/28] Adjusting devices for the cutter-bar -- The entry covers mechanisms that change the position, angle, or height of the cutter bar in agricultural harvesters or mowers, allowing adaptation to varying crop conditions or terrain. These devices include hydraulic, pneumatic, or manual adjustment systems that control the cutter-bar's clearance and tilt to improve cutting efficiency.
[A01D 34/283] Adjustment of the cutter bar in a vertical plane, i.e. to adjust the angle between the cutter bar and the soil -- A01D 34/283 covers mechanisms for adjusting the cutter bar of a vertical harvester in the vertical plane, allowing the angle between the cutter bar and the soil to be set. This facilitates optimal cutting performance for grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/286] Adjustment of the cutter bar on a horizontal plane, i.e. to adjust the angle between the cutter bar and the tractor -- The CPC subclass A01D 34/286 covers devices that adjust a mower's cutter bar on a horizontal plane, allowing the angle between the cutter bar and the tractor to be changed for height or cutting-angle control. It is part of the harvesters category within agriculture.
[A01D 34/30] Driving mechanisms for the cutters -- A01D 34/30 relates to the driving mechanisms that power the cutting elements of harvesters or mowers used on grass, cereals and other crops. It includes gear, belt, hydraulic or electric drives that transmit power to cutter bars, rotating blades or other cutting tools.
[A01D 34/305] Balancing -- Balancing refers to mechanisms or methods that keep harvesters or mowers level and stable while cutting grass, cereals or other crops. It includes devices that distribute weight, adjust tilt, or counteract uneven loads to improve cutting performance and reduce operator fatigue.
[A01D 34/32] Connecting-rods for knife-driving mechanisms -- This subgroup covers connecting rods that transmit motion to knife-driving mechanisms in agricultural harvesters and mowers. The rods link a power source to the cutting knives, ensuring proper blade speed and synchronization for grass, cereal or other crop harvesting.
[A01D 34/33] Devices for connecting the rod to the knife-bar -- A01D 34/33 refers to devices that attach the drive rod to the knife-bar on harvesters or mowers used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops. These connectors ensure power transmission from the machine's axle to the cutting blade, enabling efficient harvesting operations.
[A01D 34/34] Devices for connecting the rod to the crank-pin of the driving mechanism -- This subclass covers mechanisms that link the connecting rod to the crank-pin in the drive train of harvesters or mowers, enabling conversion of rotary motion to reciprocating motion for cutting or gathering. It includes couplings

[A01D 34/37] electric -- A01D 34/37 covers harvesting or mowing equipment that is powered by electricity, including electric mowers and harvesters for grass, cereals and other crops. The code applies to machines where an electric motor provides the drive instead of an internal-combustion engine.
[A01D 34/38] fluid -- A01D 34/38 covers harvesters or mowers that use fluid power-hydraulic, pneumatic or other liquid or gas systems-to operate cutting, gathering or conveying elements. It includes machines where fluid drives blades, reels, conveyors or similar components during the harvesting of grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/40] Other details -- A01D 34/40 covers supplementary information related to harvesters or mowers used on grass, cereals or other crops, such as auxiliary features, optional attachments, or specifications that do not fall within the primary functional categories of the device. This subclass captures details like control mechanisms, safety devices, or ergonomic aspects that complement the main harvesting function.
[A01D 34/404] having cutters driven to oscillate in a horizontal plane -- A01D 34/404 designates harvesters or mowers whose cutting elements are driven to oscillate in a horizontal plane, enabling the blades to sweep side-to-side while moving forward. This mechanism is used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops and falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities ► Agriculture ► Harvesting.
[A01D 34/408] and cooperating with counter-cutters -- The A01D 34/408 subclass covers harvesters or mowers that operate together with counter-cutters, devices that cut material from the opposite side of the main cutting element. It pertains to agricultural machines designed for grass, cereals or other crops, improving cut quality and reducing residue.
[A01D 34/412] having rotating cutters -- A01D 34/412 defines harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops that employ rotating cutters, a sub-category of harvesting equipment within the broader agriculture group of the CPC system.
[A01D 34/416] Flexible line cutters -- A01D 34/416 covers agricultural harvesters or mowers that cut grass, cereals or other crops using a flexible line, such as a nylon or plastic cord, instead of rigid blades. These devices rely on the rapid rotation of the line to slice vegetation.
[A01D 34/4161] Means for feeding cutter line -- Means for feeding cutter line are devices that supply and advance a continuous cutting element, such as a flexible line or cable, to the rotating cutter head of mowers or harvesters. The mechanism ensures consistent line positioning for efficient cutting of grass, cereals, or other crops, often using rollers, reels or tension systems to control line feed rate.
[A01D 34/4162] automatically -- A01D 34/4162 refers to harvesting machines that function automatically, encompassing self-propelled or robotic grass, cereal or crop mowers equipped with automatic control for steering, cutting height, or harvesting operations. It classifies devices that perform these tasks without manual intervention.
[A01D 34/4163] by triggered line feedout, e.g. bump-feeding -- A01D 34/4163 defines harvesters or mowers that advance the crop line by a triggered feed-out mechanism, such as bump-feeding, rather than continuous transport. This subclass covers devices used for grass, cereals or other crops where the feeding action is initiated by a mechanical trigger that releases a short segment of material for cutting or gathering. It falls under the broader category of harvesting equipment in the CPC hierarchy.
[A01D 34/4165] Mounting of the cutter head -- A01D 34/4165 covers the mounting of cutter heads on harvesters or mowers used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops. It includes the structures, fastening means and adjustment mechanisms that attach the rotating cutting unit to the machine's frame.
[A01D 34/4166] Mounting or replacement of the lines -- A01D 34/4166 covers the mechanisms and procedures used to mount, install or replace cutting lines or cords on grass, cereal or other crop harvesters and mowers, including devices that secure the line to the cutter head or facilitate its quick change. This subclass addresses the practical aspects of line handling rather than the cutting action itself.
[A01D 34/4167] Protection devices -- Protection devices for harvesters or mowers are safety or shielding components that guard the operator, the machine, and the crop during cutting or gathering operations. They may include guards, shields, cut-off mechanisms, or other apparatus that prevent accidental contact, damage, or loss of material while the equipment works.
[A01D 34/4168] Constructional details of the flexible lines -- A01D 34/4168 pertains to the design and construction features of flexible lines employed in harvesters or mowers for cutting grass, cereals or other crops, covering material composition, attachment methods, tensioning mechanisms and configurations that allow the line to adapt to varying field conditions.
[A01D 34/42] having cutters rotating about a horizontal axis, e.g. cutting-cylinders -- This subgroup covers agricultural harvesters or mowers that use cutting elements rotating about a horizontal axis, such as cylindrical reels or drum cutters, to shear grass, cereals or other crops. The design focuses on rotary cutter mechanisms rather than reciprocating blades.
[A01D 34/43] mounted on a vehicle, e.g. a tractor, or drawn by an animal or a vehicle -- This subclass covers harvesters or mowers that are mounted on a vehicle such as a tractor or are drawn by an animal or vehicle. It relates to equipment used for cutting grass, cereals or other crops, falling under the A01D 34/43 category of the Cooperative Patent Classification.

[A01D 34/53] Helically shaped cutting members -- A01D 34/53 refers to helically shaped cutting members used in harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops. These spiral or twisted blades slice plant material as the machine moves, enhancing cutting efficiency and reducing clogging.
[A01D 34/535] with cutting members pivotally attached to the rotating axle, e.g. flails -- A01D 34/535 classifies harvesters or mowers in which the cutting members are mounted on a rotating axle by a pivot, allowing them to swing outward during operation; typical examples are flail-type cutters used for grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/54] Cutting-height adjustment -- The CPC subclass A01D 34/54 covers devices that allow the operator to set and vary the cutting height of harvesters or mowers used on grass, cereals and similar crops. It includes mechanical, hydraulic or electronic systems that raise or lower the cutting elements to achieve the desired cut level.
[A01D 34/56] Driving mechanisms for the cutters -- This subclass covers devices that transmit power to the cutting elements of agricultural harvesters or mowers, such as gear drives, belts, chains, hydraulic or pneumatic systems, and mechanisms for controlling speed or torque. It relates to the means of moving cutter bars, rotary blades or sickles during the harvesting of grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/57] actuated by advance of the machine -- A01D 34/57 covers harvesters or mowers whose cutting or gathering elements are automatically driven by the forward motion of the machine, using the advance of the vehicle to actuate the working parts rather than a separate power source. This sub-class applies to grass, cereal or other crop harvesters where propulsion and operation are mechanically linked.
[A01D 34/58] electric -- This subclass includes agricultural harvesters and mowers that are powered by electricity, such as electric-driven grass and cereal cutting machines. It applies to any electric propulsion or drive system used in crop harvesting equipment.
[A01D 34/60] fluid -- A01D 34/60 covers harvesting or mowing machines that employ a fluid-such as water, air, or hydraulic liquid-to assist cutting, cleaning or conveying of the crop. The fluid may be sprayed onto the material, used to power a cutting aid, or to transport harvested material within the harvester. This subclass falls under harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops.

It includes auxiliary features, optional arrangements or other detailed aspects of the machine that fall outside the defined functional categories.

[A01D 34/661] Mounting means -- A01D 34/661 covers mounting means for harvesters or mowers used in agriculture, such as devices that secure cutting bars, tines, or other working tools to the frame of a mower, combine, or similar harvesting machine. [A01D 34/664] Disc cutter bars -- Disc cutter bars are rotating steel discs mounted on harvesters or mowers that cut grass, cereals and other crops by shearing vegetation as the disc spins. They replace conventional blades, offering efficient, consistent cutting for a range of agricultural harvesting equipment.
[A01D 34/665] modular -- A01D 34/665 refers to modular harvesters or mowers for grass, cereals or other crops, where the machine is constructed from interchangeable modules that can be assembled, reconfigured or replaced to suit different cutting tasks, crop types or operational scales.
[A01D 34/667] Means for directing the cut crop -- Means for directing the cut crop are devices attached to harvesters or mowers that guide the harvested material after it is cut, ensuring it moves toward collection or processing points. Typical examples include deflectors, guide plates, conveyors or chute systems that control the flow of grass, cereals or other crops.
[A01D 34/668] rotating -- The A01D 34/668 subclass covers harvesting or mowing equipment that employs a rotating mechanism to cut or gather crops. [A01D 34/833] with blades on endless sprocket chains -- This subgroup covers agricultural machines that cut grass, cereals or other crops using a cutting system in which the blades are mounted on a continuous loop of sprocket chain. The endless chain drives the blades around a path, allowing efficient mowing or harvesting.
[A01D 34/835] specially adapted for particular purposes -- A01D 34/835 covers harvesters or mowers used for grass, cereals or other crops that are specially adapted for particular purposes, such as operation on steep slopes, in wet conditions, or with crop-specific cutting widths or mechanisms. [A01D 37/02] with receiving platform and binding apparatus but without elevating canvases -- The subclass A01D 37/02 pertains to reaper-binder machines that incorporate a receiving platform for harvested material together with a binding mechanism to form bundles, while lacking any elevating canvas that lifts the cut crop. It describes equipment that gathers and binds crops directly on the ground.
[A01D 37/04] conveying the stalks in vertical position -- A01D 37/04 covers devices or methods that move harvested stalks while keeping them upright, such as mechanisms that lift and convey cut crops in a vertical orientation for collection or further processing. It falls under the reaper-binder subclass of harvesting equipment.
[A01D 37/06] binding with stalks or straw bands -- The A01D 37/06 entry concerns harvesting tools that bind cut material using natural fibers such as stalks or straw bands. It applies to reaper-binder machines where the binding element is formed from the harvested crop itself rather than metal or synthetic ties, enabling a continuous, low-cost bundling process.
[A01D 39/00] Independent binders, e.g. for hay -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Equipment thereon for binding harvested produce, e.g. knotters -> A01D59/00 - Stationary apparatus or hand tools for forming or binding hay or straw into bundles -> A01F1/00 - Bundling articles -> B65B13/00 - Knotting -> D04G
[A01D 39/005] Binders combined with devices for picking-up the harvest -- This class covers agricultural machines that integrate binding mechanisms with harvest pick-up equipment, enabling simultaneous gathering and bundling of crops such as hay, straw or forage, and includes devices that bind the material as it is conveyed from the field into a handling system.
[A01D 41/00] Combines, i.e. harvesters or mowers combined with threshing devices -- A01D 41/00 pertains to agricultural machines that unite harvesting or mowing functions with threshing mechanisms, such as combine harvesters. These combined devices cut, gather and separate grain in a single operation, enhancing field efficiency.
[A01D 41/02] Self-propelled combines -- A self-propelled combine is a harvesting machine that integrates cutting, gathering and threshing functions in a single vehicle equipped with its own power source, allowing it to move through the field without external traction. It combines the actions of a mower and a threshing device to separate grain from stalks while advancing under its own drive.
[A01D 41/04] Tractor-driven combines -- Tractor-driven combines are self-propelled harvesting machines that integrate mowing, reaping and threshing functions, allowing a single tractor to perform cutting, gathering and grain separation in a single pass through the field.
[A01D 41/06] Combines with headers -- A01D 41/06 covers agricultural machines that integrate a header with a combine harvester, allowing simultaneous cutting and threshing of crops. These combines are designed to harvest grain or forage by performing the harvesting and threshing functions in a single, continuous operation.
[A01D 41/08] Combines with thresh before the stalk is cut -- A01D 41/08 refers to combine harvesters that perform threshing before the stalk is cut, integrating a threshing unit ahead of the cutting mechanism. This design separates grain from the plant earlier in the harvesting process, improving efficiency for crops harvested without prior stalk removal.
[A01D 41/10] Field threshers with windrow pick-up apparatus -- A01D 41/10 covers agricultural machines that combine harvesting and threshing functions and are equipped with a windrow pickup system to collect cut material and feed it directly into the threshing mechanism. These field threshers integrate cutting, gathering and threshing in a single unit, typically for cereals and similar crops.
[A01D 41/12] Details of combines -- A01D 41/12 covers the detailed technical features of combines, which are agricultural machines that integrate harvesting and threshing functions. The entry includes specifications of the threshing, separating, cleaning and handling mechanisms that enable simultaneous cutting, threshing and grain collection in a single unit.
[A01D 41/1208] Tanks for grain or chaff  for threshing machines  A01F12/60 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Emptying, filling tanks  Unloading  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Grain tanks for threshing machines -> A01F12/60
[A01D 41/1217] Unloading mechanisms -- A01D 41/1217 covers unloading mechanisms for combines and similar harvesting machines, such as conveyors, elevators, augers or other devices that transfer harvested crop from the threshing area to a trailer, wagon or storage bin. The subclass addresses the design and operation of these systems to improve efficiency and reduce loss during crop removal.
[A01D 41/1226] Extension mechanisms -- A01D 41/1226 covers extension mechanisms for combined harvesting machines, such as mowers paired with threshing units, that increase the effective working width or adjust the reach of the equipment. The class includes telescopic, hinged or sliding components that can be lengthened, retracted or angled to suit field conditions and improve coverage.
[A01D 41/1235] Platforms for sacking -- This CPC subclass A01D 41/1235 relates to platforms designed to hold sacks or bags that collect harvested material in combines or mower-threshers. It covers the structural elements that support sacking during the harvesting and threshing process.
[A01D 41/1243] Devices for laying-out or distributing the straw -- Devices that spread or arrange straw after it is cut, typically mounted on combine harvesters or mowers-threshers, to lay the straw uniformly for baling, mulching or disposal. The equipment may consist of rotating arms, discs or conveyor belts that distribute straw across the field or into windrows, improving handling and field conditions.
[A01D 41/1252] Anti-dust devices -- Anti-dust devices are apparatuses fitted to combine harvesters or similar harvesting machines to suppress or capture dust generated during cutting, gathering or threshing. They may employ suction fans, filters, baffles or spray systems that limit airborne particles, improving visibility and reducing environmental impact.
[A01D 41/1261] Access devices for personnel -- A01D 41/1261 pertains to equipment that enables operators or maintenance workers to reach, enter, or exit harvesting machines such as combines, mowers or threshers, including ladders, steps, platforms, walkways and other access structures. The code classifies these safety and convenience features within agricultural harvesting technology.
[A01D 41/127] Control or measuring arrangements specially adapted for combines -- A01D 41/127 relates to control or measuring systems specially adapted for combine harvesters, which integrate harvesting and threshing functions. The entry covers automation, monitoring and adjustment devices that manage the operation of these combined agricultural machines.
[A01D 41/1271] for measuring crop flow -- A01D 41/1271 pertains to devices installed in combine harvesters that monitor the rate or volume of crop material moving through the machine, providing real-time flow measurement to optimise threshing and separation.
[A01D 41/1272] for measuring grain flow -- A01D 41/1272 covers measuring grain flow in combine harvesters or other harvesting machines that integrate cutting and threshing functions. It includes sensors, flow meters and
[A01D 41/1273] for measuring grain loss -- This subclass covers devices integrated into combined harvesters or threshers that detect and quantify grain loss during operation, using sensors or measurement systems to monitor throughput and discard rates, helping growers assess performance and reduce waste.
[A01D 41/1274] for drives -- A01D 41/1274 covers the drive mechanisms used in combined harvesting machines, such as harvesters or mowers that incorporate integrated threshing devices. The subclass addresses power transmission, motor arrangements and control systems that enable the coordinated operation of cutting and threshing functions within a single piece of agricultural equipment.
[A01D 41/1275] for the level of grain in grain tanks -- A01D 41/1275 relates to mechanisms that detect or display the amount of grain stored in the tanks of combined harvesters or mowers with threshing units. It includes sensors, sight-glasses or other level-indicating devices used to monitor grain volume during operation.
[A01D 41/1276] for cleaning mechanisms -- The A01D 41/1276 subclass concerns cleaning mechanisms used in harvesters or mowers that are combined with threshing devices. It includes elements such as sieves, cleaning fans, straw walkers and other grain-separating or residue-removing equipment that improve the quality of the harvested material.
[A01D 41/1277] for measuring grain quality -- This CPC subgroup covers devices incorporated in combines or other combined harvesting and threshing machines that assess grain quality during operation. It includes sensors or analytical systems that measure parameters such as moisture, protein content, impurity levels or test weight to inform sorting, processing or yield management decisions.
[A01D 41/1278] for automatic steering -- A01D 41/1278 covers automatic steering systems for combine harvesters or mowers that integrate threshing functions, enabling the machine to adjust its path without driver input. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy for harvesting equipment within human necessities.
[A01D 41/133] Drying devices -- A01D 41/133 pertains to drying devices incorporated in combine harvesters or mowers with threshing units. It covers apparatuses that remove moisture from harvested crops, such as grain-drying systems, airflow or heat generators, and related control mechanisms integrated into the combine.
[A01D 41/14] Mowing tables -- Mowing tables are apparatuses mounted on harvesting machines that support the cut crop as it is gathered by the mower or combine-type harvester, directing the material toward the threshing mechanisms. They enable uniform feeding and improve the efficiency of the cutting and threshing processes in agricultural harvesting equipment.
[A01D 41/141] Automatic header control -- A01D 41/141 relates to automatic header control for combined harvesters or mowers and threshing devices, covering systems that sense crop conditions and adjust the cutting width, height or position of the header using actuators to optimise harvesting performance.
[A01D 41/142] Header drives -- Header drives are mechanisms that transmit power from a combine harvester's engine to the header, enabling the cutting, gathering and threshing functions of the combined harvester or mower-thresher. They include gearboxes, shafts, belts or hydraulic systems that synchronize the header's operation with the main machine. This subclass groups all drive arrangements specifically for the header component of harvesting equipment.
[A01D 41/144] Foldable headers -- A01D 41/144 covers agricultural machines that combine harvesting and threshing functions with a header that can be folded for transport or storage. These foldable headers are used on combines or mowers to reduce width when not in operation, facilitating maneuverability and ease of handling.
[A01D 41/145] Header lifting devices -- This subclass covers mechanisms that raise or lower the header of a combine harvester or mower-threshing machine to adjust cutting height, including hydraulic, pneumatic and mechanical lifting systems and their control arrangements.
[A01D 41/147] Sunflower header attachments -- A01D 41/147 covers attachments for combines or mowers that are specifically designed to cut and collect sunflower heads. These header devices adapt standard harvesting equipment to handle the large, dense flower heads, allowing simultaneous cutting and threshing in sunflower crops.
[A01D 41/148] Telescopic headers -- Telescopic headers are harvesting attachments that can extend their cutting-width, allowing combine harvesters to adjust the header length for different crop rows and conditions, providing flexible and efficient gathering.
[A01D 41/16] Devices for coupling mowing tables to conveyors -- A01D 41/16 covers mechanisms that link a mowing or cutting table to a conveyor in harvesting equipment, enabling harvested material to be transferred directly onto the transport line. The entry includes brackets, clamps, hinges and adjustable couplings that secure the table while allowing the necessary movement for efficient operation.
[A01D 42/00] Mowers convertible to apparatus for purposes other than mowing -- A01D 42/00 covers agricultural mowers that are designed to be reconfigured into other machines for tasks beyond simple mowing, such as mulching, tedding or windrowing. The classification includes mechanisms, attachments or modular components that allow quick conversion, enabling the same equipment to perform multiple harvesting functions.
[A01D 42/005] Mulching  shredders or like cutting implements for comminuting plant waste  per se   A01G3/002 -- Mulching shredders are cutting implements designed to comminute plant waste directly during harvest, producing fine mulch that can be left on the field. They are typically mounted on mowers that can be converted for other agricultural purposes, allowing the same equipment to both cut crops and process residues. This facilitates soil enrichment and reduces the need for separate mulching machinery.
[A01D 42/02] Raking -- A01D 42/02 classifies raking devices that can be attached to, or converted from, mowers for functions other than mowing, such as gathering, leveling or spreading harvested material. It covers mechanisms for rakes, combs or similar tools used in harvesting operations.
[A01D 42/04] Soil working -- A01D 42/04 covers soil-working equipment that can be transformed from a mower into another type of apparatus for non-mowing functions, such as cultivation or tillage. It falls under the harvesting subgroup of agricultural technology within the broader human necessities field.
[A01D 42/06] Sweeping or cleaning lawns or other surfaces -- A01D 42/06 covers devices that sweep or clean lawns, driveways, sidewalks, patios or similar surfaces, typically by using rotating brushes, rollers or suction rather than cutting. The classification applies to mowers that can be converted into cleaning equipment for non-mowing purposes.
[A01D 42/08] Sweeping snow -- A01D 42/08 covers devices that convert mowers or similar harvesting machines into snow-sweeping equipment, including attachments such as rotating blades, brushes, or conveyors that collect and discharge snow from the ground. The classification applies to convertible apparatus designed for clearing snow rather than cutting crops.

The focus is on equipment that performs picking on a per-item basis rather than bulk harvesting.

[A01D 5/00] Containers for whetstones for use during harvesting -- A01D 5/00 covers containers designed to hold whetstones that are used for sharpening harvesting tools in the field. It includes equipment that lifts, sweeps, gathers, or otherwise consolidates these dispersed harvests for further handling or processing.

[A01D 51/005] Loaders for beets, beetleaf or potatoes -- A01D 51/005 covers loaders designed to lift and transport beets, beetleaf or potatoes from the field into collection containers or transport vehicles

It includes any apparatus that gathers, sweeps, or otherwise accumulates cereal kernels, such as wheat, barley or rye, from the soil surface. This subclass falls under the broader hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Harvesting → Apparatus for gathering crops spread on the soil.

This subclass falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities > Agriculture > Harvesting.

[A01D 57/10] using fans -- A01D 57/10 pertains to harvesters or mowers for grass or cereals that incorporate fans, such as for airflow management, grain separation, cooling or dust removal, thereby enhancing harvesting efficiency. [A01D 57/14] Single-platform rakes -- A01D 57/14 covers single-platform rakes used in harvesting equipment for grass or cereal crops. The code classifies the rake as a component of harvesters or mowers, distinguishing it from multi-platform or other gathering devices.
[A01D 57/16] Tilting tables -- A01D 57/16 covers tilting tables used on harvesters or mowers for grass and cereals, enabling the harvested material to be inclined for easier unloading, processing, or transport. The feature typically involves a pivoting mechanism that can adjust the angle of the table while maintaining stability and alignment with the cutting or gathering equipment. This subclass falls under components of harvesters within the broader agriculture and human necessities categories.

It includes designs where belts transport the harvested crop from the cutting zone to collection, storage or further processing units.
[A01D 57/22] for standing stalks -- The subclass covers components of harvesting or mowing machines that are intended to work on crops that remain upright, such as devices that cut or gather standing stalks of grass or cereals. It includes mechanisms like mower decks, reel blades, and other attachments that engage crops without first laying them down.
[A01D 57/24] Grass-boards -- A01D 57/24 relates to grass-boards, the plates fitted to the rear of a mower or harvester that guide cut grass or cereal stalks toward a collection system. These components direct the material, improving gathering efficiency in agricultural harvesting machines.
[A01D 57/26] Plates arranged behind the cutter-bar for guiding the cut grass or straw -- The A01D 57/26 entry covers plates positioned behind a cutter-bar on grass or cereal harvesters and mowers that serve to guide the cut material, such as grass or straw, away from the cutting zone and toward collection or discharge points. These plates are part of the harvester's component system that improves handling and flow of the cut crop.
[A01D 57/28] Windrower attachments with tines -- A01D 57/28 covers windrower attachments equipped with tines that gather and arrange cut grass or cereal crops into rows for subsequent processing. The subclass pertains to the design, configuration and mounting of the tine elements on the windrower.
[A01D 57/30] Rotating attachments for forming windrows -- A01D 57/30 relates to rotating attachments that form windrows on harvesters or mowers used for grass or cereal crops. These components gather cut material into orderly rows to facilitate subsequent collection or processing.

It is a subset of equipment for binding harvested produce within the broader agriculture harvesting category.
[A01D 59/04] Knotters -- Knotters are agricultural devices that automatically tie or knot harvested produce, such as bunches of grapes, strawberries or other crops, into bundles for easier handling and transport. They are mounted on harvesting machinery and form part of equipment for binding harvested produce.
[A01D 59/06] Needles -- A01D 59/06 covers needles employed in equipment for binding harvested produce, such as those used to stitch together stalks, bundles or bags of crops. [A01D 59/08] Discharge arms -- Discharge arms are components of harvesting machinery that convey and release binding material such as twine or netting onto harvested produce. They are typically mounted on the equipment frame and move in sync with cutting or gathering mechanisms to apply the binding material accurately.
[A01D 59/10] Devices for separating cohering sheaves -- A01D 59/10 covers harvesting tools that separate cohered sheaves, such as mechanisms that break apart bundles of stalks or grain that have been bound together during cutting. These devices are used to improve handling and processing of harvested produce by detaching the individual stems without damaging the crop. It falls under equipment for binding harvested produce within the agriculture-harvesting hierarchy.
[A01D 59/12] Containers for the twine -- A01D 59/12 pertains to containers specifically designed to store and dispense twine for binding harvested produce, such as reels, baskets or dispensers that feed twine to binding equipment. It falls under equipment for binding harvested produce within agricultural harvesting.
[A01D 59/14] Sheaf counters -- A01D 59/14 covers devices that count the number of sheaves produced during grain harvesting, typically integrated with binding or gathering equipment. These counters monitor bundle flow to provide real-time yield data, facilitate harvest management and may trigger automatic actions in the binding system.
[A01D 61/00] Elevators or conveyors for binders or combines -- The A01D 61/00 class covers elevators and conveyor systems that are built into binders or combine harvesters to transport harvested material within the machine. It includes mechanisms for raising, lowering or moving crops from the cutting area to storage or further processing components. This subclass falls under the broader harvesting category of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01D 61/002] transversal conveying devices -- **Glossary:** - Transversal: Perpendicular to the direction of movement of the machine.

[A01D 63/00] Outside dividers -- A01D 63/00 covers devices fitted to agricultural harvesting machines that separate or direct material at the outer edges of the working width. Outside dividers are structures positioned beside the main harvesting tool to guide crops away from the side, improve collection efficiency and reduce loss. They are used on combines, reapers and similar equipment to manage material flow at the perimeter of the machine.
[A01D 63/02] Rotating dividers -- A01D 63/02 covers rotating dividers used in harvesting equipment to separate crop material from stalks or other plant parts. The devices employ one or more rotating blades or discs that guide the material into separate streams, improving the efficiency of cutting, threshing or cleaning operations in agricultural harvesting. This subclass falls within the broader category of harvesting tools for human necessities.
[A01D 63/04] Non-rotating dividers -- A01D 63/04 covers non-rotating dividers used in harvesting equipment, i.e., fixed guards, static screens or stationary chutes that separate or guide harvested material without any rotating movement. These devices are employed to direct crops into desired paths during harvest.
[A01D 65/00] Grain-crop lifters -- Grain-crop lifters are machines or attachments used in harvesting to raise standing grain plants from the field so they can be conveyed to threshing or collection equipment. Typical designs include mechanical arms, conveyor belts or rotary devices that gently lift stalks without damaging the heads, improving efficiency in cereal crop harvesting.
[A01D 65/02] Lifting fingers -- Lifting fingers are the articulated components fitted to grain-crop lifters that grasp and raise harvested stalks or ears, enabling efficient removal of the crop from the field while minimizing damage to the grains.
[A01D 65/04] with revolving staffs -- The CPC subclass A01D 65/04 covers grain-crop lifters that employ revolving staffs, meaning rotating arms or shafts that lift harvested material. These mechanisms are used in agricultural harvesting equipment to continuously raise and transport grain crops from the field to collection points.
[A01D 65/06] Lifting devices arranged on the inner side of the cutter-bar -- A01D 65/06 covers lifting devices that are mounted on the inner side of a cutter-bar in harvesting machinery. These mechanisms raise cut grain-crops toward the conveyor or threshing area, improving collection efficiency in combines and similar harvesters.
[A01D 65/08] Guard attachments for the wheels -- Guard attachments for the wheels of grain-crop lifters are protective devices fitted to the wheel rims or spokes to prevent entanglement of crop material, soil or debris, reducing wear and the risk of wheel blockage during harvesting operations.

[A01D 69/03] fluid -- This subclass refers to fluid-based power transmission elements employed in the drive systems of agricultural harvesters or mowers. [A01D 78/002] Drum-turner-tedders with lateral discharge -- A drum-turner-tedding machine equipped with a drum that lifts, turns and spreads cut hay while a lateral discharge opening throws the material sideways, facilitating even drying and collection during harvesting. It combines turning and tedding functions in a single unit to improve efficiency of haymaking operations.
[A01D 78/003] Drum-turner-tedders with throwing wheel -- A01D 78/003 covers drum-turner-tedders equipped with a throwing wheel, used in haymaking to lift, turn and spread cut forage. The rotating drum and attached tines move relative to the machine while the wheel projects the material forward for more even drying and collection.
[A01D 78/005] Turners; Tedders -- This subclass covers agricultural machines that turn or spread cut forage, such as turners and tedders, in which the tines or beaters move relative to the main frame to distribute material. It is applied in haymaking to improve drying and uniformity of the harvested crop.
[A01D 78/006] Rotating turners -- Rotating turners are mechanisms fitted to haymaking machines that spin to overturn cut forage, promoting uniform drying and leveling of the material. They are classified under harvesting equipment where the tines move relative to the machine, facilitating efficient processing of harvested crops.
[A01D 78/007] Control mechanisms therefor -- A01D 78/007 covers the control systems used in haymaking equipment where the tines move relative to the machine, such as mechanisms that adjust, synchronize, or regulate tine motion during harvesting. It includes hydraulic, mechanical, electronic or pneumatic devices that govern the positioning, speed or timing of the tines.
[A01D 78/008] with forks -- A01D 78/008 covers haymaking machines in which the tines that gather or cut the crop are moved by fork mechanisms that shift relative to the machine frame, addressing the specific means of tine actuation within agricultural harvesting equipment.
[A01D 78/02] with tine-carrying bars or equivalent members which interconnect heads rotating about horizontal axes, e.g. of rotary-drum type -- A01D 78/02 covers hay-making machines where the cutting tines are connected by bars or similar members that link heads rotating on horizontal axes, such as rotary-drum harvesters. The classification applies to designs that synchronize tine movement through these interconnecting structures.
[A01D 78/04] the tine-carrying members moving obliquely or at right angles to the direction of travel of the machine -- This CPC subclass A01D 78/04 covers haymaking machines where the tine-carrying members move obliquely or at right angles to the travel direction, allowing tines to be repositioned relative to the main frame for improved crop handling. It
[A01D 78/06] with tine-carrying endless chains or belts -- A01D 78/06 covers haymaking machines whose tines are attached to an endless chain or belt that moves relative to the main frame, allowing the tines to be raised, lowered or repositioned during operation. This mechanism is used in harvesters to improve handling of cut forage.
[A01D 78/08] with tine-carrying rotary heads or wheels -- A01D 78/08 covers harvesting equipment in which the tines are mounted on rotary heads or wheels that move relative to the machine. The classification applies to hay-making devices where rotating tined units gather, lift and transport cut plant material as the machine advances. This sub-group falls under the broader hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture and harvesting.
[A01D 78/10] the tines rotating about a substantially vertical axis -- A01D 78/10 classifies haymaking machines whose tines are mounted so they rotate around a substantially vertical axis, allowing the tines to move relative to the machine during harvesting. This sub-group falls under the broader categories of agriculture, harvesting, and human necessities.
[A01D 78/1007] Arrangements to facilitate transportation specially adapted therefor  A01D78/1021  takes precedence; for other agricultural machines or implements  A01B73/00 ; carriers  A01D80/005 -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Telescopic frames -> A01D78/1021
[A01D 78/1014] Folding frames -- A01D 78/1014 covers folding frames used in haymaking machines where the tines move relative to the machine. The frames can be collapsed for compact storage or transport and then unfolded for operation, allowing the equipment to switch between harvesting and a compact configuration. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → agriculture → harvesting.
[A01D 78/1021] Telescopic frames -- A01D 78/1021 covers telescopic frames used in haymakers where the tines move relative to the machine, allowing the harvesting apparatus to adjust its width or height during operation. The telescoping structure enables the tines to be extended or retracted to accommodate varying crop conditions and improve cutting efficiency. This sub-class falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, harvesting, and haymakers with moving tines.
[A01D 78/1028] Pivotable rotor support arms  A01D78/1007  takes precedence -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements to facilitate transportation specially adapted therefor -> A01D78/1007
[A01D 78/1035] pivotable by orientation of the support wheels -- A01D 78/1035 covers haymakers whose cutting tines are mounted so they can pivot according to the orientation of the support wheels, allowing the tines to move relative to the machine frame for better alignment with the harvested crop. This feature improves flexibility in harvesting by adjusting tine position as the machine turns.
[A01D 78/1042] Steering devices -- A01D 78/1042 covers steering mechanisms for haymaking equipment in which the tines move relative to the machine chassis. It includes hydraulic, mechanical or combined linkages that adjust the direction of the tines during harvesting, enabling precise control of cutting and gathering operations.
[A01D 78/105] Ground support for rotors -- A01D 78/105 covers structures that support the rotors of agricultural harvesters, such as haymakers, to maintain alignment, dampen vibration and ensure reliable cutting action while the tines move relative to the machine. These ground-support elements may be fixed or adjustable and are designed to improve stability and efficiency during harvesting operations.
[A01D 78/1057] Drive mechanisms -- A01D 78/1057 covers the drive mechanisms that power the tines of hay-making machines, enabling the tines to move relative to the main frame during harvesting. These mechanisms include gear trains, belts, hydraulic or pneumatic systems that convert engine output into the reciprocating or rotary motion required for cutting and handling forage.

[A01D 78/1071] Having only one rotor -- A01D 78/1071 classifies haymaking machines equipped with a single rotor, where the tines move relative to the machine, facilitating efficient cutting and gathering of hay. This specific classification is part of the broader Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[A01D 78/1078] Having only one row of rotors arranged on the same horizontal line perpendicular to the advance direction of the machine -- A harvesting device for haymaking that features a single row of rotors aligned on the same horizontal line and positioned perpendicular to the machine's forward motion, with the tines moving relative to the machine's frame. This arrangement simplifies the rotor layout while maintaining effective cutting action.
[A01D 78/1085] Having two rows of rotors on two different horizontal lines perpendicular to the advance direction of the machine -- A01D 78/1085 covers hay-making machines that use two rows of rotors, each placed on a different horizontal line and arranged perpendicular to the machine's direction of travel, so the tines move relative to the machine during harvesting.
[A01D 78/1092] Having more than two rows of rotors on more than two different horizontal lines perpendicular to the advance direction of the machine -- This CPC subclass covers haymaking equipment where the cutting tines are mounted on more than two rows of rotors that are arranged on more than two separate
[A01D 78/12] the tines having an additional movement superimposed upon their rotary movement -- A01D 78/12 classifies haymakers whose tines rotate while also executing an auxiliary motion-such as oscillation, reciprocation, or tilt-superimposed on the rotary action, enhancing cutting or gathering of forage. It belongs to the harvesting group within the broader agriculture category.
[A01D 78/125] by a guiding track -- A01D 78/125 covers harvesting equipment, specifically haymakers, in which the tines are moved relative to the machine by means of a guiding track. The track controls the position or movement of the working tools during harvesting. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture, and harvesting.
[A01D 78/14] the tines rotating about a substantially horizontal axis -- This subclass covers agricultural harvesting machines, specifically haymakers, in which the tines are mounted to rotate about a substantially horizontal axis, allowing the tines to move relative to the machine during operation.
[A01D 78/142] Arrangements for transport by movement of the heads carrying rake arms -- A01D 78/142 covers arrangements that transport the heads of hay-making machines by moving them, usually with tines that shift relative to the machine, allowing the rake arms to be repositioned for efficient harvesting.
[A01D 78/144] in a vertical plane -- The subclass A01D 78/144 refers to haymaking or harvesting machines whose tines are driven to move in a vertical plane relative to the machine body. It covers arrangements where the tines lift and lower, such as reciprocating or oscillating mechanisms that collect, turn or discharge cut material.
[A01D 78/146] in a horizontal plane -- A01D 78/146 classifies haymaking devices whose tines or flails move in a horizontal plane relative to the machine, such as rotary or swinging mechanisms that harvest or turn forage while remaining level with the ground.
[A01D 78/148] Rakes having only one heads carrying arm -- The CPC entry A01D 78/148 refers to harvesting machines that use a rake with only a single head attached to a carrying arm. It includes haymakers whose tines move relative to the machine body for gathering cut crops.
[A01D 78/16] with positive drive of the heads or wheels -- A01D 78/16 classifies harvesting machines, such as haymakers, whose cutting or gathering heads and wheels are powered by a positive mechanical drive-gears, belts, shafts or motor-driven wheels-rather than relying only on the forward motion of the vehicle to move the tines. The entry covers arrangements that actively rotate or propel the heads or wheels to lift, transport or discharge the harvested material.
[A01D 78/18] of the reciprocating tine type -- A01D 78/18 classifies haymaking equipment whose tines move reciprocally relative to the machine's frame. It covers machines in which the tines swing or slide back-and-forth during operation to cut, lift or discharge harvested material. This subclass is used for designs that employ a reciprocating tine mechanism for harvesting hay.
[A01D 78/20] the tines reciprocating in a direction parallel to the direction of travel of the machine -- A01D 78/20 covers haymaking machines whose tines move back-and-forth in a line that runs parallel to the forward motion of the implement, allowing the tines to gather and lift crop material while the machine advances.
[A01D 80/00] Parts or details of haymakers -- The code A01D 80/00 covers individual components and sub-assemblies used in haymakers, such as cutting mechanisms, conveyor belts, pickup rollers, threshing units and adjustment devices. It also includes replacement parts, accessories and detailed designs that enable the machine to cut, gather, and arrange forage into hay.
[A01D 80/005] Carriers for the transport of haymakers  arrangements of the haymaker to facilitate transportation  A01D78/1007 -- A01D 80/005 classifies carriers and transport arrangements designed for moving haymakers, covering the structures and devices that enable safe and efficient relocation of the machine. It is a subcategory of parts or details of haymakers within the harvesting section of agricultural technology.
[A01D 80/02] Tines -- The CPC code A01D 80/02 designates the tines used in haymakers, the metal prongs that comb, lift or spread harvested forage during agricultural harvesting. This entry falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture and harvesting.
[A01D 82/00] Crop conditioners, i.e. machines for crushing or bruising stalks -- A01D 82/00 concerns crop-conditioning equipment used in harvesting, specifically machines that crush or bruise plant stalks to facilitate drying, handling or processing. These devices are employed in agriculture to improve the quality and marketability of harvested crops such as cereals, fibers or forage.
[A01D 82/02] Rollers for crop conditioners -- This subclass covers rollers used in crop-conditioning equipment that crush or bruise plant stalks during harvest, helping to improve drying and handling of the harvested material. The rollers are classified under CPC A01D 82/02.

[A01D 87/0084] Rear loaders -- Rear loaders are agricultural implements mounted at the back of tractors or harvesters that lift, tilt and discharge harvested material such as hay, straw or other field crops. This falls under the broader hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture, and harvesting.
