[A01D 89/001] Pick-up systems -- The A01D 89/001 entry defines pick-up systems used on agricultural machines such as loaders, chaff-cutters, balers and field-threshers to gather harvested material. It includes the design and arrangement of devices that lift, transport or convey crops from the field into the equipment for further processing. This class falls within the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Harvesting.
[A01D 89/002] Rotors -- Rotors are the rotating components that transmit power to the picking mechanisms of agricultural harvesting equipment such as loaders, chaff-cutters, balers and field-threshers, enabling the collection, handling and processing of crops during harvest.
[A01D 89/003] Endless belts -- The CPC subclass A01D 89/003 covers endless belts employed in agricultural harvesting devices such as pick-ups for loaders, chaff-cutters, balers and field-threshers, where the belt forms a continuous loop to convey material.
[A01D 89/004] Mountings, e.g. height adjustment, wheels, lifting devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mounting means for the pick-up in order to connect it to the machine where it is installed (e.g. harverster, loading wagon, etc).
[A01D 89/005] Drives -- A01D 89/005 concerns the drive mechanisms used in harvesting equipment such as pick-ups for loaders, chaff-cutters, balers and field-threshers. It includes power transmission systems - mechanical, hydraulic or electric - that provide the motion required to operate these implements.
[A01D 89/006] Accessories  feeding devices for baling presses  A01F15/10 ; for cutting machines  A01F29/10 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for the pick-up, i.e. devices which do not form part of the pick-up arrangements covered by the previous groups ( A01D89/00 - A01D89/005 ), but which also form part of a pick-up device. See subgroups for some particular accessories which should be classified in one of those and not in this head group.
[A01D 89/007] Devices for preparing the picking-up of the windrow -- The CPC subclass A01D 89/007 covers equipment that prepares windrows for collection by pick-up devices such as loaders, balers, chaff-cutters or threshers. It includes mechanisms that arrange, align or otherwise ready the cut crop before it is lifted or conveyed, improving harvesting efficiency.
[A01D 89/008] Devices cooperating with the pick-up -- A01D 89/008 covers devices that cooperate with the pick-up of harvesting machines such as loaders, chaff-cutters, balers or field-threshers, providing attachment, guidance or synchronization to improve material handling, feeding or positioning during collection.
[A01D 9/00] Forks -- A01D 9/00 pertains to forks employed in harvesting, covering hand-held and mechanical devices for lifting, turning or moving harvested crops such as fruits, vegetables or grains. The subclass includes various designs, materials and configurations aimed at durability and ergonomic use in field conditions.
[A01D 90/00] Vehicles for carrying harvested crops with means for selfloading or unloading -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pick up 6 with feeler wheel 7 and cutting device 11 comprising the conveying rotor 12 and the knife assembly 13 in the cutting frame.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Combined with mowers -> A01D43/06
[A01D 90/02] Loading means -- A01D 90/02 defines the loading means incorporated in vehicles that transport harvested crops, enabling the machines to load or unload the produce autonomously. It covers mechanisms such as conveyors, ramps, grabs or other devices that transfer crops from fields or storage onto the carrier without external assistance.
[A01D 90/04] with additional cutting means -- This subclass includes agricultural transport vehicles that carry harvested crops and feature self-loading or self-unloading mechanisms, together with additional cutting implements for further processing of the material.

[A01D 90/083] Round-bale trailers  trailers in general  B62D -- A01D 90/083 covers round-bale trailers and similar trailers designed to transport harvested crops and equipped with mechanisms for self-loading or unloading. These vehicles are classified under agriculture harvesting equipment for moving and handling round bales.
[A01D 90/10] Unloading means -- A01D 90/10 covers devices that enable a vehicle transporting harvested crops to unload its load without external assistance. The unloading means may include conveyors, augers, chutes or other mechanisms that automatically release the crop into a storage bin, processing unit or the field. This subclass falls under harvesting equipment that combines transport with self-loading or self-unloading capabilities.
[A01D 90/105] Automatic side-emptying forage wagons -- A01D 90/105 concerns automatic side-emptying forage wagons, vehicles used in harvesting that carry crops and feature built-in mechanisms for self-loading and side unloading of the material without manual intervention.
[A01D 90/12] with additional devices or implements -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Additional devices or implements for the vehicles for carrying harvested crops with means for self-loading or unloading, i.e. devices which do not form part of the aspects covered by the previous groups ( A01D90/00 - A01D90/105 , A01D90/14 and A01D90/16 ), but which also form part of such vehicles (e.g. a drying mechanism for treating the product loaded onto the vehicle).
[A01D 90/14] Adaptations of gearing for driving, loading or unloading means -- The A01D 90/14 subclass pertains to gear-related adaptations employed in self-propelled vehicles that carry harvested crops, specifically those that drive the vehicle or operate its loading and unloading mechanisms. It includes gear reductions, power-train configurations and gear-controlled actuators designed to enable automatic loading, unloading or propulsion of the crop-carrying equipment.
[A01D 90/16] self-propelled -- A01D 90/16 covers self-propelled machines that transport harvested crops and are equipped with built-in mechanisms for loading or unloading the produce. These vehicles are used in agricultural harvesting operations to move crops without external assistance.
[A01D 9/02] with tines rigidly connected to the handle -- A01D 9/02 covers harvesting tools whose forks have tines permanently fixed to the handle, such as pitchforks used for lifting, turning or spreading loose material like hay, straw, compost or harvested crops. The rigid connection distinguishes them from detachable-tine implements.
[A01D 9/04] with exchangeable tines -- A01D 9/04 covers forks whose tines can be removed and replaced, allowing the operator to change tine spacing, length or material to suit different crops, soils or tasks. This interchangeability improves adaptability and efficiency in harvesting operations.
[A01D 9/06] combined with strippers, grippers or the like -- A01D 9/06 covers harvesting devices that incorporate fork elements together with stripping or gripping mechanisms such as strippers, grippers or similar attachments. The classification applies to tools that use forks to lift or handle crops while simultaneously removing them from stems or other supporting structures, improving efficiency in the gathering process.
[A01D 91/00] Methods for harvesting agricultural products -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods for harvesting, but not particular machines or devices. Said methods must be characterised by a number of steps or procedures to be followed during harvesting (e.g. a method for harvesting sugar cane consisting of cutting the plant at 20%, 50% and 70% of its height, letting the different pieces dry on the ground for two days on different windrows, and collecting them separately).
[A01D 91/02] Products growing in the soil -- A01D 91/02 covers agricultural products that develop in soil, such as field crops, vegetables, fruits, tubers and grains, as well as any harvested produce derived directly from soil-grown plants. It refers to the material itself rather than the methods or equipment used to cultivate or harvest it.
[A01D 91/04] Products growing above the soil -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fruit, hops -> A01D46/00
[A01D 93/00] Harvesting apparatus not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- A01D 93/00 covers harvesting machines or tools that are not classified in any other subgroup of the harvesting-apparatus subclass, encompassing novel, unconventional or multifunctional devices that do not fit existing categories.
[A01F] PROCESSING OF HARVESTED PRODUCE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The scope of this subclass is composed of five separate parts, relating to the five parts of the subclass title. For ease of reference each section of these definitions is separated under five corresponding simplified headings: "Threshing", "Baling", "Bundling", "Cutting" and "Storing".  Threshing (relating to main groups A01F5/00 - A01F12/00 and A01F17/00 - A01F21/00 ):  Methods and apparatus for separating grains or seeds from harvested plants or plant parts by mechanical action.  Baling (relating to main groups A01F13/00 - A01F21/00 ):  Methods and apparatus for forming bales of straw, hay or like harvested products of plant origin.  Bundling (relating to main group A01F1/00 ):  Stationary apparatus or hand tools for forming or binding bundles of straw, hay or like harvested products of plant origin.  Cutting (relating to main groups A01F3/00 and A01F29/00 ):  Apparatus specially adapted for cutting or disintegrating straw, hay or like already harvested products of plant origin.  Storing (relating to main group A01F25/00 ):  Mechanical aspects (e.g. material handling aspects) of apparatus (e.g. silos) or methods that are specially adapted for storing harvested agricultural or horticultural crops.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Separation of grains or seeds from plants in the place where they grow -> A01D - Combines, i.e. harvesters or mowers combined with threshing devices -> A01D41/00 - Combines which thresh before the stalk is cut -> A01D41/08 - Field threshers with windrow pick-up apparatus -> A01D41/10 - Harvesting of standing crops -> A01D45/00 - Picking of fruits, vegetables, hops or the like; Devices for shaking trees or shrubs -> A01D46/00 - Delivering mechanisms for harvesters or mowers -> A01D57/00 - Elevators or conveyors for binders or combines -> A01D61/00 - Accessories for harvesters or mowers -> A01D75/00 - Pick-ups for harvested crop -> A01D89/00 - Arrangements for making or setting stacks in connection with harvesting -> A01D85/00 - Cutting or disintegrating in combination with mowing -> A01D43/08 - Pick-ups for harvested crop -> A01D89/00 - Shredding tea leaves -> A23F3/12 - Fodder mashers for preparing animal feeding-stuffs -> A23N17/02 - Cutting tobacco -> A24B7/00 - Spice mills -> A47J42/00 - Arrangements for making or setting stacks, used in connection with harvesting -> A01D85/00 - Devices for preserving flowers -> A01G5/06 - Arrangements in barns for treatment of tobacco -> A24B1/02 - Suspending devices for tobacco leaves -> A24B1/08 - Cabinets for perishable goods -> A47B71/00 - Fruit or potato storage cabinets, racks or tray for domestic use -> A47B75/00 - Closed containers for foodstuffs -> A47J47/02 - Drying hops -> C12C3/02 - Storing hops -> C12C3/04 - Civil engineering aspects of bulk storage containers, e.g. silos -> E04H7/00  **Glossary:** - bale: ("bale" as a noun) a compressed, solid package of harvested material, which is either held together by thread or webs or formed by the material itself (with or without additives) to maintain its shape - bundle: (noun) a relatively loose package of harvested material, normally consisting of straws or stalks that are oriented in parallel to each other. It can either be held together by thread or webs or be formed by the material itself. - combine: (noun) harvester or mower combined with a threshing device - harvest: (verb) separate useful parts from growing plants in situ, mow plants or gather whole plants or their useful parts from the ground or from where the plants grow, with the intention to make use of the separated, mown or gathered plant material - mow: ("mow" as a verb) cut down grass, cereals or other standing plants - threshing: separation of grains or seeds from harvested plants or plant parts (e.g. cereals, maize/corn, peas, beans or pine cones) by mechanical action (e.g. shaking, stamping, rubbing, beating or applying intermittent pressure)
[A01F 1/00] Stationary apparatus or hand tools for forming or binding straw, hay or the like into bundles -- A01F 1/00 covers stationary machines and hand tools designed to gather harvested plant material such as straw or hay and shape it into bundles, often by tying, netting, or otherwise binding the material together for easier handling, transport, or storage. It includes devices that compress or align the material before binding.
[A01F 1/02] Hand-operated tools -- A01F 1/02 covers hand-operated, stationary devices used to form or bind straw, hay or similar materials into bundles, such as manual presses, cradles or tying tools employed in agricultural processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 1/04] Fastening or tying devices -- A01F 1/04 covers devices used to secure harvested straw, hay or similar plant material into bundles, including clamps, ties, twine dispensers and other fastening mechanisms. The classification applies to both stationary apparatus and hand-held tools that bind the material by mechanical or manual means.
[A01F 1/06] Ties for bundles -- A01F 1/06 covers ties, cords, bands or similar devices used to bind straw, hay or comparable agricultural material into bundles. The subclass includes both stationary apparatus and hand-operated tools designed specifically for forming or securing such bundles.
[A01F 11/00] Threshing apparatus specially adapted for maize -- A01F 11/00 covers threshing machines that are specifically designed for maize, including devices that feed stalks, separate kernels from cobs and perform cleaning or sorting functions. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 11/02] for flax -- The CPC subclass A01F 11/02 covers threshing apparatus specially adapted for flax, detailing machines that separate flax fibers from seed during harvest processing. It sits within the hierarchy human necessities - agriculture - processing of harvested produce - threshing apparatus specially adapted for maize.
[A01F 11/04] for clover or like seeds, e.g. lucerne -- A01F 11/04 designates threshing or cleaning devices specifically adapted for harvesting clover or similar leguminous seeds such as lucerne. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy for human necessities, agriculture, processing of harvested produce and covers mechanisms that separate the seeds from plant material in these crops.
[A01F 11/06] for maize, e.g. removing kernels from cobs -- A01F 11/06 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers specialized machinery designed for processing harvested maize, specifically for the task of separating kernels from their cobs. This classification is part of a broader category that deals with agricultural produce processing under human necessities.
[A01F 11/08] for palm fruit, e.g. releasing the fruit from the stalk -- A01F 11/08 covers devices for detaching palm fruit from
[A01F 12/00] Parts or details of threshing apparatus -- A01F 12/00 covers the individual components and detailed features of threshing machines used in agriculture, such as beaters, cylinders, sieves, cleaning mechanisms, and any specialized attachments that improve grain separation or handling. This sub-class groups the mechanical parts that enable the primary threshing function within broader harvest-processing equipment.
[A01F 12/10] Feeders -- Feeders are components that convey harvested material to the threshing zone of agricultural machines, ensuring a steady supply of grain or other produce for processing. They include conveyors, augers or similar devices that guide the crop into the threshing apparatus, improving efficiency and uniformity in the separation of grain from stalks.
[A01F 12/12] without band-cutters -- A01F 12/12 refers to components of threshing machines that do not include band-cutters. It covers structural parts or auxiliary elements of threshing apparatus used in agricultural
[A01F 12/14] with band-cutters -- A01F 12/14 covers components of threshing machines that incorporate band-cutters, mechanisms that cut the continuous band of stalks or stems during threshing to separate grain from the plant material.
[A01F 12/16] Safety devices -- A01F 12/16 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, used by the EPO and USPTO, covers safety devices for parts or details of threshing apparatus. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy dealing with human necessities, agriculture, and processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/18] Threshing devices -- A01F 12/18 covers threshing devices, which are parts or details of threshing apparatus used in processing harvested produce. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy Human Necessities - Agriculture - Processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/181] Adjustable threshing mechanisms -- Adjustable threshing mechanisms are components of threshing equipment that allow the spacing, speed or pressure of the threshing elements to be varied in order to optimize grain separation for different crops or conditions. By enabling on-the-fly changes, they improve efficiency, reduce grain damage and accommodate variations in crop size, moisture content or field residue.
[A01F 12/182] Stripper beaters after the threshing drum -- Stripper beaters after the threshing drum are auxiliary components mounted on a threshing machine that further separate grain from straw and chaff after the drum has performed primary threshing. They typically consist of rotating bars or beaters that strike the material to dislodge remaining kernels, improving harvest efficiency.
[A01F 12/184] Cleaning means -- A01F 12/184 covers cleaning means designed for threshing apparatus, addressing devices or methods that remove residue, dust or plant material from the machine during or after operation. It falls within the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Processing of harvested produce → Parts or details of threshing apparatus.
[A01F 12/185] Mounting means -- Mounting means are components that attach, support or connect parts of threshing apparatus to each other or to the machine frame. They include brackets, clamps, bolts, shafts and other fixtures that secure conc
[A01F 12/187] Vibratory threshing mechanisms -- A01F 12/187 covers components of threshing machines that use vibration to separate grain from harvested stalks, such as vibrating screens, sieves, conveyors and related drive mechanisms. The class applies to any part or detail that creates or transmits oscillatory motion for threshing, whether integrated into a larger apparatus or provided as a stand-alone unit. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/20] Threshing cylinders with ribs -- Threshing cylinders with ribs are cylindrical components of threshing machines that have raised ribs or ridges along their surface. The ribs increase mechanical action on harvested grain, improving seed-stalk separation and reducing clogging during processing.
[A01F 12/22] Threshing cylinders with teeth -- A01F 12/22 covers threshing cylinders that are fitted with teeth to improve the separation of grain from straw or other plant material during threshing. The toothed cylinders rotate within the threshing apparatus, combing and breaking the harvested produce to release the seeds.
[A01F 12/24] One-part threshing concaves -- A one-part threshing concave is a single, integral component of a threshing machine that forms the curved surface against which harvested grain is pressed and separated from stalks. It combines the functions of a concave and a screen in a single piece, simplifying construction and maintenance.
[A01F 12/26] Multi-part threshing concaves -- A multi-part threshing concave is a set of interchangeable or linked concave sections mounted inside a threshing drum or cylinder, designed to improve grain separation by providing variable clearance and airflow. The multiple pieces allow adjustment for different crops and moisture levels, enhancing efficiency and reducing grain damage during the processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/28] Devices for adjusting the concaves -- The subclass A01F 12/28 covers mechanisms that adjust the curvature of concave parts in threshing equipment, such as movable concave plates, cams or linkages that can be set to different positions to control seed separation. These devices are used in agricultural machines that process harvested grain by varying the gap and angle between the concave and the threshing drum.
[A01F 12/30] Straw separators -- Straw separators are devices used in threshing machinery to divide harvested grain from the accompanying straw, improving grain cleanliness and facilitating straw handling. They typically employ screens, air flows or mechanical elements that allow fine grains to pass while retaining longer straw fibers, and are integral to modern agricultural processing lines.
[A01F 12/305] combined with additional grain extracting means -- A01F 12/305 refers to threshing machines that incorporate additional grain-extracting devices such as secondary sifters, aspirators or cleaning units to improve separation of grain from stalks. It is classified under parts or details of threshing apparatus within the processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/32] with shaker screens or sieves -- **Glossary:** - Screens or sieves: On the walker.
[A01F 12/34] Sieve elements -- A01F 12/34 designates sieve elements - the screens, meshes or perforated plates used in threshing machines to separate grain or seed from stalks and chaff during the processing of harvested produce. These components are integral to threshing apparatus for efficiently filtering and conveying the harvested material.
[A01F 12/36] Sieve elements specially adapted for handling short straw -- This subclass covers sieve components designed to handle short-straw in threshing machines, such as screens or mesh elements that let straw pass while retaining grain. It falls under processing of harvested produce within the Human Necessities sector.
[A01F 12/38] with juxtaposed and independently-moved shaker bars or the like -- A01F 12/38 covers threshing devices that employ multiple shaker bars positioned side by side, each capable of independent movement, to improve the agitation and separation of harvested material during processing. This subclass specifies the arrangement and action of these bars as a distinct part of threshing apparatus.
[A01F 12/385] with endless straw-conveying surface -- A01F 12/385 designates components of threshing machines that incorporate an endless surface to convey straw during processing of harvested crops. This subclass covers continuous belt or chain conveyors that move straw away from the threshing zone, improving separation efficiency and handling of residues in grain production. It sits within the hierarchy of human necessities → agriculture → processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/39] with straw carriers in the form of rotors or drums -- A01F 12/39 covers parts of threshing machines that incorporate straw-carrying mechanisms built as rotors or drums. The feature is used in agricultural equipment for separating straw from grain during harvest processing.
[A01F 12/395] Conical or cylindrical straw separators with internal working surface -- Conical or cylindrical straw separators with an internal working surface are components of threshing equipment that separate straw from grain by guiding material along the inner surface, using gravity and motion to achieve separation. They are mounted inside the threshing drum or housing to improve efficiency in processing harvested produce.
[A01F 12/40] Arrangements of straw crushers or cutters -- The A01F 12/40 subclass covers the layout and configuration of straw-crushing or cutting units incorporated in threshing machines. It addresses how these components are positioned, linked, or integrated to improve the handling and reduction of straw during harvest processing.
[A01F 12/42] Apparatus for removing awns from the grain -- This subclass covers machines designed to detach awns - hair-like bristles - from harvested grain during processing. It includes devices integrated into threshing or cleaning systems that strip, scrape or blow off awns to improve grain quality.
[A01F 12/44] Grain cleaners -- A01F 12/44 concerns grain cleaners, which are components or devices used in threshing apparatus to remove impurities and separate grain from chaff during the processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/442] Rotary cleaners -- Rotary cleaners are components of threshing machines that use rotating elements to separate grain from straw and chaff. They commonly consist of cylindrical drums or brushes that agitate harvested material, improving cleaning efficiency during processing.
[A01F 12/444] Fanning means -- A01F 12/444 pertains to the fanning components of threshing equipment used in agricultural processing of harvested crops. The classification covers mechanisms that air-move or spread grain after threshing to separate it from husks and improve drying. It is part of the broader Human Necessities → Agriculture → Processing of Harvested Produce hierarchy.
[A01F 12/446] Sieving means -- A01F 12/446 covers sieving means incorporated in threshing apparatus for separating harvested material such as grain from chaff or other residues. It falls under the Human Necessities > Agriculture > Processing of Harvested Produce > Parts or details of threshing apparatus subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A01F 12/448] Sieve adjusting means -- A01F 12/448 covers the mechanisms that adjust a sieve's position or clearance in a threshing machine, allowing the operator to control the size of material that passes through during grain separation. These adjusting means are part of the sieve assembly and are used to fine-tune the processing of harvested produce.
[A01F 12/46] Mechanical grain conveyors -- Mechanical grain conveyors are machines that move grain within threshing or post-harvest processing
[A01F 12/48] Air conduits or blowers for grain -- A01F 12/48 covers the air-moving components-such as ducts, channels, and blowers-integrated into threshing or grain-handling machinery for transporting, drying, or separating harvested grain. These elements form part of the apparatus that moves air through the grain processing system.
[A01F 12/50] Sack-filling devices -- A01F 12/50 pertains to sack-filling devices used in the processing of harvested produce, particularly in threshing operations. The entry covers mechanisms that automatically transfer grains, seeds or other crop material into bags, often integrated with threshing or cleaning equipment to streamline handling and improve efficiency.
[A01F 12/52] Arrangements for returning unthreshed grain to the threshing device -- This subclass covers mechanisms that feed grain which has not yet been threshed back into the threshing machine, enabling continuous processing and minimizing material loss. It includes conveyors, elevators and redirecting devices integrated with threshers in agricultural harvesting equipment.
[A01F 12/54] Arrangements for collecting or removing dust -- This subclass pertains to dust-control features incorporated into threshing machines, describing any ducts, filters, fans, collection chambers or similar arrangements that capture, convey or discharge the dust generated while processing harvested crops, thereby keeping the work area clean and protecting equipment.
[A01F 12/56] Driving mechanisms for the threshing parts -- The subclass defines the power-transmission devices that drive the threshing components of harvest-processing machines, including gear, belt, chain, hydraulic, pneumatic or other mechanical linkages that convey motion to drums, beaters or similar separating parts.
[A01F 12/58] Control devices -- A01F 12/58 covers control devices used in threshing machines and related harvest-processing equipment. The classification includes mechanisms that regulate speed, feed, or operation of threshing components to improve efficiency and protect grain quality.
[A01F 12/60] Grain tanks -- A01F 12/60 covers grain tanks, the storage components of threshing machines that receive and hold harvested grain before it is removed for further handling. These tanks may include features for filling, unloading, and protecting the grain during the threshing process.
[A01F 13/00] Baling straw or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  As the title describes, documents concerning devices which are hand-operated (i.e. by hand) to bale straw, hay or the like should be classified here.
[A01F 15/00] Baling presses for straw, hay or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Presses (i.e. machines which are not hand-operated) capable of producing a bale (material on which a substantial compression has been carried out).
[A01F 15/005] for conditioning bales, e.g. rebaling -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Reducing the size of a bale by re-compressing it after it has been formed a first time.
[A01F 15/02] with press-boxes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices in which the product is introduced in a box in order to produce a bale, but without compression means which compress the material after its introduction in the box (e.g. rams, compression belts, etc)  Illustrated example of subject matter classified in A01F15/02  Example from US5193449 by Ransom Woodbury S.

[A01F 15/042] Plungers -- The CPC subclass A01F 15/042 covers plungers, the components that move or compress material inside baling presses designed for straw, hay or similar harvested crops. These devices are used to drive the material into the press chamber, aiding formation of compact bales.
[A01F 15/044] with open pressing chambers -- A01F 15/044 refers to baling presses for straw, hay or similar crops that compress material in open pressing chambers rather than sealed ones, allowing the harvested produce to be formed into bales while the chamber remains exposed during operation. This subclass falls under processing of harvested produce within the broader agriculture category.
[A01F 15/046] with press-boxes -- A01F 15/046 classifies baling presses for straw, hay or similar harvested crops that incorporate press-boxes, which enclose the material while it is compressed into a bale.
[A01F 15/06] with double-action plunger -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Baling devices comprising a plunger which compresses material when moving in either direction.  Illustrated example of subject matter classified in A01F15/06  Example from FR1381197 by Sperry Rand Corp.
[A01F 15/07] Rotobalers, i.e. machines for forming cylindrical bales by winding and pressing -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The construction of the rotobaler for the purpose of winding the material in order to form a cylindrical bale. Typically they also bind or wrap the bale once finished, but those aspects are dealt with in other subgroups, in the same way as some other particular aspects of the construction not directily related to the configuration of the baling chamber (e.g drives, feeding devices, etc). [A01F 15/08] Details -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This head group should only be used for details which do not have a proper group available under the subgroups of this class, and which do not relate to the general principle behind the previous head groups ( A01F15/005 , A01F15/02 , etc), but to details of such devices which could be applicable to different types of presses (plunger presses, rotobalers, etc). E.g. a counter of bales mounted on a square baler and based on an image-processing system which detects the bale once dropped on the field.
[A01F 15/0816] Devices for dispensing chemicals in bales during formation -- The A01F 15/0816 subclass covers apparatus integrated into baling presses that apply chemical agents-such as herbicides, insecticides, or preservatives-to straw, hay or similar material while the bale is being formed, ensuring uniform treatment during the compacting process.
[A01F 15/0825] Regulating or controlling density or shape of the bale -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices which regulate or control density or shape of a bale which is being produced in a baler.

It is placed in the hierarchy of human necessities → agriculture → processing of harvested produce, specifically for baling presses that handle loose, fibrous crops.
[A01F 15/0841] Drives for balers -- This subclass covers mechanisms that provide power to baling machines used to compact straw, hay, or similar crop residues. It includes gear, belt, hydraulic, pneumatic and electric drive systems that transmit motion to the baler's forming components.
[A01F 15/085] for round balers -- This subclass covers round balers, machines that form cylindrical bales of harvested crops such as straw or hay. It includes designs where the material is rolled into a continuous round shape and bound for storage or transport. The classification is used for patents concerning the structure, operation, and control of such baling equipment.
[A01F 15/0858] for the tying devices or needles -- A01F 15/0858 designates tying devices or needles used in baling presses for straw, hay or similar harvested material. It covers mechanisms that feed, position or actuate such tying elements to bind the bales during the processing stage of agricultural produce.

[A01F 15/145] Twine knotters -- A01F 15/145 designates twine knotters used on baling presses for straw, hay or similar harvested crops. These mechanisms automatically form, tighten and cut twine knots to secure bales, enabling continuous baling and simplifying handling and transport of the finished product.
[A01F 15/146] Wire twisters -- Wire twisters are devices used in baling presses to twist and secure wire around bundles of straw, hay or similar harvested crops, forming a compact bale. They form part of the mechanical system that binds the material before it is lifted or stored.
[A01F 15/148] Monitoring the tying, e.g. mistie detectors -- A01F 15/148 relates to devices that monitor the tying stage of baling presses for straw, hay or similar materials, detecting faults such as missed or improper ties. The entry includes mistie detectors that sense when a tie has not been correctly applied and provide an alert to correct the operation.

[A01F 15/18] Endless belts, rolls or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Endless belts, rolls or the like elements which are used to delimit a pressing chamber in a rotobaler.
[A01F 17/00] Accessories for threshing machines or baling presses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Conveyors for transporting straw along relatively long distances in order to reach the thresher or baler. E.g. to cover the distance between two machines, or two parts of the machine.  Example from DE1026230 by Arnold Spethmann.
[A01F 17/02] Mechanical conveyors -- Mechanical conveyors for threshing machines or baling presses are devices that move harvested material between processing stages, typically using belts, chains, rollers or screw augers. They are integrated into the equipment to transport crops efficiently while minimizing labor and handling damage.

[A01F 19/00] Devices for securing threshing machines or baling presses to the ground, e.g. for compensating for unevenness of the ground -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bases for threshing machines and balers in order to work in a fixed position on the field, and also levelling devices for ensuring a certain orientation with regards to horizontal. [A01F 2009/00] Threshing machines with flails -- Threshing machines equipped with flails are agricultural devices that separate grain or seed from harvested stalks by striking the material with rotating or oscillating bars. The flails break up the plant material while allowing the valuable kernels to pass through for collection, improving efficiency in the processing of harvested produce. This technology falls under the CPC class A01F for implements used in threshing and gathering crops.
[A01F 2012/188] Rethreshing devices -- A01F 2012/188 covers machines and devices used to re-thresh harvested crops, separating grain from stalks and husks after cutting. The entry includes mechanical threshers, combine-harvester threshing units and related apparatus that break and clean the material to recover the seed.

[A01F 2015/072] Braking means for the film roll in balers which wrap the bale before opening the pressing chamber in order to stretch the film while wrapping -- A01F 2015/072 in the CPC for the EPO and USPTO covers braking devices applied to the film roll of a baler that engage before the pressing chamber is opened. The brake stretches the film while it is being wrapped around the bale, improving seal integrity.
[A01F 2015/0725] Film dispensers for film rollers in a satellite type wrapper, e.g. holding and tensioning means for the film roller -- The CPC subclass A01F 2015/0725 covers devices that feed film onto rollers in a satellite-type wrapping system, specifically the mechanisms that hold the film roller and apply tension to ensure smooth, controlled dispensing of the wrapping film. This includes various holding and tensioning structures designed for efficient film handling during the packaging process.
[A01F 2015/073] Features related to the horizontal rotating arm of a wrapping device of the satellite type -- This subclass groups patents that deal with the horizontal rotating arm of satellite-type wrapping devices, covering the arm's structure, drive and control mechanisms, and its interaction with the wrapping material. It addresses features that enable the arm to rotate around a central axis to encircle and bind agricultural or horticultural produce.
[A01F 2015/0735] Combined machines that include a press bale and a wrapping device in a further step, e.g. turning table, not in the same closed pressing chamber -- This CPC subclass covers machines that first form a bale in a press and then, in a subsequent operation, wrap the bale using a separate device such as a turning table, with the wrapping occurring outside the original closed pressing chamber.
[A01F 2015/074] Mechanisms for discharging a bale from the wrapping machine -- The A01F 2015/074 subclass relates to baling equipment, specifically mechanisms that release or discharge a compressed bale from a wrapping or baling machine. It covers devices, shafts, levers or pneumatic/ hydraulic systems that open the machine and eject the wrapped bale for handling or transport.
[A01F 2015/0745] Special features of the wrapping material for wrapping the bale -- A01F 2015/0745 covers inventions that improve or specify the wrapping material used to bind agricultural bales, such as biodegradable films, moisture-control layers, or reinforced textiles that
[A01F 2015/075] Opening the pressing chamber before wrapping, and then wrapping by rotating the bale without displacing it out of the open pressing chamber -- The CPC entry A01F 2015/075 covers a baling method in which the pressing chamber is opened before the bale is wrapped, after which the bale is wrapped by rotating it while it remains inside the chamber, eliminating the need to displace the bale out of the press.
[A01F 2015/0755] Configuration of support surfaces which can move in order to rotate a bale around at least one axis while wrapping -- The CPC entry A01F 2015/0755 pertains to agricultural machinery or devices that feature movable support surfaces designed to rotate a bale about one or more axes during the wrapping process, facilitating uniform coverage of the bale with protective film or netting. It covers configurations where the support can be adjusted or moved to achieve the desired rotation while the bale is being wrapped.
[A01F 2015/076] Wrapping device incorporating sensors -- A01F 2015/076 falls within the agricultural machinery group and covers wrapping devices that integrate sensors to monitor or control the wrapping process, such as sensor-guided bale binders, crop protectors or packaging systems that detect tension, temperature or moisture during operation.
[A01F 2015/077] Pressing chamber formed by belts and rollers -- A01F 2015/077 classifies agricultural machines whose pressing chamber is built from continuous belts and rollers that compress harvested material to extract liquid. The arrangement provides a flexible, continuous pressing zone, often used for fruits, vegetables or oilseed processing in harvesting or post-harvest equipment.
[A01F 2015/0775] Pressing chambers with fix volume -- A01F 2015/0775 covers pressing chambers that maintain a constant, predetermined internal volume during operation, typically used in agricultural or horticultural equipment for extracting liquids such as juice or oil from harvested material. The fixed-volume design ensures consistent pressure and throughput, improving efficiency and product quality.
[A01F 2015/078] Pressing chamber formed exclusively by flexible elements, e.g. belts -- A01F 2015/078 covers pressing chambers that are made solely from flexible components, such as belts, rather than rigid walls. The classification applies to machines that form a sealed compression zone using these movable elements to squeeze or extract material, commonly found in agricultural or food-processing equipment.
[A01F 2015/0785] Latching or locking means for the tailgate of the rotobaler -- A01F 2015/0785 covers devices that secure the rear gate of a rotary baler, providing a latch or lock that keeps the tailgate closed while the machine compacts material and permits controlled opening for unloading. The classification includes mechanical, hydraulic or pneumatic mechanisms designed specifically for rotobaler tailgate operation.
[A01F 2015/079] Sledge for rollers of the pressing chamber -- A01F 2015/079 refers to a sledge designed to engage and move the rollers inside a pressing chamber, typically used in equipment that extracts liquids or compresses material. The device provides a manual or powered means to apply force to the rollers, ensuring uniform pressure during the pressing operation.
[A01F 2015/0795] Pressing chamber with variable volume -- A pressing chamber whose internal space can be altered during operation, allowing the pressure applied to the material to be adjusted for different loads
[A01F 2015/0808] Balers incorporate an inclinometer -- This CPC subgroup concerns agricultural balers that are equipped with an inclinometer, a device that measures the angle or tilt of the bale or the machine during operation. The inclusion of the inclinometer is intended to improve control of bale formation, packing density, and discharge by monitoring and adjusting the machine's inclination.
[A01F 2015/0866] Clutching means for the knotting process;  Bale length measuring means for triggering the clutching mean -- A01F 2015/0866 covers agricultural baling equipment, specifically mechanisms that grip or clutch the material during the knotting stage of bale formation, and includes bale-length measuring devices that automatically trigger the clutching function when a preset length is reached.
[A01F 2015/0891] Weighing the finished bale before falling to ground -- The CPC subclass A01F 2015/0891 covers devices or methods for measuring the weight of a bale after it has been formed but before it is released to the ground, typically integrated into baling equipment to monitor output and ensure consistent product quality. This includes sensors, scales, or control systems that determine bale mass prior to discharge.
[A01F 2015/102] the pressing chamber is fed from the bottom side -- A01F 2015/102 covers apparatus in which the material to be pressed enters the pressing chamber from the bottom side, allowing the workpiece to be loaded beneath the chamber and pushed upward during compression. This feeding arrangement is typical of juicing, oil-extraction or other agricultural processing machines that use bottom-up feeding to improve product flow and chamber sealing.
[A01F 2015/103] the pressing chamber is fed from a lateral side -- A01F 2015/103 covers agricultural or food-processing presses in which the material to be compressed enters the pressing chamber from a side opening rather than from the top or bottom. The lateral feed arrangement allows continuous or semi-continuous operation and is typical of oilseed, fruit or vegetable presses designed for efficient material handling.
[A01F 2015/105] the pressing chamber is fed from the top side -- The CPC class A01F 2015/105 covers presses in which the material to be processed is introduced into the pressing chamber from the top side, such as fruit- or vegetable-juice presses where the feed drops onto a perforated plate and is then subjected to pressure. This orientation of feeding is a defining feature of the apparatus described in the entry.
[A01F 2015/107] Means for withdrawing knives, rotor or walls of the feeding chamber in case of plugging or congestion -- The CPC A01F 2015/107 classification covers mechanisms that allow knives, rotors or feeding-chamber walls of agricultural machinery to be removed or retracted when the system becomes blocked or congested. It pertains to designs that prevent jamming by providing quick-release or withdrawal features for the cutting or feeding components.
[A01F 2015/108] Cutting devices comprising cutter and counter-cutter -- A01F 2015/108 covers agricultural or horticultural cutting implements in which a primary cutter works together with a counter-cutter to shear plant material. The arrangement is used in tools such as pruning shears, fruit pickers or harvesters where the counter-cutter provides a supporting surface for clean cuts.
[A01F 2015/143] Guiding fingers for twines in order to avoid the slippage of twines to the sides of the bale when tying it -- This CPC subgroup covers devices or features that guide twine during bale tying to prevent lateral slippage, typically using protruding elements
[A01F 2015/183] Constructional details of belts of the press chamber -- The subclass A01F covers agricultural and forestry machinery, especially devices for harvesting or processing crops. The 2015/183 subgroup specifies the constructional details of belts in a press chamber, including material choice, geometry, mounting methods, tensioning arrangements and how the belts are integrated with the press.
[A01F 2015/186] Constructional details of rollers or the bearings of the rollers of the press chamber -- The CPC subclass A01F 2015/186 covers technical features related to the construction of rollers and their bearings within the press chamber of agricultural or horticultural machines. It includes designs that improve roller stability, rotation precision, and load handling during soil or crop processing. This subclass focuses on mechanical details that enhance the performance and durability of press-chamber rollers.
[A01F 2025/142] Machinery for making sausage-like groups of bales by wrapping or bagging -- A01F 2025/142 covers machinery that forms baled material into continuous, sausage-like bundles and then applies a wrapping or bagging material to secure the bundle. The equipment is used in hay, straw or similar
[A01F 2025/145] Bagging machines for loose materials making some kind of "sausage" on the field -- A01F 2025/145 covers agricultural bagging machines that shape loose bulk material into a continuous sausage-like form directly in the field, including mechanisms for feeding, forming and sealing the product.
[A01F 2025/147] the containers are specially adapted for storing or drying the products in vacuum -- A01F 2025/147 pertains to containers specially adapted for storing or drying agricultural products under vacuum, such as sealed bins, chambers or packaging that remove air to inhibit spoilage and moisture loss. The code falls within the A01F subgroup on harvesting, handling and storage of plant produce.
[A01F 2025/2045] Unloading arrangements including a conveyor belt for transferring the material to a hopper -- A01F 2025/2045 pertains to unloading arrangements that use a conveyor belt to move harvested material into a hopper, typically integrated with agricultural harvesters or threshing machines to enable continuous discharge and reduce manual handling.
[A01F 2025/2054] Machinery for cutting successive parallel layers of material in a trench silo -- The classification covers equipment designed to cut material stored in a trench-type silo into successive parallel layers for easier removal or further processing. [A01F 2025/2072] Scoops with an extracting tool mounted inside or in the vicinity of said scoop -- This subclass covers agricultural scoops that incorporate an extracting device positioned within or near the scoop cavity to remove material directly as it is collected, such as seed, soil or pest specimens, enabling simultaneous scooping and extraction in a single operation.
[A01F 2025/2081] Hydraulic features of the trench silos unloading devices, e.g. hydraulic circuits -- A01F 2025/2081 covers hydraulic aspects of trench-silo unloading equipment, detailing the design and operation of hydraulic circuits, actuators and control systems that move, position and discharge stored material from underground silos. It pertains to agricultural feed-handling machinery that uses fluid power to manage bulk feed or silage extraction.
[A01F 2025/209] Manual portable tools for unloading a silo, e.g. for cutting out a block -- A01F 2025/209 pertains to manual portable tools designed for unloading a silo, specifically devices used to cut out a block of stored material for removal. [A01F 25/00] Storing agricultural or horticultural produce -- A01F 25/00 relates to the storage of harvested agricultural or horticultural produce, encompassing methods and apparatus that preserve freshness, control temperature, humidity and gas composition, and protect against spoilage or pests. It includes facilities and systems for handling, conditioning, and maintaining the quality of crops after harvest.

[A01F 29/00] Cutting apparatus specially adapted for cutting hay, straw or the like -- A01F 29/00 covers cutting devices that are specifically engineered for harvesting and processing forage such as hay, straw or comparable plant material. The apparatus typically includes mechanisms like flails, sickles or rotary cutters that slice the material into manageable lengths for baling, feeding or further handling. It falls under the broader class of equipment for processing harvested produce in agriculture.

[A01F 29/02] having rotating knives with their cutting edges in a plane perpendicular to their rotational axis -- A cutting apparatus for hay, straw or similar crops uses rotating knives whose cutting edges lie in a plane that is perpendicular to the axis of rotation, allowing the blades to slice through material as they turn. This design is classified under CPC A01F 29/02.
[A01F 29/025] with feeding direction parallel to axis -- A01F 29/025 refers to cutting machines designed specifically for hay, straw or similar harvested crops in which the material is fed parallel to the axis of the cutter. The subclass falls under processing of harvested produce and details apparatus where the feeding direction aligns with the cutting shaft.
[A01F 29/04] with feeding direction transverse to axis -- A01F 29/04 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to cutting apparatus specifically designed for processing hay, straw, or similar materials, where the feeding direction of the material is perpendicular to the axis of the cutting mechanism. This classification is used by both the European and United States patent offices to organize patents related to agricultural machinery.
[A01F 29/06] having rotating knives with their cutting edges on a cylinder surface, e.g. of the helical-type -- This CPC subgroup covers cutting apparatus for hay, straw or similar harvested crops that use rotating knives whose cutting edges are mounted on the surface of a cylinder, such as helical-type arrangements. The cylindrical configuration allows continuous shearing as the cylinder turns, improving efficiency in forage processing.

It includes mechanisms for positioning, aligning, locking and adjusting blade angle or height to ensure efficient cutting of harvested plant material. It includes hydraulic, mechanical, pneumatic or other transmission systems that convert energy into the motion needed for the cutting apparatus. [A01F 5/00] Threshing -- A01F 5/00 covers threshing, the mechanical or manual process of separating grain or seed from the stalks, husks or other plant material after harvest. [A01F 7/02] with rotating tools -- A01F 7/02 covers threshing machines that use rotating tools to separate grain or seed from stalks and husks. The classification applies to apparatus where the threshing action is provided by rotating bars, discs or other rotary elements, typically driven mechanically.
[A01F 7/04] with axles transverse to the feeding direction -- A01F 7/04 refers to threshing apparatus whose driving axles are arranged transverse to the direction in which the harvested material is fed, meaning the axles run across the feed path rather than parallel to it. This subclass includes machines such as drum or roller threshers where the crop is conveyed perpendicular to the rotating axles.
[A01F 7/06] with axles in line with the feeding direction -- A01F 7/06 classifies threshing machines whose drive axles are positioned parallel to the direction in which the crop is fed through the apparatus, meaning the axle line runs along the feed path rather than across it. This arrangement aligns the power transmission with the material flow, often used to improve efficiency or simplify the mechanical layout of the threshing device.

This design is part of agricultural equipment for processing harvested crops.

[A01F 7/067] with material-flow influencing means -- The subclass A01F 7/067 covers threshing apparatus that includes components designed to direct, control, or modify the flow of material through the machine. This may involve guides, chutes, deflectors or similar features that influence how harvested crops are conveyed and processed during threshing. It is a subcategory of threshing apparatus within the broader group of processing harvested produce.
[A01F 7/70] with flails -- A01F 7/70 defines threshing machines that employ flails to strike harvested crops, separating grain from stalks. It falls under processing of harvested produce in the agriculture section of the CPC system, which is part of the broader human necessities category.

[A01G 13/37] Arrangements for laying out or removing ground coverings -- Group A01G13/37 is impacted by reclassification into group A01G13/39 . Groups A01G13/37 and A01G13/39 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01G 13/372] for removing ground coverings -- A01G 13/372 covers techniques and tools used to eliminate or control unwanted ground-cover vegetation around cultivated plants, including mechanical removal, mowing, or chemical treatment, as part of plant protection in horticultural practice.
[A01G 13/38] Anchoring means for ground coverings -- A01G 13/38 covers devices or methods for fixing ground-covering materials to the soil, such as stakes, clips, nets, weights or other anchoring structures that secure mulch, weed-suppressing fabrics or living covers, aiding plant protection and soil stability.
[A01G 13/39] Arrangements for perforating installed ground coverings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arrangements for forming perforations or holes in ground coverings, e.g. by cutting, punching, blanching, hooking or heating, after laying out the coverings on the ground.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a pointed member (15) for piercing a ground covering.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a heat generating component (23) for burning perforations in a ground covering.
[A01G 15/00] Devices or methods for influencing weather conditions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates an apparatus for precipitation of water for rainfall enhancement in water-stressed regions.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a cloud seeding system using hoses.  3.  Figure 3 illustrates a weather modification vehicle including a flare system capable of burning to create a dispersant which may induce precipitation in target clouds.
[A01G 17/00] Cultivation of hops, vines, fruit trees, or like trees -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Forestry -> A01G23/00
[A01G 17/005] Cultivation methods -- The CPC subclass A01G 17/005 defines the specific cultivation methods used for hops, vines, fruit trees and comparable woody crops, covering agronomic techniques such as pruning, training, spacing, trellising and other practices that optimise growth, yield and product quality. It falls under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Horticulture → Cultivation of hops, vines, fruit trees, or like trees.
[A01G 17/02] Cultivation of hops or vines -- A01G 17/02 covers methods and apparatus for growing hops and grape vines, including planting, training, pruning and harvesting techniques specific to these crops.
[A01G 17/023] Machines for priming and/or preliminary pruning of vines, i.e. removing shoots and/or buds  trimming apparatus for trellis work  A01G3/0408 -- A01G 17/023 covers machinery designed to prime or preliminarily prune vines by removing shoots or buds, including devices that trim vines along trellises. The classification pertains to equipment that prepares vines for later cultivation steps, facilitating efficient vine management.
[A01G 17/026] Machines for removing leaves of vines -- A01G 17/026 covers devices designed to strip or peel foliage from vines during cultivation. These machines typically use rotating brushes, rollers or air streams to detach leaves while
[A01G 17/04] Supports for hops, vines, or trees -- The A01G 17/04 subclass covers devices and structures used to support climbing crops such as hops, vines, and trees during cultivation. It includes trellises, stakes, wires, frames and similar systems that keep plants upright, improve airflow, and aid in harvesting.
[A01G 17/06] Trellis-work -- A01G 17/06 Trellis-work covers the design, construction and arrangement of support structures for vines, hops, fruit trees and similar climbing plants in horticulture, enabling their training and management. It includes various trellis systems, frames, wires and other devices used to hold and guide plant growth.
[A01G 17/08] Tools for attaching hops, vines, or boughs to trellis-work -- A01G 17/08 covers devices used to secure hops, grape vines or other woody shoots to trellis structures in horticultural production. These tools include hooks, clips, ties and similar fastening implements that facilitate training and supporting plants during growth.
[A01G 17/085] Espalier machines; Tying machines -- A01G 17/085 covers machines and devices used to train and support woody plants in horticulture, specifically equipment for espalier systems and for tying or securing branches of hops, vines, fruit trees and similar crops. This includes mechanical frames, tensioning systems and automated tying tools that facilitate orderly growth and ease of harvest.
[A01G 17/10] Holders for boughs or branches -- A01G 17/10 covers devices that hold or support boughs or branches in the cultivation of hops, vines, fruit trees and similar plants. These holders maintain proper spacing, prevent breakage and aid pruning or training of the plants, and are classified under horticulture within the agriculture segment of human necessities.
[A01G 17/12] Tree-bands -- Tree-bands are flexible strips or rings placed around the trunks or young stems of trees to provide support, protect against injury, or guide growth in orchards, vineyards and similar horticultural settings. They are usually made of plastic, rubber, metal or fabric and can be adjusted for seasonal expansion.
[A01G 17/14] Props -- A01G 17/14 covers props, which are structural supports such as stakes, poles or frames used to hold up and train cultivated hops, vines, fruit trees or similar woody plants during growth. The provision of these devices helps maintain plant posture, improve sunlight exposure and facilitate harvesting in horticultural practices.
[A01G 17/16] Devices for driving-in or pulling-out props -- A01G 17/16 covers tools and machines that insert or extract support stakes (props) used in the cultivation of hops, vines, fruit trees and similar plants. The devices may be manual, lever-type, hydraulic or pneumatic and are designed to drive the prop into the ground or pull it out without harming the plant.
[A01G 17/18] Means for filling-up wounds in trees -- A01G 17/18 covers devices, materials or methods used to fill or seal wounds in trees, such as bark dressings, sealants, plugs and grafting aides, aimed at protecting exposed wood from infection, desiccation or pest attack and promoting healing. The entry falls under horticulture for the cultivation of hops, vines, fruit trees and similar woody plants.
[A01G 18/00] Cultivation of mushrooms -- A01G 18/00 covers methods and systems for growing edible and medicinal fungi, including selection of substrate, inoculation techniques, environmental control, and harvesting procedures. It falls under the horticulture subclass of agriculture, which is part of the broader human necessities sector.
[A01G 18/10] Mycorrhiza -- A01G 18/10 covers technologies and methods that promote or utilize mycorrhiza, the symbiotic association between fungal mycelium and plant roots, within mushroom cultivation and broader horticultural practices. It includes inoculation techniques, substrate preparations, and management strategies that enhance nutrient exchange and plant health through these fungal-plant relationships.
[A01G 18/20] Culture media, e.g. compost -- A01G 18/20 relates to culture media used in mushroom cultivation, covering the composition, preparation and treatment of substrates such as compost that support fungal growth. It addresses formulations and methods for producing suitable growing media within horticultural mushroom production.
[A01G 18/22] Apparatus for the preparation of culture media, e.g. bottling devices -- This subclass covers machines and systems that mix, sterilise and package nutrient media used for growing mushrooms, including devices that dispense the prepared substrate into containers or bottles. It includes bottling equipment designed to handle sterile culture media for horticultural applications.
[A01G 18/30] Accessories for use before inoculation of spawn, e.g. sterilisers -- A01G 18/30 covers devices and tools employed before inoculating mushroom spawn, notably equipment for sterilising substrate or tools to ensure a contaminant-free environment. These accessories are used in mushroom cultivation under the horticulture branch of the Cooperative Patent Classification, facilitating clean preparation of the growth medium.
[A01G 18/40] Cultivation of spawn -- The A01G 18/40 subclass covers the production and handling of mushroom spawn, including methods for inoculating substrate, preparing and storing the mycelial material, and the equipment used to propagate the initial fungal cultures that will later be used for fruiting body cultivation. It focuses on the early stage of mushroom farming before full growth.
[A01G 18/50] Inoculation of spawn -- A01G 18/50 covers methods and apparatus for introducing fungal culture material, called spawn, into a substrate to start mushroom cultivation. It includes techniques for mixing, layering or injecting spawn into compost, logs, sawdust or other growth media, and may address timing, temperature control and inoculum distribution to ensure uniform colonization. This subclass falls under horticulture practices for mushroom production.
[A01G 18/55] Forming inoculation holes -- A01G 18/55 relates to the creation of inoculation holes in mushroom cultivation substrates, covering methods and apparatus for drilling, boring or otherwise preparing openings to receive fungal spawn or other inoculum, enabling uniform distribution and efficient colonisation.
[A01G 18/60] Cultivation rooms -- A01G 18/60 covers facilities designed for growing mushrooms, including the design, construction, and environmental control of indoor cultivation rooms. It addresses aspects such as
[A01G 18/61] Growth guides for fruiting bodies, e.g. paper rolls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for guiding the fruiting body, or carpophore, of a mushroom during growth, e.g. to obtain a desired form (illustration in Fig.1).  Paper rolls, paper wraps, wrapping papers, etc. for guiding the carpophores (illustration in Fig.2).  Apparatus for attaching the growth guides to the cultivation containers.  Methods therefor.
[A01G 18/62] Racks -- Racks for mushroom cultivation are devices that hold and organize growing media to provide adequate airflow, light and moisture control, facilitating efficient fruiting and harvest. They are used in horticultural mushroom production to support substrates on multiple levels, improving space utilization and allowing easy handling of the crop.
[A01G 18/64] Cultivation containers -- A01G 18/64 covers containers specifically designed for growing mushrooms, including trays, bins, bags and other vessels that support substrate preparation, humidity control and harvest. The code is used to classify inventions relating to the shape, material and functional features of mushroom-cultivation equipment.
[A01G 18/65] characterised by the lids, e.g. lids with filters -- A01G 18/65 covers mushroom-cultivation systems that incorporate lids, for example lids fitted with filters to permit gas exchange while shielding the substrate. These lids help maintain humidity, control airflow and reduce contamination in indoor mushroom growing containers.
[A01G 18/66] Cultivation bags -- A01G 18/66 relates to mushroom production using specialized bags as containers, describing the materials, design features, and methods for inoculating, incubating and fruiting mushrooms within sealed or breathable bags that facilitate controlled growth conditions.
[A01G 18/68] Cultivation bottles -- A01G 18/68 relates to the use of bottles or similar containers for mushroom cultivation, describing designs that enable inoculation, substrate containment and fruiting body development while controlling moisture, gas exchange and temperature within the vessel. This subclass focuses on horticultural techniques that employ sealed or vented bottles to optimise mushroom growth.
[A01G 18/69] Arrangements for managing the environment, e.g. sprinklers -- A01G 18/69 covers systems and installations that control the growing environment for mushroom cultivation, such as irrigation, sprinklers, ventilation, temperature and humidity regulation, and other equipment used to maintain optimal conditions for fungal growth.
[A01G 18/70] Harvesting -- A01G 18/70 covers techniques and apparatus for harvesting cultivated mushrooms, including methods for separating the fruit bodies from the growing medium, gathering, and transporting the produce, as well as equipment such as picking tools, conveyors, and collection systems.
[A01G 18/80] Accessories for use after harvesting, e.g. scrapers -- A01G 18/80 relates to accessories employed after mushrooms are harvested, such as scrapers that remove substrate or clean the fruiting bodies, facilitating post-harvest handling.
[A01G 2/00] Vegetative propagation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Methods of asexual reproduction of plants, i.e. NOT involving seeds.  Methods of vegetative propagation involving specialized plant organs such as:  Methods of vegetative propagation involving suckering, i.e. transplanting suckers.  Specific apparatus therefor not otherwise provided for.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Plant reproduction by tissue culture techniques -> A01H4/00
[A01G 20/00] Cultivation of turf, lawn or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Hand-held edge trimmers or shears for lawn -> A01G3/06 - Edging for lawn -> A01G9/28 - Lawn-mowers -> A01D34/00
[A01G 2003/005] Removing buds -- A01G 2003/005 covers techniques and tools for removing buds from plants, including pruning, bud-snipping, and other mechanical or manual methods used to control growth, improve fruit quality, or prevent disease in horticultural and agricultural practice.
[A01G 2003/007] Using spray -- The CPC subclass A01G 2003/007 covers the use of spray methods for applying substances to plants, soils or related agricultural targets. It includes devices, techniques and processes that dispense liquids or powders as a fine mist or jet to deliver pesticides, fertilizers, growth regulators or other treatments.
[A01G 2003/023] with means for grasping or collecting the cut objects -- A01G 2003/023 covers agricultural or horticultural machines that include devices for grasping, gathering or collecting cut material, such as crop harvesters, mowers or cutters equipped with conveyors, bins, bags or other collection mechanisms that retain the harvested parts.
[A01G 2003/0443] with height-adjustable platforms -- A01G 2003/0443 covers agricultural or horticultural machines that incorporate height-adjustable platforms, allowing the operator or service personnel to raise or lower a working surface as needed. It includes tractors, harvesters, sprayers and similar implements where the adjustable platform improves access and ergonomics.
[A01G 2003/0452] with platforms for persons -- A01G 2003/0452 covers agricultural tractors that are equipped with platforms intended for persons, such as operator seats, passenger platforms or work platforms mounted on the vehicle. This subgroup classifies machines where the platform is an integral part of the tractor's design for carrying or supporting people during operation or maintenance.
[A01G 2003/0461] with reciprocating knives -- A01G 2003/0461 covers agricultural harvesting or processing devices that employ reciprocating knives as the cutting element. The subclass includes machines such as grain or forage harvesters where the knives move back-and-forth to shear plant material, facilitating separation of crops from stalks.
[A01G 2005/005] Thorn removers -- A01G 2005/005 covers tools, devices or methods used to remove thorns, spines or similar sharp plant parts, typically for agricultural or horticultural purposes. It falls within the CPC subclass A01G, which deals with plant protection and cultivation equipment, and is used by patent offices to classify inventions that facilitate safe handling of thorny crops or ornamental plants.
[A01G 2009/1446] with perforations -- A01G 2009/1446 is a CPC subgroup that covers plant-propagation items, sowing or planting aids, and related equipment that incorporate perforations. It includes trays, pots, films, or other containers designed with holes to improve drainage, aeration, or seed placement during cultivation. This classification helps identify products that use perforated structures to support plant growth.
[A01G 2009/1453] containing textile products -- A01G 2009/1453 covers agricultural or horticultural apparatus and methods that incorporate textile materials such as nets, bags, ropes, or woven containers. The classification applies to devices designed to hold, protect, transport, or support plants, produce, or soil using fabrics, meshes, or other textile constructions.
[A01G 2009/1461] containing woven structures -- A01G 2009/1461 falls under the CPC sector for agricultural and horticultural equipment and covers items that incorporate woven structures such as nets, fabrics or meshes used for supporting, protecting or treating plants. It includes devices like protective covers, plant support nets and other woven implements employed in cultivation.
[A01G 2009/1484] Glazing apparatus -- A01G 2009/1484 covers devices and systems for installing, supporting or maintaining glazing on agricultural buildings, greenhouses or similar structures. It includes frames, hinges, sealing mechanisms and automated opening or closing equipment that facilitate light transmission while protecting crops from weather. The code is used to classify inventions related to greenhouse glazing technology.
[A01G 2009/1492] Cleaning apparatus -- A01G 2009/1492 covers apparatus for cleaning agricultural products, such as harvested crops, seeds, or fodder. The devices separate dust, debris, foreign matter or unwanted material from the product using mechanical, pneumatic or hydraulic means before further processing.
[A01G 2009/248] with distillation of water -- A01G 2009/248 covers plant-cultivation methods or equipment that incorporate water distillation, typically to provide purified water for irrigation, hydroponic systems or other growth environments. The sub-group addresses processes that distill water before it is applied to plants, ensuring removal of impurities that could affect growth or product quality.
[A01G 20/10] Pre-cultivated sod or turf -- A01G 20/10 covers the preparation, handling and installation of pre-cultivated sod or turf that has already been grown and rooted on a supporting substrate. It includes methods for transporting, storing and laying such ready-made grass mats for immediate use in lawns, sports fields or landscaping.
[A01G 20/12] Apparatus for cutting sods or turfs -- A01G 20/12 relates to machines that cut sections of grass, turf or sod for removal, transport or re-laying. The apparatus may use blades, wheels or cutting cylinders that separate a strip of turf from the ground and can be powered mechanically, hydraulically or electrically. It is used in lawn renovation, sports-field maintenance and turf harvesting.

[A01G 20/15] specially adapted for stacking sods or sod rolls -- A01G 20/15 covers tools or machines designed specifically for handling sod, including devices that stack sod pieces or rolled sod for transport, storage or installation in turf and lawn cultivation. These implements facilitate the efficient organization and movement of sod units during landscaping or agricultural operations.
[A01G 2017/065] Trellis-work for supporting vines having wire-tensioning devices -- A01G 2017/065 relates to trellis systems used to support vines, particularly grapevines, that include wire-tensioning devices for adjusting and maintaining plant spacing and load. The classification covers horticultural structures that enable efficient training, support, and mechanised management of climbing crops.
[A01G 20/18] Apparatus for laying sods or turfs -- A01G 20/18 covers machines and devices designed to place pre-grown sod or turf onto soil, facilitating rapid establishment of lawns, sports fields or landscaped areas. The apparatus may include rollers, carriers, or automated mechanisms that transport and press the sod into position, improving efficiency and uniformity in turf cultivation.
[A01G 20/20] Cultivation on mats -- A01G 20/20 concerns the cultivation of turf, lawn or similar vegetation on mats, encompassing techniques for establishing, maintaining and repairing grass or groundcover grown on woven, synthetic or natural matting. It includes methods for anchoring, irrigating, fertilising and protecting the planted material to ensure stable, uniform growth on that substrate.
[A01G 20/30] Apparatus for treating the lawn or grass surface -- Apparatus for treating the lawn or grass surface refers to machines and devices designed to maintain, improve or protect turf, such as mowers, aerators, sprayers, irrigation units, scarifiers and weed-control equipment. These tools are used to cut, loosen, water, fertilise, or otherwise manage grass areas in gardens, sports fields, parks and similar settings. The classification covers the mechanical and automated systems employed for these lawn-care functions.
[A01G 20/35] Garden rollers -- A01G 20/35 covers garden rollers, devices used in turf and lawn cultivation to compact or level soil, seed beds and finished surfaces. These rollers may be weighted, hollow or equipped with wheels or drums and are employed to improve seed contact, reduce surface unevenness and promote uniform growth in horticultural settings.

[A01G 22/05] Fruit crops, e.g. strawberries, tomatoes or cucumbers -- A01G 22/05 relates to technologies and methods for cultivating fruit crops such as strawberries, tomatoes and cucumbers, encompassing plant breeding, specific cultivation practices, harvesting equipment and treatment techniques tailored to these horticultural products.
[A01G 22/10] Asparagus -- A01G 22/10 pertains to the horticultural practices for growing asparagus, covering methods of planting, cultivation, maintenance, and harvesting of this specific crop.
