[A01K 1/0035] Transportable or mobile animal shelters -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shelters specially adapted for being transported and placed where needed. These shelters can be portable, collapsible, and modular, can include feeding, watering and an outdoor space and can be moved by the provision of wheels, towing hooks and the like.  These shelters are intended to be built or installed in situ for animals on the way and/or to be transported and installed in the place where needed to give shelter to animals.
[A01K 1/0041] Rotary, round or circular animal barns -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Buildings for housing animals comprising a mainly circular or round shape or rotating, e.g. circular plan view or round walls. These buildings can be rotary, can comprise flat walls forming a kind of circumference, e.g hexagonal, octagonal, and decagonal, can be oval, can be connected to other structures and can be for individual or multiple animals.
[A01K 1/0047] Air-conditioning, e.g. ventilation, of animal housings  Laboratory cage ventilation  A01K1/0313 ; Cat or dog houses with climate control means  A01K1/0335 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Means for heating, cooling, ventilating, drying or humidifying stable rooms in general.  Methods and systems for dispersing ozone into a stable room, e.g. for the reduction of flies, odour or dust.  Climate and ventilation control means, e.g. alarm system or responsive timer.  Means of cleaning waste air:  Methods for influencing and determining the airflow, e.g. by installation of metal panels.  Specially adapted details, e.g. roof lids.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Cages for laboratory animals comprising forced ventilation means -> A01K1/0313 - Cat or dog houses with climate control means -> A01K1/0335

[A01K 1/0058] Construction of air inlets or outlets in roofs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Construction of air inlets or outlets in roofs, e.g. fans and ducts etc.  Methods of controlling ventilation in roofs.  Ventilation ceiling and panels therefor.
[A01K 1/0064] Construction of air inlets or outlets in walls -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Air inlets or outlets in walls of animal housings which serve to enhance airflow or ventilation within the housings.  May be by means of sheet metal panels with vents, ventilators or valves.  Ventilating system may have a feedback control system responsive to temperature, or may be able to control humidity and movement of forced air. [A01K 1/0088] Animal shelters especially adapted for keeping young cattle, i.e. calves -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shelters specially adapted for young farm animals, especially calves. These shelters can be transportable, collapsible, modular, can include warming or cooling means, feeding, watering or an outdoor space.  Boxes for containing one calf.  Small igloos for young farm animals.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a calf stall with extension.  2. [A01K 1/0094] Animal shelters or barns especially adapted for keeping horses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Enclosures directed for raising horses. These enclosures can include cooling or warming, feeding and/or watering and can be individual or multiple horses.  Structures to be installed in stalls for horses, e.g. to prevent crib biting.  Gates especially designed for horse stalls.  Horse protectors and safety kerbing in doors, walls or buildings, e.g. skirting or stable bank.
[A01K 1/01] Removal of dung or urine -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Collecting solid or liquid manure from stables into any container, e.g. waste treatment bags, composting boxes, (liquid) manure tanks.  Mechanical devices for removing manure from animal stalls, e.g. scraper.  Device may be automatic, e.g. vehicle.  Device for transporting manure inside the stable or stall.  Treatment of manure, e.g. drying, heating, cooling, deodorizing.  Details of channels or (slatted) floors, which are intended to collect manure or urine.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2a.  2b.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floor coverings e.g. bedding-down sheets -> A01K1/015
[A01K 1/0103] of liquid manure  treating manure  A01C3/00 ; treatment of water, waste water, sewage or sludge  C02F -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Stables and apparatus for collecting liquid animal manure, e.g. collection pit, tank etc.  Details like dung channel, inlet valve etc.  Treatment of liquid manure, e.g. heating, cooling, deodorization, etc.  Different ways of converting liquid animal waste.  Devices and methods for cleaning stable floor.  Apparatus for disposal or cleaning of waste-water.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Treating manure -> A01C3/00 - Treatment of water, waste water, sewage or sludge -> C02F
[A01K 1/0107] Cat trays; Dog urinals; Toilets for pets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Indoor or outdoor toilets for pets.  Cat litter boxes that may be portable or disposable:  Ready-made fillings for such toilets, may be for independent use.  Toilets may have flushing means, disinfection means.  Pet-sheets that may be absorbing, impregnated with perfume, deodorant, disinfectant or pesticide, which pet-sheets are integrated within or used directly with the litter box, cat tray, dog urinal or toilet.  Toilets that may include reward mechanisms or pet-attracting scents or features, e.g. a fire hydrant, for training animals to use toilets.  Toilets for pets that may have a feature for controlling temperature in or around the pet toilet.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates a cat bathroom box.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates a portable canine waste station.

A litter box that contains a litter tray apparatus that collects particles of litter scattered from the paws.  Paw cleaning litter mat, e.g. magnetic pet litter system.  Paw cleaning device or structure may be integral to the litter box or a separate component therefrom.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.  3.

[A01K 1/0152] Litter -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Litter or sheets for use in animal stables, cages, etc.  Means and apparatus for making litter.
[A01K 1/0154] comprising inorganic material -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Litter comprising inorganic material, e.g. deodorants, insect repelling material, dyed, ph-indicating material, etc.  Litter may be absorbent, dust reducing, odour-inhibiting, etc.  Litter may comprise clay, gypsum, aluminium sulfate, material for detecting infections, etc.  Different means of processing litter.

Mechanical devices may be manual, automatic or autonomous.  Bedding removal and refilling system for animal cages.  May be for livestock stalls or chicken runs. Boxes may be transportable.

[A01K 1/0236] Transport boxes, bags, cages, baskets, harnesses for animals; Fittings therefor  for poultry  A01K31/002 ; Transportable cages, e.g. for pigeons  A01K31/07 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for transporting animals from one place to another by hand or inside a transport e.g. in automobile or trailer, by plane, by train, etc.  Transportation boxes for pets which can be collapsible, demountable, can have fastening means for mounting on the seat of an automobile and/or can be worn by the user. [A01K 1/0245] Boxes or cages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rigid containers for transporting animals which can be intended for transport by hand, in automobiles, airplanes, trains, ships or shipping containers or any other means for transportation.  The boxes/cages can also have provisions for food/water, ventilation, etc.
[A01K 1/0254] Bags or baskets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bags, baskets or similar not rigid/flexible containers for transporting animals which can have handles or shoulder straps for carrying and can leave parts of the animal outside.
[A01K 1/0263] Harnesses -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Harnesses for transporting, carrying or lifting animals, e.g. in order to assist the animal with movement, walking or mobility. Harnesses for assisting animals with walking which fall under this place are intended to aid the animal with walking. These harnesses often have a handle or undercarriage support for supporting the animal during lifting, for example, to help the animal climb stairs.  The harnesses can be attached to a seatbelt system.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.  3.  Figure 3 illustrates a multi-purpose pet harness.
[A01K 1/0272] Boxes, bags, cages, baskets, harnesses especially adapted for transport in or on automobiles -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Restraining means adapted to carry animals in or on automobiles, which can be interacting with the safety belt, passenger seat, trunk or luggage compartment, trailer or the like.  The restraining means can be a box, a bag, a cage, a basket, a harness or the like which can be either connected to the safety belt or built together with it, can be adapted for transport in the trunk of an automobile or can be intended to be transported in or on a car, bus, truck or tractor.  This group only covers (road) transport in wheeled motor vehicles.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  2.

[A01K 1/06] Devices for fastening animals, e.g. halters, toggles, neck-bars or chain fastenings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for fastening animals to stable structures: may include chains, movable braces. Bars may be movable in any plane or way.  Details therefor, e.g. locking systems, feeding rack beam, anti-hanging system, etc  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Simultaneously releasing several animals -> A01K1/08
[A01K 1/0613] Devices for immobilisation or restraint of animals, e.g. chutes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices, in particular pens or boxes, for immobilising animals, e.g. animal chute, squeezes or crushers.  May include animal head restrainer, weighing appliances.  Devices may be mountable on a vehicle, portable, adjustable in size.  The immobilisation is confining or cramping rather than fettering; lifting or overturning may be additional.  Details for these devices, e.g. doors, tail gates.  Starting boxes for rodeos, pig holders, sheep cradles, drafting gates.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[A01K 1/062] Neck-bars -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for tying up an animal; stanchions suspended from a structural part above, forming a movable collar-like frame.  may be automatic; self-locking.  Details therefor, e.g. alignment device, triggering mechanism, etc.  Stanchions do not form part of, nor cooperate with, a grid. They are independent of grids.
[A01K 1/064] Chain fastenings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for fastening chains, or similar elements around animals' necks, to the stable structure.  May be fastened around the necks or bellies of the animals.  Fastening points to stable may be movable.  Fastening element is carried by animal, independent of the stable structure.  Details therefor, e.g. hooks, halter fittings.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[A01K 1/08] Arrangements for simultaneously releasing several animals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for simultaneously releasing several fastened or confined animals.  May be released by, e.g. a chain mechanism that simultaneously retracts multiple animal restraint devices or opens multiple doors when pulled.  Animals may be fastened or confined in any manner.  Release means may be automatic or by hand, e.g. connected with door controls, or frightening means.  Parts of stable may be movable.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[A01K 1/10] Feed racks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hay-racks.  Open-framework barriers formed by slats, grids, bars, rails or the like, for feeding roughage, indoors or outdoors (except for poultry or small animals).  May be movable (by animal or motor), portable, collapsible or transportable.  Devices may contain a trough.  Details therefor, e.g. fastening grids. Biologically marking methods and apparatus, e.g. applying antigen.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Marking poultry or other birds -> A01K35/00

Electronic identification means may be used, for example, to associate monitoring data with an individual animal, or to control the movement, feeding, or other behaviour of individual animals. [A01K 2003/007] Fencing and guiding systems for amphibia -- A01K 2003/007 covers fencing and guiding systems designed specifically for amphibians. It includes barriers, enclosures, trenches and other structures that prevent escape, protect habitats, or direct the movement of frogs
[A01K 21/00] Devices for assisting or preventing mating -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for preventing mating, e.g. harness for dogs, bulls, rabbits, etc.  Diapers for female dogs.  Stimulation of ovulation methods and devices.  Means for guiding a boar past sows, e.g. boar cart.
[A01K 2207/00] Modified animals -- A01K 2207/00 covers inventions concerning animals that have been genetically or otherwise altered to enhance desirable traits for agricultural use, including methods of breeding, genetic engineering and related biotechnological modifications. The class falls under animal husbandry within the broader agriculture segment of human necessities.
[A01K 2207/05] Animals modified by non-integrating nucleic acids, e.g. antisense, RNAi, morpholino, episomal vector, for non-therapeutic purpose -- A01K 2207/05 covers animals whose genomes are altered using non-integrating nucleic acids such as antisense oligonucleotides, RNAi constructs, morpholinos or episomal vectors, where the modifications are intended for purposes other than therapy, for example research or production traits.
[A01K 2207/10] Animals modified by protein administration, for non-therapeutic purpose -- This subclass covers methods of altering animals by administering specific proteins to induce desired traits, such as improved growth, milk composition, or disease resistance, when the purpose is not therapeutic treatment of the animal. It includes techniques that use protein feed additives, injections or implants to modify physiological or metabolic characteristics for agricultural or commercial benefit.
[A01K 2207/12] Animals modified by administration of exogenous cells -- This subclass concerns animals whose characteristics are altered by administering living cells from another source, such as stem-cell transplants, genetically engineered cells, or microbial symbionts, to achieve desired physiological or productive effects, and it excludes changes made through germ-line modification.
[A01K 2207/15] Humanized animals -- Humanized animals are genetically engineered animals that carry human genes, cells or tissues, enabling them to express human biological traits for biomedical research, drug testing or production of therapeutic proteins. The CPC entry A01K 2207/15 classifies inventions related to the creation, breeding, or use of such modified animals.
[A01K 2207/20] Animals treated with compounds which are neither proteins nor nucleic acids -- The subclass A01K 2207/20 covers the treatment of genetically modified or otherwise altered animals with compounds that are not proteins or nucleic acids, such as small-molecule drugs, minerals, vitamins or other chemical agents. It includes methods for administering these substances to improve health, productivity or specific traits of the animals.
[A01K 2207/25] Animals on a special diet -- This CPC subgroup covers technologies for providing animals with a special diet, including specially formulated feeds, feeding devices, and management methods that deliver tailored nutrition to livestock, pets or other animals to meet specific health, growth or production requirements.
[A01K 2207/30] Animals modified by surgical methods -- A01K 2207/30 is a CPC subclass that deals with animals whose biological characteristics have been changed by surgical techniques, including grafts, implants, organ removal or other operative interventions. It falls under the "modified animals" group within animal husbandry in the agriculture sector.
[A01K 2207/35] Animals modified by environmental factors, e.g. temperature, O -- A01K 2207/35 covers inventions dealing with animals whose characteristics are altered through external environmental conditions such as temperature, lighting, humidity or diet. It includes methods, devices or systems that induce physiological, developmental or genetic changes by exposing the animal to such factors, and relates to the broader field of modified animals in animal husbandry.
[A01K 2217/00] Genetically modified animals -- The CPC subclass A01K 2217/00 covers inventions involving genetically modified animals used in animal husbandry. It includes methods for altering animal genomes, the resulting transgenic or edited animals, and related breeding or production techniques aimed at improving traits such as growth, disease resistance, or product quality.
[A01K 2217/03] Animals modified by random mutagenesis, e.g. using ENU, chemicals -- This CPC subgroup covers animals whose genomes have been altered through random mutagenesis techniques, such as exposure to chemicals like ENU, rather than precise gene-editing methods. It pertains to the use of these nondirected mutagenic agents to create genetically modified livestock or other farmed species.
[A01K 2217/05] Animals comprising random inserted nucleic acids (transgenic) -- A01K 2217/05 covers animals that contain randomly inserted nucleic acid sequences, i.e., transgenic animals created through genetic engineering. This subclass falls under animal husbandry within agriculture and pertains to genetically modified animals.
[A01K 2217/052] inducing gain of function -- A01K 2217/052 covers techniques for inducing gain-of-function traits in genetically modified animals, such as inserting or editing genes to confer new or enhanced characteristics. It includes methods that deliberately increase biological activity, resistance, or productivity of livestock for agricultural purposes.
[A01K 2217/054] inducing loss of function -- A01K 2217/054 covers techniques for deliberately disabling or reducing the activity of specific genes in genetically modified animals, typically to study gene function or produce desired traits. It falls under animal husbandry within agriculture, focusing on loss-of-function induction in engineered livestock.
[A01K 2217/056] due to mutation of coding region of the transgene (dominant negative) -- The subclass A01K 2217/056 covers genetically modified animals in which the coding region of an introduced transgene has been altered to produce a dominant-negative effect, thereby suppressing or interfering with the normal function of the target gene. This type of mutation is used in animal husbandry to confer specific traits or control biological pathways.
[A01K 2217/058] due to expression of inhibitory nucleic acid, e.g. siRNA, antisense -- A01K 2217/058 covers techniques for producing genetically modified animals in which an inhibitory nucleic acid-such as siRNA or an antisense molecule-is expressed to suppress specific target genes. The classification applies to animal-husbandry applications that use these nucleic acids to achieve desired traits or disease resistance.
[A01K 2217/07] Animals genetically altered by homologous recombination -- A01K 2217/07 pertains to the use of homologous recombination techniques to modify the genome of animals, creating genetically altered livestock or other species for agricultural purposes. It includes methods of inserting, deleting, or replacing DNA sequences to achieve desired traits.
[A01K 2217/072] maintaining or altering function, i.e. knock in -- This classification covers methods for introducing genetic material into an animal to increase or modify the activity of a gene, such as knock-in techniques that insert or replace DNA sequences to confer new or enhanced traits.
[A01K 2217/075] inducing loss of function, i.e. knock out -- A01K 2217/075 covers techniques for producing loss-of-function, or knockout, modifications in genetically altered animals. It classifies methods that deliberately disable specific genes to alter traits, supporting research and breeding within animal husbandry and agricultural applications. This entry falls under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Genetically modified animals.
[A01K 2217/077] heterozygous knock out animals displaying phenotype -- A01K 2217/077 is a CPC subclass within Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Genetically modified animals and pertains to heterozygous knockout animals that display a specific phenotype, covering inventions related to their creation, breeding or use for research or commercial purposes.
[A01K 2217/15] Animals comprising multiple alterations of the genome, by transgenesis or homologous recombination, e.g. obtained by cross-breeding -- A01K 2217/15 covers animals that carry two or more genome modifications introduced by transgenesis or homologous recombination, including those obtained through cross-breeding. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Genetically Modified Animals.
[A01K 2217/20] Animal model comprising regulated expression system -- The CPC subclass A01K 2217/20 covers animal models that incorporate a regulated expression system, enabling controlled activation or suppression of specific genes within genetically modified animals. It pertains to research tools for studying gene function, disease mechanisms, or therapeutic interventions in livestock or laboratory species.
[A01K 2217/203] Animal model comprising inducible/conditional expression system, e.g. hormones, tet -- This subclass covers animal models that have been genetically engineered to carry inducible or conditional expression systems, such as hormone-regulated or tetracycline-controlled gene switches, allowing researchers to activate or deactivate specific genes on demand. It applies to livestock and other animals used in agriculture and animal husbandry for functional studies, trait development, or biomedical research.
[A01K 2217/206] Animal model comprising tissue-specific expression system, e.g. tissue specific expression of transgene, of Cre recombinase -- This subclass covers animal models engineered to express a transgene or Cre recombinase under the control of a tissue-specific promoter, enabling targeted genetic modification in designated organs or cell types for research on gene function and disease mechanisms.
[A01K 2217/30] Animal model comprising expression system for selective cell killing, e.g. toxins, enzyme dependent prodrug therapy using ganciclovir -- This classification covers genetically engineered animal models that contain an expression system designed to kill specific cells, such as through toxin genes or enzyme-prodrug systems like HSV-TK with ganciclovir. It pertains to the use of such models in research or production, enabling selective cell ablation.
[A01K 2227/00] Animals characterised by species -- A01K 2227/00 covers patent subject matter related to animals identified or distinguished by their species within the field of animal husbandry. It includes inventions that are specific to a particular species, such as breeding techniques, feed formulations, health management, or handling methods tailored to that species.
[A01K 2227/10] Mammal -- A01K 2227/10 designates the subclass "Mammal" within the Cooperative Patent Classification for animal husbandry, covering inventions that involve breeding, raising, feeding, health management, or other technical aspects specific to mammalian species. This code helps separate mammal-related livestock technologies from those for birds, fish, or other animal groups.
[A01K 2227/101] Bovine -- A01K 2227/101 classifies patents concerning bovine, i.e., cattle, within the animal husbandry section that groups animals by species. It covers inventions related to the breeding, rearing, health, nutrition, genetics or management of cattle.
[A01K 2227/102] Caprine -- A01K 2227/102 (Caprine) is the CPC subclass covering goats and related species. It includes inventions concerning the breeding, rearing, feeding, health care, milking and other husbandry practices specific to caprine animals within the broader animal husbandry field.
[A01K 2227/103] Ovine -- A01K 2227/103 classifies patent subject matter that specifically concerns ovine animals, i.e., sheep, within the broader animal husbandry group of the CPC. It is used for inventions related to the breeding, feeding, health, handling, or production of sheep, distinguishing them from other livestock species.
[A01K 2227/105] Murine -- A01K 2227/105 designates patents dealing specifically with mice, covering methods, apparatuses or compounds for breeding, genetics, housing, health care, or other species-specific aspects of murine husbandry. It falls under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Animals characterised by species, focusing on technology tailored to this rodent.
[A01K 2227/106] Primate -- A01K 2227/106 is a CPC subclass under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Animals characterised by species, and it covers inventions related specifically to primates, including methods, apparatuses, and products for their keeping, breeding, feeding, or management in agricultural, experimental or related environments.
[A01K 2227/107] Rabbit -- A01K 2227/107 covers rabbits, a lagomorph species raised for meat, fur, breeding or research, fitting the animal husbandry subclass for species-specific animal categories.
[A01K 2227/108] Swine -- A01K 2227/108 designates patent subject matter concerning swine, covering their breeding, feeding, housing, health management and related agricultural practices. It resides in the CPC hierarchy under animal husbandry within agriculture.
[A01K 2227/30] Bird -- A01K 2227/30 classifies inventions related to birds within animal husbandry, covering breeding, rearing, feeding, housing and health management of avian species such as poultry, game birds and ornamental birds for meat, egg, feather or other production purposes. It falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Animals characterised by species.
[A01K 2227/40] Fish -- This CPC subclass A01K 2227/40 relates to fish as a species within animal husbandry, covering inventions for breeding, rearing, feeding, health management and harvesting of fish in aquaculture and related processing.
[A01K 2227/50] Amphibians, e.g. Xenopus -- A01K 2227/50 pertains to amphibians such as Xenopus, classifying them as a distinct group of animals characterised by species. It is placed within the hierarchy Human Necessities, Agriculture, Animal Husbandry, Animals characterised by species in the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01K 2227/70] Invertebrates -- A01K 2227/70 covers invertebrate species-such as insects, mollusks, crustaceans and other non-vertebrate organisms-reared or managed in animal husbandry for agricultural purposes. It resides under Human Necessities > Agriculture > Animal Husbandry > Animals characterised by species.
[A01K 2227/703] Worms, e.g. Caenorhabdities elegans -- The CPC entry A01K 2227/703, under Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Animals characterised by species, pertains to worms such as Caenorhabditis elegans. It covers inventions-methods, apparatuses, and biotechnological processes-focused on using these nematodes for research, screening, or production purposes.
[A01K 2227/706] Insects, e.g. Drosophila melanogaster, medfly -- A01K 2227/706 falls within the CPC hierarchy for animal husbandry and covers inventions directed specifically at insects identified by species, such as Drosophila melanogaster or the Mediterranean fruit fly. It includes methods, compositions or devices for breeding, rearing, testing or controlling these particular insects in agricultural or research contexts.
[A01K 2267/00] Animals characterised by purpose -- A01K 2267/00 covers inventions related to animals that are defined or selected for a specific purpose, such as dairy cattle, meat-type livestock, working horses, or pets bred for particular tasks. It includes breeding, genetic selection and management methods aimed at achieving the designated function of the animals.
[A01K 2267/01] Animal expressing industrially exogenous proteins -- The code refers to animals that have been genetically modified to produce non-native proteins for industrial purposes, such as pharmaceuticals, enzymes or other valuable biomolecules in their milk, blood or tissue. It covers the organisms and methods used to express these exogenous proteins in farmed species.
[A01K 2267/02] Animal zootechnically ameliorated -- A01K 2267/02 covers animals that have been zootechnically ameliorated, meaning their genetic, physiological or behavioural traits have been deliberately improved through selective breeding, genetic-modification or other husbandry techniques to enhance productivity, health or welfare for agricultural purposes.
[A01K 2267/025] Animal producing cells or organs for transplantation -- The class A01K 2267/025 covers animals that are bred, genetically modified, or otherwise engineered to serve as bioreactors producing cells, tissues or whole organs for transplantation into humans or other recipients. It includes transgenic livestock, cloned animals and similar organisms cultivated specifically for biomedical grafts, and falls within the animal-husbandry section of agricultural classifications.
[A01K 2267/03] Animal model, e.g. for test or diseases -- A01K 2267/03 relates to animal models used for testing or studying diseases, covering the selection or breeding of animals specifically for biomedical or veterinary research
[A01K 2267/0306] Animal model for genetic diseases -- The CPC subclass A01K 2267/0306 concerns the use of animals deliberately selected, bred or genetically engineered to serve as models for inherited disorders. It includes methods and systems that employ such animal models to study disease mechanisms, evaluate diagnostics or test therapeutic interventions for genetic diseases.
[A01K 2267/0312] Animal model for Alzheimer's disease -- A01K 2267/0312 covers the use of animals specifically bred or selected as experimental models to study Alzheimer's disease, including their genetic modification, induction of pathology, and evaluation of therapeutic interventions.
[A01K 2267/0318] Animal model for neurodegenerative disease, e.g. non- Alzheimer's -- The CPC entry A01K 2267/0318 falls under HUMAN NECESSITIES → AGRICULTURE → ANIMAL HUSBANDRY → Animals characterised by purpose and covers the use of animals as models for studying neurodegenerative diseases other than Alzheimer's, such as Parkinson's or Huntington's disease, to evaluate therapeutic approaches or understand disease mechanisms.
[A01K 2267/0325] Animal model for autoimmune diseases -- A01K 2267/0325 covers the use of animals as experimental models to study autoimmune diseases, including the induction, observation, and measurement of immune-mediated disorders for research or therapeutic testing. It falls under animal husbandry, specifically animals characterised by purpose, within the broader human necessities and agriculture hierarchy.
[A01K 2267/0331] Animal model for proliferative diseases -- The code A01K 2267/0331 covers the use of animals as experimental models to investigate proliferative disorders such as cancers or hyperplastic conditions, enabling the testing of therapeutic agents or the study of disease mechanisms. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for animal husbandry focused on purpose-specific animals, linking agricultural practice with biomedical research on abnormal cell growth.
[A01K 2267/0337] Animal models for infectious diseases -- A01K 2267/0337 covers the use of animals as experimental models to study, reproduce, or evaluate infectious diseases, including the development of disease-specific strains, infection protocols, and validation of diagnostic or therapeutic methods. It is applied when the animal's purpose is to mimic human or veterinary pathogens for research, vaccine testing, or drug efficacy assessment within the animal husbandry subclass.
[A01K 2267/0343] Animal model for prion disease -- A01K 2267/0343 covers the use of animals as experimental models for studying prion diseases, including techniques for inducing infection, monitoring disease development, and evaluating diagnostic or therapeutic approaches in livestock or laboratory species.
[A01K 2267/035] Animal model for multifactorial diseases -- A01K 2267/035 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to animals used as models for studying multifactorial diseases, which are conditions caused by a combination of genetic and environmental factors. This classification is part of a broader category that includes animals characterized by their specific purposes in research or agriculture.
[A01K 2267/0356] Animal model for processes and diseases of the central nervous system, e.g. stress, learning, schizophrenia, pain, epilepsy -- The entry A01K 2267/0356 covers the use of animals specially bred or selected as models for investigating central-nervous-system processes and disorders, such as stress, learning, schizophrenia, pain and epilepsy. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Human Necessities → Agriculture → Animal Husbandry → Animals characterised by purpose.
[A01K 2267/0362] Animal model for lipid/glucose metabolism, e.g. obesity, type-2 diabetes -- A01K 2267/0362 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to animals used as models for studying lipid and glucose metabolism, including conditions like obesity and type-2 diabetes. These animal models are crucial for research into metabolic disorders and developing therapeutic interventions.
[A01K 2267/0368] Animal model for inflammation -- A01K 2267/0368 covers inventions that use animals as experimental models to investigate inflammatory processes, including methods for inducing, measuring, or treating inflammation in such models. It
[A01K 2267/0375] Animal model for cardiovascular diseases -- This subclass covers the use of animals specifically selected or engineered as models to study or simulate cardiovascular diseases, including methods for inducing, monitoring and treating such conditions in the animal. It relates to research tools in veterinary and biomedical contexts that facilitate drug testing and the investigation of cardiovascular pathophysiology.
[A01K 2267/0381] Animal model for diseases of the hematopoietic system -- The entry A01K 2267/0381 covers the use of animals as experimental models to study disorders of the blood-forming (hematopoietic) system, such as anemia, leukemia or clotting abnormalities, providing a basis for research, diagnosis or therapy development.
[A01K 2267/0387] Animal model for diseases of the immune system -- A01K 2267/0387 covers the use of animals as experimental models to investigate diseases of the immune system, including methods for inducing, monitoring or treating immune disorders in animal subjects to support biomedical research and drug development.
[A01K 2267/0393] Animal model comprising a reporter system for screening tests -- A01K 2267/0393 covers animal models that have been engineered to contain a reporter system, such as a detectable gene or protein, which produces a measurable signal in response to a biological event. These models are used in screening tests to evaluate the activity, toxicity or efficacy of compounds, genes or environmental agents, facilitating rapid assessment in drug discovery, diagnostics or environmental monitoring.

[A01K 37/00] Constraining birds, e.g. wing clamps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for preventing birds from flying, escaping, sitting down, picking or dropping excrements.  Attachments rings for birds feet.  Anti-picking devices, e.g. blinders, lenses or goggles.  Constraining birds for artificial insemination.
[A01K 39/00] Feeding or drinking appliances for poultry or other birds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Feeding or drinking devices for poultry or other birds, may be automatic (e.g. battery feeding system).  Window mountable bird feeders.  Bird feeding supports.  Feeding or drinking devices for wild poultry or birds, e.g. hummingbird feeders.  Feeding or drinking devices for domestic birds.  Bird feeders may be disposable.  Bird feeders may recycle bird food.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[A01K 39/01] Feeding devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Poultry feeders, often comprising mechanical distributing means from a storage to one or more feeding stations, e.g. a long trough.  Chain feeders.  Conveying chains or belts therefor, conveying cables.  Hoppers therefor, including vibrators or controls.  Conveyor or trough combinations therefor, e.g. conveyor in trough wall.  Battery feeding systems, without other important battery features.  Devices may include feed level detectors.  Trough-type feeders with connection means for distributing systems.  Feed-flow regulating means.  Bird-feeding devices for outside, may be insect-proof. [A01K 39/0113] with means to prevent other animals or insects, e.g. squirrels or ants, from eating also -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Squirrel proof bird feeding device, by means of e.g. a spinning mechanism triggered by weight, electric shock device, slippery side panels, etc.  Baffles for preventing animals from climbing up a birdhouse.  Rodent repelling by means of e.g. several water filled moats, protective pet dish, etc.
[A01K 39/012] filling automatically -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bird or poultry feeding devices with automatic filling of a feed compartment, e.g. gravity.  Devices include a storage, e.g. incorporated hopper.  Excluded are the possible conveying means from a storage to the feeder(s): See A01K39/01 .  Means for controlling the feeding device, e.g. animal actuated.  Feeders with multiple levels.  Devices may be combined with shelters.  Devices may include time-control means.
[A01K 39/0125] Panfeeding systems; Feeding pans therefor -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Pan feeding systems for poultry.  Device may include an adjustable feed level control.  Means of controlling supply of feed to the trough.  Feeding device may include time controlling means.  Details therefor, e.g. feed drop tube, etc.  Feeding pans, feed box, etc.
[A01K 39/014] Feed troughs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Bird troughs, e.g. with bird-actuated covers.  Bird throwers, i.e. throwing-out feed, by means of e.g. propelling, centrifugal.  Troughs for batteries.  Grids for troughs, anti-roost devices for troughs.  Trough aspect is prevailed, feeder may include a hopper or other delivery means, e.g. a foraminous hopper for direct access.  Devices may include a shelter.  Heating or lighting means may be incorporated.
[A01K 39/02] Drinking appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Poultry watering or providing liquids to poultry, e.g. nectar, liquid dispensing systems.  Devices may be bird-actuated, other than nipples.  Drinking system may be remote controlled.  Flush apparatus for watering system.  Drinking troughs; their accessories, e.g. covers.  Details of general importance, e.g. anti-freeze devices, heating devices, overflow plugs.  Battery drinking systems.  Cleaning apparatus for watering devices.  Structures for supporting water systems, e.g. a hanger. [A01K 43/00] Testing, sorting or cleaning eggs ; -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for performing different kind of treatment steps with eggs, e.g. transporting, conveying, picking up, putting down, ordering, aligning, emptying, irradiating etc, impregnating, crack-checking, cracking (e.g. for embryo extraction), sorting, measuring/weighing, etc.  Devices for packaging and storing eggs.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Egg collecting or counting -> A01K31/165 - Investigating or analyzing eggs, e.g. by candling -> G01N33/08

[A01K 45/00] Other aviculture appliances, e.g. devices for determining whether a bird is about to lay -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Transporting or conveying birds (in particular poultry in quantities). Influencing or measuring biological activity, e.g. by electrical field, blue light.  Irradiation or other exposure to treatment or stimuli to increase or improve egg or meat production or otherwise achieve a beneficial physiological change.  Assisting birth, e.g. shell-openers. Poultry collecting devices, by means of e.g. picking up, etc.  Devices for transporting poultry, e.g. containers.

Introducing material into eggs, e.g. feeding a subject prior to hatch, fertilizing eggs.  Pre-hatch sex determination.  Method for localizing allantoic fluid. [A01K 47/00] Apiculture -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Beehives, nesting boxes, e.g. Kenya hives.  Beehives may be portable, disposable, biodegradable, etc.  Covers for beehives.  Bee observation devices.
[A01K 47/02] Construction or arrangement of frames for honeycombs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Frames for, or including, honeycombs.  Methods and devices for producing or assembling the frames.  Frames may be foldable.  Reinforcing elements for honeycombs.  Means for holding honeycombs or frames in the beehive.  Means for keeping distance between frames or honeycombs.  Honeycombs may be complete (ready to breed), or to be completed by bees.  Combination of frames with honeycombs, the frames having particular importance.  Mounting honeycombs into frames, e.g. by heating wires.
[A01K 47/04] Artificial honeycombs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Artificial honeycombs or foundations.  Methods and devices for producing them.  Ways of assembling them into frames.  Extraction of honey from specially designed honeycomb, e.g. by heat.  Honeycombs may be complete (ready to breed), or to be completed by bees.  Means for producing foundation plates, e.g. wax foundation.
[A01K 47/06] Other details of beehives, e.g. ventilating devices, entrances to hives, guards, partitions or bee escapes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Entrance-gates, may be anti-parasite. Control devices for entrance, etc.  Trapping (sick) bees device.  Ventilation means for beehives.  Methods for obtaining honey from bees.  Bee venom collecting device.  Pollinating systems (removing pollen from the bees when returning, e.g. brush at the entrance).
[A01K 49/00] Rearing-boxes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Any means for rearing bees, not covered by other groups.  Treating bees, e.g. by irradiation.  (Artificial) insemination of queen bees.  Measuring or testing bees.  Artificially hatching queen bees.  Containers for transporting (e.g. shipping) queen bees.

Bale distributors or shredders for (outdoor) feeding.  Feeders may contain mixers, e.g. mixing, mincing, weighing devices.  Feeding wagons.  Feeding stacks. [A01K 51/00] Appliances for treating beehives or parts thereof, e.g. for cleaning or disinfecting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Anti-parasite entrances. Bee feeders may be automatic.  Devices may include heating system.  Means for supplying fresh water for the bees.

Devices for controlling/ monitoring behaviour of bees.  Devices for lifting an moving bee- hives.  Bee smokers.  Devices for assessing bee temperament.
[A01K 57/00] Appliances for providing, preventing or catching swarms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for detecting bee-swarms.  Devices for catching or trapping bee swarms.  Devices for controlling swarming of bees, e.g. by means of pheromones.  Appliances for re-queening a hive.  Devices for breeding bee colonies.
