It includes systems that create motion or other visual stimuli to frighten or confuse target species.
[A01M 29/08] using reflection, colours or films with specific transparency or reflectivity -- A01M 29/08 pertains to agricultural scaring or repelling devices that employ reflective surfaces, coloured or tinted films, or other materials with controlled transparency or reflectivity to deter animals, especially birds, from crops or other protected areas.
[A01M 29/10] using light sources, e.g. lasers or flashing lights -- A01M 29/10 relates to agricultural scaring or repelling devices that use light sources, such as lasers, flashing or strobe lights, to deter animals-especially birds-from crops or facilities.
[A01M 29/12] using odoriferous substances, e.g. aromas, pheromones or chemical agents -- This subclass covers devices or methods that repel or deter animals by emitting odorous substances such as aromas, pheromones or other chemical agents. It is applied in agricultural contexts for bird-scaring, pest control or other animal-deterrent systems.
[A01M 29/14] using thermal effects -- A01M 29/14 refers to the use of thermal means-such as heat, fire, heated surfaces or infrared emissions-to frighten or repel animals, especially birds, in agricultural settings. It covers devices that generate or harness temperature differences to create an uncomfortable environment that drives pests away without physical contact. This subclass falls under scaring or repelling devices within the broader category of catching, trapping or scaring of animals.
[A01M 29/16] using sound waves -- A01M 29/16 covers agricultural devices that repel or scare animals by emitting sound waves, such as acoustic bird-deterrent systems that generate specific frequencies, pulses or noises to startle or discourage wildlife from entering fields or other cultivated areas. This subgroup falls under scaring or repelling apparatus within the catching, trapping or scaring of animals class.
[A01M 29/18] using ultrasonic signals -- The A01M 29/18 subclass covers scaring or repelling devices that employ ultrasonic signals to deter animals, especially birds, in agricultural environments. It includes systems that generate high-frequency sound to create an aversive effect without physical contact, fitting within the broader catching, trapping or scaring of animals category.
[A01M 29/20] with generation of periodically explosive reports -- A01M 29/20 covers scaring or repelling devices that create periodic explosive sounds to deter animals, such as bird-scarers that fire small charges or generate loud bangs at set intervals. The classification applies to agricultural tools designed to protect crops by using repeated explosive reports as a deterrent.
[A01M 29/22] using vibrations -- A01M 29/22 covers inventions that use vibrations to scare or repel animals, particularly birds, in agricultural settings. The classification pertains to devices that generate mechanical or acoustic vibrations to deter wildlife from crops or facilities.
[A01M 29/24] using electric or magnetic effects, e.g. electric shocks, magnetic fields or microwaves -- The A01M 29/24 classification covers systems that deter or repel animals by applying electric, magnetic or microwave energy, such as shock devices, magnetic fields or directed microwaves. These technologies are used as bird-scaring or animal-repellent apparatuses within agricultural settings.
[A01M 29/26] specially adapted for birds, e.g. electrified rods, cords or strips -- A01M 29/26 covers bird-scaring or repelling devices that are specially adapted for avian use, such as electrified rods, cords or strips designed to deter birds from crops or other areas. These devices are part
[A01M 29/28] specially adapted for insects -- A01M 29/28 covers devices specifically adapted to scare or repel insects, such as modified bird-scaring apparatus that use physical, acoustic or visual means to keep insects away from crops or other areas. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Catching, Trapping or Scaring of Animals, focusing on insect-specific deterrent technologies.
[A01M 29/30] preventing or obstructing access or passage, e.g. by means of barriers, spikes, cords, obstacles or sprinkled water -- A01M 29/30 covers methods and devices that prevent animals from accessing or moving through an area by creating physical or sensory obstacles such as barriers, spikes, cords, obstacles, or sprinkled water, serving as deterrents in agricultural settings. This subclass falls under scaring or repelling devices within the broader human necessities-agriculture-catching, trapping or scaring of animals hierarchy.
[A01M 29/32] specially adapted for birds, e.g. spikes -- A01M 29/32 covers bird-deterring apparatus that are specially adapted for birds, such as spikes and similar devices mounted on surfaces to prevent perching or roosting. These devices belong to the scaring or repelling category within agricultural animal control.
[A01M 29/34] specially adapted for insects -- A01M 29/34 concerns devices specially adapted for insects, used to scare, repel or trap insects in agricultural environments, such as insect-specific deterrents or trapping apparatus.
[A01M 3/00] Manual implements, other than sprayers or powder distributors, for catching or killing insects, e.g. butterfly nets -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example from NL1019153CC:  **Glossary:** - Insects: Arthropods (insects, spiders, ...)
[A01M 3/002] Insect nets -- A01M 3/002 covers manually operated implements for catching or killing insects, such as butterfly nets and similar devices, and excludes sprayers or powder-distributing tools.
[A01M 3/005] Manual suction tools for catching insects -- A01M 3/005 covers hand-operated suction devices used to capture insects, such as portable vacuum tubes or suction cups, intended for agricultural or pest-control purposes. It falls under manual implements for catching or killing insects within the broader hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture and animal trapping.
[A01M 3/007] Manual heating or burning apparatus, e.g. using steam, heated elements or waves  flame-throwers  A01M15/00 -- A01M 3/007 classifies manually operated devices that apply heat, steam, heated elements or flame to kill or deter insects and other pests, such as handheld steam generators, heated plates or small flame-throwers used in agricultural settings. It is a subcategory of manual implements for catching or killing insects, situated under catching, trapping or scaring of animals in the CPC hierarchy.
[A01M 3/02] Fly-swatters -- A01M 3/02 covers manual tools designed to swat flies and other insects, such as hand-held fly-swatters, used in agriculture and other settings for catching or killing insects.
[A01M 3/022] using adhesives -- This subclass covers manual tools that use adhesive substances to capture insects, such as sticky nets or glue-coated traps, for agricultural pest control. It applies to implements other than sprayers or powder distributors and includes methods of applying adhesives to catch or kill insects.
[A01M 3/025] using electrocution -- A01M 3/025 covers manual devices, other than sprayers, used to catch or kill insects by applying an electric shock, for example net-type tools like butterfly nets that electrocute captured insects. The classification groups hand-operated implements that rely on electrocution rather than mechanical crushing or chemical means.
[A01M 3/027] using resilient means or projectiles -- A01M 3/027 covers methods that employ resilient mechanisms or projectiles to capture or kill insects using manual tools such as nets, traps or similar implements, excluding spray devices. It is part of the broader class for manual implements designed for insect catching or killing within agricultural animal-control technologies.
[A01M 3/04] with adhesive surfaces -- A01M 3/04 covers manually operated tools used in agriculture to capture or kill insects that employ an adhesive surface, such as sticky nets or pads, rather than spraying or powdering agents. These implements are designed for catching insects by trapping them on a tacky surface.
[A01M 31/00] Hunting appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Hunting,  Detecting and monitoring the presence of prey animals  Example from WO2012057814:
[A01M 31/002] Detecting animals in a given area -- A01M31/002 relates to methods and devices that detect the presence or location of animals within a defined area, aiding hunting appliances and other tools used for catching, trapping or scaring animals in agricultural settings. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture and animal control.
[A01M 31/004] Game callers -- This subclass covers devices that produce sounds to attract or repel game animals, such as bird calls, elk whistles and electronic callers used in hunting. It includes both portable and fixed acoustic emitters designed for use in hunting contexts, and is grouped under hunting appliances in the agriculture section.
[A01M 31/006] Game carriers -- A01M 31/006 refers to game carriers, such as cages, crates, nets, bags or sleds used to hold and transport live animals caught during hunting, including portable or mounted devices that protect the game while loading, handling and moving to a processing area.
[A01M 31/008] Lure dispensing devices -- A01M 31/008 encompasses devices that store and dispense lures to attract animals for hunting or trapping, including mechanisms that release scent, sound or visual attractants to improve capture efficiency. These lure dispensing devices are classified under hunting appliances within the agricultural sector.
[A01M 31/02] Shooting stands -- A01M 31/02 relates to shooting stands employed in hunting, providing a stable platform that supports a firearm for improved accuracy and safety while the hunter remains positioned or concealed.
[A01M 31/025] Hunting blinds, i.e. camouflage -- A01M 31/025 covers hunting blinds and similar camouflage structures designed to conceal hunters or observers while they wait for game. The classification includes portable or fixed shelters, screens and other concealment devices that blend with the environment to improve hunting effectiveness. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture and the catching, trapping or scaring of animals.
[A01M 31/04] Beater rattles -- A01M 31/04 covers beater rattles, devices that generate noise or vibration to startle, drive or herd animals during hunting or wildlife management. These appliances are typically handheld or attached to a pole and are used to flush game from cover or guide it toward traps. The class falls under the broader category of catching, trapping or scaring of animals within agriculture.
[A01M 31/06] Decoys -- A01M 31/06 concerns decoys used as hunting appliances to attract or distract game, encompassing visual, auditory or combined devices that may be stationary or movable. These items are designed to simulate prey or mates to increase hunting efficiency and are classified under catching, trapping or scaring of animals.
[A01M 5/00] Catching insects in fields, gardens, or forests by movable appliances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Example from US5400543:  **Glossary:** - Insects: Arthropods (insects, spiders, ...)
[A01M 5/02] Portable appliances -- A01M 5/02 covers portable devices used to capture insects in agricultural or natural environments such as fields, gardens, and forests. These movable appliances include hand-held traps, sweep nets, and other lightweight equipment designed for field deployment.
[A01M 5/04] Wheeled machines, with means for stripping-off or brushing-off insects -- A01M 5/04 covers wheeled devices equipped with mechanisms that strip or brush insects from crops, foliage or ground surfaces. These machines are used in fields, gardens or forests to capture insects by moving across the area.
[A01M 5/06] with adhesive surfaces -- A01M 5/06 pertains to movable insect-catching apparatus that employ adhesive surfaces to trap insects in agricultural or natural settings such as fields, gardens and forests. The devices are designed to be repositioned as needed, offering a non-lethal method of control within the broader category of catching, trapping or scaring of animals.
[A01M 5/08] with fans -- A01M 5/08 covers movable insect-catching devices that employ fans to draw insects into a capture mechanism, such as suction traps or blower-type nets used in fields, gardens or forests. The fan-assisted approach enhances collection efficiency compared with passive traps.

[A01M 7/0053] Mounting of the spraybooms -- A01M 7/0053 covers the ways spray-booms are attached to agricultural or animal-scaring equipment, including brackets, hinges, frames and other devices that secure the boom to a vehicle, platform or structure. It relates to special adaptations of liquid-spraying apparatus used for dispensing sprays, herbicides or deterrents in farming or animal-control operations. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Agriculture → Catching, Trapping or Scaring of Animals.
[A01M 7/0057] with active regulation of the boom position -- A01M 7/0057 covers agricultural sprayers that incorporate mechanisms to actively control the position of the spray boom during operation, allowing dynamic adjustment of height, angle or lateral movement to match field conditions or target crops. This active regulation improves coverage uniformity and reduces waste compared with fixed-boom designs.
[A01M 7/006] Mounting of the nozzles -- A01M 7/006 covers the arrangement and mounting of spray-nozzles on liquid-spraying devices used in agricultural animal-control methods such as catching, trapping or scaring. It includes the mechanical means of attaching, positioning or adjusting the nozzles to achieve the desired spray pattern or coverage for deterrent or capture systems.
[A01M 7/0064] Protection shields -- A01M 7/0064 covers protection shields used with liquid-spraying devices designed for catching, trapping or scaring animals. The shields guard the operator, the equipment or the surrounding area from spray splash, impact or unintended exposure while the apparatus is in use. This subclass falls under special adaptations of spraying apparatus within the broader agricultural and animal-control categories.
[A01M 7/0067] with recovering of liquids -- A01M 7/0067 covers liquid-spraying devices that incorporate means to collect and reuse the sprayed fluid, such as recovery chambers, recirculation loops or drainage systems, enabling efficient application while minimizing waste in agricultural or animal-control operations.
[A01M 7/0071] Construction of the spray booms -- This subclass covers the structural design and mounting of spray booms used on liquid-spraying equipment in agriculture, detailing shapes, lengths, joint mechanisms and support arrangements that allow the booms to be positioned, folded or adjusted for effective application.
[A01M 7/0075] including folding means -- The subclass A01M 7/0075 covers liquid-spraying devices used in agricultural or animal-control applications that incorporate folding or collapsible features. It includes sprayers, nozzles, hoses or other spray-equipment designed to fold for compact storage, transport or ease of deployment while maintaining functionality.
[A01M 7/0078] including break-away devices -- A01M 7/0078 relates to liquid-spraying devices used for catching, trapping or scaring animals that incorporate break-away mechanisms, allowing the spray unit to release or detach from its mounting when a predetermined force or condition is exceeded. These adaptations enhance safety and prevent damage by enabling the apparatus to separate automatically under stress.
[A01M 7/0082] Undercarriages, frames, mountings, couplings, tanks -- A01M 7/0082 covers the structural components of liquid-spraying devices used in animal catching, trapping or scaring, such as undercarriages, frames, mountings, couplings and tanks that support or contain the spray system. The subclass addresses special adaptations or arrangements of these apparatuses within agricultural pest-control equipment.
[A01M 7/0085] Tanks -- This subclass includes designs and configurations of tanks that store and supply liquids to specialized spraying devices used in agricultural animal-control operations, such as catching, trapping or scaring animals. It addresses tank capacity, material, mounting and integration with pumps or nozzles to achieve reliable delivery of the applied liquid.
[A01M 7/0089] Regulating or controlling systems  the delivery being related to the movement of a vehicle  B05B9/06 -- A01M 7/0089 pertains to regulating or controlling systems for liquid-spraying apparatus, specifically where the delivery is influenced by the movement of a vehicle, integrating agricultural spraying with vehicular dynamics. This classification falls under the broader categories of human necessities and agriculture, focusing on specialized adaptations for animal catching, trapping, or scaring.
[A01M 7/0092] Adding active material  used as well for the introduction of fertilisers in water  A01C23/042 -- A01M 7/0092 pertains to special adaptations or arrangements of liquid-spraying apparatus designed for adding active materials, such as fertilizers, into water, which is also covered under the classification A01C23/042. This entry focuses on the integration of substances that enhance agricultural productivity through precise application methods.
[A01M 7/0096] Testing of spray-patterns -- This subclass deals with methods and devices for evaluating the distribution of liquid from spray equipment used in agricultural animal control. It covers testing of spray patterns, including measurement techniques, visual or instrumental analyses, and procedures to ensure adequate coverage for catching, trapping or scaring animals.

[A01N] PRESERVATION OF BODIES OF HUMANS OR ANIMALS OR PLANTS OR PARTS THEREOF -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions, physical forms thereof, and the application or method of use of specific materials, compositions or single compounds for the following purposes:  Compositions used to protect the wound and scions of newly grafted plants or to cover the wounds on pruned plants (i.e. grafting wax).  Chemical agents used for the sexual sterilisation of invertebrates (e.g. insects).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preservation of foods, foodstuffs or non-alcoholic beverages -> A23B - Preservation or chemical ripening of harvested fruits or vegetables -> A23B7/00 - Processes specially adapted for the preservation of materials in order to produce animal feeding-stuffs -> A23K30/00 - Compositions for medical, dental or toiletry purposes which kill or prevent the growth or proliferation of unwanted organisms -> A61K - Sex sterilants for animals other than invertebrates -> A61K  **Glossary:** - acaricide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying mites and ticks or making them less harmful - alkali metal: one of the following elements: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs or Fr - alkaline earth metal: one of the following elements: Ca, Sr, Ba or Ra - arthropodicide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying arthropods, e.g. insects, arachnids, crustaceans or making them less harmful - biocide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing, destroying or mitigating any living organism (e.g. plant or animal). Examples of a biocide are: acaricide, arthropodicide, fungicide, insecticide, molluscicide or rodenticide (see Synonyms and Keywords). - disinfectant: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing, destroying or mitigating microorganisms - fungicide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying moulds and fungi or making them less harmful - halogen: one of the following elements: F, Cl, Br, I or At - heavy metal: metal other than light metal - herbicide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying plant life or making them less harmful - insecticide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying insects or making them less harmful - light metal: one of the following elements: alkali metal, alkaline earth metal, Be, Al or Mg - metal: element other than non-metal - molluscicide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying molluscs, e.g. snails, clams or making them less harmful - non-metal: one of the following elements: H, B, C, Si, N, P, O, S, Se, Te, noble gas or halogen - pesticide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying any pest (e.g., insects, rodents) or making them less harmful - plant: young tree, shrub, vegetable or flower newly planted or intended for planting - plant growth regulator: materials which alter the plant or may affect plant growth through a chemical modification of the plant metabolism, such as auxins - rodenticide: any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying rodents, e.g. rats or mice, or making them less harmful
[A01N 1/00] Preservation of bodies of humans or animals, or plants, or parts thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions and methods intended for use in the preservation of dead bodies of humans or animals, for example to prevent them from further decay, or for use in taxidermy.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Preservation of foodstuffs -> A23 - Medicinal preparations containing materials from mammals or birds, e.g. blood, sperm -> A61K35/12
[A01N 1/10] Preservation of living parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions, apparatus or methods for the preservation, including cryopreservation, of excised living body parts, such as organs and parts thereof, in order to maintain their viability.
[A01N 1/12] Chemical aspects of preservation -- Group A01N1/12 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/52 . Groups A01N1/12 and C12N5/52 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/122] Preservation or perfusion media -- Group A01N1/122 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/522 . Groups A01N1/122 and C12N5/522 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/124] Disinfecting agents, e.g. antimicrobials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions/methods in which disinfection agents are used for preservation purposes, for example, to increase the storage stability of, e.g. organs by inhibiting bacterial growth.
[A01N 1/125] Freeze protecting agents, e.g. cryoprotectants or osmolarity regulators -- Group A01N1/125 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/525 . Groups A01N1/125 and C12N5/525 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/126] Physiologically active agents, e.g. antioxidants or nutrients -- Group A01N1/126 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/526 . Groups A01N1/126 and C12N5/526 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/128] Chemically defined matrices for immobilising, holding or storing living parts, e.g. alginate gels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Formulations like gels or solid fibrous matrices but also ice-seeding particles.
[A01N 1/14] Mechanical aspects of preservation -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mechanical inventions that are not covered by any of the groups defining containers, perfusion apparatus or refrigeration apparatus, e.g. access control for preserved samples.
[A01N 1/142] Apparatus -- Group A01N1/142 is impacted by reclassification into groups A61M1/0272 - A61M1/0277 and C12N5/542 . Groups A01N1/142 , A61M1/0272 - A61M1/0277 and C12N5/542 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/143] for organ perfusion -- The code A01N 1/143 falls under the CPC subgroup for preserving bodies, specifically covering techniques that perfuse organs to keep them viable during storage or transport. It includes systems and devices that circulate protective fluids through isolated organs, used in agriculture, veterinary, medical
[A01N 1/144] for temperature control, e.g. refrigerators or freeze-drying apparatus -- Group A01N1/144 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/544 . Groups A01N1/144 and C12N5/544 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/145] Stationary or portable vessels generating cryogenic temperatures, e.g. liquid nitrogen baths -- Group A01N1/145 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/545 . Groups A01N1/145 and C12N5/545 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/146] Non-refrigerated containers specially adapted for transporting or storing living parts whilst preserving -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Containers not comprising mechanical means that actively alter temperature. The use of phase change materials, e.g. ice, to passively keep the living part cool is not considered "active" refrigeration.
[A01N 1/147] Carriers for immersion in cryogenic fluid for slow freezing or vitrification -- Group A01N1/147 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/547 . Groups A01N1/147 and C12N5/547 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/148] with provisions specially adapted for transporting -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Documents specifically addressing a transport aspect, e.g. providing means to counteract shocks or tilting.
[A01N 1/16] Physical preservation processes -- Group A01N1/16 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/56 . Groups A01N1/16 and C12N5/56 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/162] Temperature processes, e.g. following predefined temperature changes over time -- Group A01N1/162 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/562 . Groups A01N1/162 and C12N5/562 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/165] Pressure processes, e.g. following predefined pressure changes over time -- Group A01N1/165 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/565 . Groups A01N1/165 and C12N5/565 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 1/168] using electromagnetic fields or radiation -- Group A01N1/168 is impacted by reclassification into group C12N5/568 . Groups A01N1/168 and C12N5/568 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[A01N 2300/00] Combinations or mixtures of active ingredients covered by classes -- A01N2300/00 is only used as a subsequent symbol in C-Sets and should not be allocated as a single symbol. Detailed information about C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is present in the Definitions of A01N27/00 .
[A01N 25/00] Biocides -- Attention is drawn to the definitions of groups of chemical elements following the title of section C . In groups A01N27/00 - A01N65/00 , the last place priority rule is applied, i.e. at each hierarchical level, in the absence of an indication to the contrary, an active ingredient is classified in the last appropriate place. A composition, i.e. a mixture of two or more active ingredients is classified in the last of groups A01N27/00 - A01N65/00 that provides for at least one of these active ingredients. Any part of a composition which is not identified by the classification according to Note (3), and which itself is determined to be novel and non-obvious, must also be classified in the last appropriate place in groups A01N27/00 - A01N65/00 . The part can be either a single ingredient or a composition in itself. Any part of a composition which is not identified by the classification according to Note (3) or (4), and which is considered to represent information of interest for search, may also be classified in the last appropriate place in groups A01N27/00 - A01N65/00 . This can, for example, be the case when it is considered of interest to enable searching of compositions using a combination of classification symbols. Such non-obligatory classification should be given as "additional information". Where a compound is described as existing in tautomeric forms, it is classified as if existing in the form which is classified last in the system. Compounds covered by different main groups according to alternatively specified parts of their formulae are classified in every one of the relevant main groups. Salts formed between two or more organic compounds are classified as the compound providing the essential ion and it is also classified as the compound providing the other ion. Salts or metal chelates of an organic compound are classified as that compound. In this subclass, a foodstuff is not considered as an active ingredient. Different materials applied in sequence, at different times, are considered as a mixture of all materials employed. Synergistic or potentiated compositions are classified as if the synergist or potentiator were an active ingredient. In groups A01N25/00 - A01N65/00 , the symbol X means nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur or a halogen; Y means nitrogen, oxygen or sulfur. A dotted line between atoms indicates an optional bond, e.g. indicates one or two single bonds or a double bond.
[A01N 25/002] containing a foodstuff as carrier or diluent, i.e. baits -- A01N 25/002 relates to pest-control compositions that use a foodstuff as the carrier or diluent, essentially forming baits that attract the target organism. The subclass includes formulations where edible ingredients deliver biocidal agents to the pest.
[A01N 25/004] rodenticidal -- A01N 25/004 covers rodenticidal agents, a subgroup of biocides used to control rodents that threaten the preservation of human, animal or plant bodies and parts thereof. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture and preservation of bodies, specifying chemical or biological means for rodent extermination.
[A01N 25/006] insecticidal -- A01N 25/006 designates insecticidal agents used for protecting crops, livestock, humans or plants from insects. It includes chemical, biological or combined substances and their preparation, as
[A01N 25/008] molluscicidal -- A01N 25/008 covers molluscicidal agents, i
[A01N 25/02] containing liquids as carriers, diluents or solvents -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions in which the solvent system is the distinguishing feature. Decisive is the actually disclosed form: solutions which are intended to be emulsified or dispersed such as emulsifiable or dispersible concentrates usually obtain the classification A01N25/02 whereas the final diluted products obtain classification A01N25/04 .
[A01N 25/04] Dispersions, or gels -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group also covers nanoemulsions or nanodispersions.  In accordance with the Specification Guidelines for Liquid Formulated Pesticides of the FAO (cf. http://www.fao.org/docrep/007/y4353e/y4353e0b.htm), emulsifiable or dispersible concentrates are considered solutions whereas suspension concentrates, flowable concentrates, capsule suspensions and oil-based suspension concentrates are suspensions.
[A01N 25/06] Aerosols -- A01N 25/06 relates to aerosol preparations that contain biocidal substances for the preservation or protection of humans, animals, plants or their parts. It covers pressurised containers that disperse fine droplets of chemical agents used for disinfection, pest control or other protective treatments.
[A01N 25/08] containing solids as carriers or diluents -- This subclass deals with formulations for plant protection that incorporate solid carriers or diluents, such as granules, powders, or tablets, used to deliver active biocidal ingredients. It covers the composition and preparation of solid carrier-based agrochemical products.
[A01N 25/10] Macromolecular compounds -- A01N 25/10 covers macromolecular compounds used as biocidal agents for preserving crops, livestock, or stored plant material. It includes polymers, peptides and other large molecules that act to control pests, pathogens or decay, fitting within the agriculture-biocides branch of the Human Necessities hierarchy.
[A01N 25/12] Powders or granules -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Also co-crystals or polymorphs.
[A01N 25/14] wettable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Wettable powders, i.e. exclusively suspendable or dispersible powders or granulates. (Water) soluble powders: A01N25/12 .
[A01N 25/16] Foams -- Foams are dispersions of gas bubbles in a liquid matrix used as carriers for biocidal agents applied to crops, livestock or stored produce. The classification covers formulations where the foam itself delivers pesticides, disinfectants or preservative chemicals to control pests, diseases or spoilage.
[A01N 25/18] Vapour or smoke emitting compositions with delayed or sustained release -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Compositions/methods relating to sustained/delayed release of compounds in the vapor phase and not to a release in the liquid phase. In case of doubt this classification may be given.
[A01N 25/20] Combustible or heat-generating compositions -- A01N 25/20 covers compositions that produce heat or flame for use as biocidal agents in agriculture, such as incendiary or self-heating formulations applied to crops, livestock, or storage facilities to control pests and pathogens.
[A01N 25/22] containing ingredients stabilising the active ingredients -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Ingredients which chemically stabilise the active ingredient(s). Physical stabilisation such as crystallisation inhibition does not obtain this classification.
[A01N 25/24] containing ingredients to enhance the sticking of the active ingredients -- A01N 25/24 covers formulations that include additives to improve the adhesion of active biocidal agents to surfaces or target organisms. It applies to pesticides, disinfectants and similar products where sticking agents are incorporated to ensure the active ingredient remains effective after application.
[A01N 25/26] in coated particulate form -- A01N 25/26 designates biocidal agents that are provided as coated particulate materials, such as granules or powders with a protective or controlled-release layer, intended for preserving plants, animals or humans from pests and diseases.
[A01N 25/28] Microcapsules -- A01N 25/28 relates to microcapsules employed in biocidal formulations for the preservation of plants, animals or humans, typically within agricultural applications. It covers encapsulated active agents that release gradually to protect crops or other biological material from pests, diseases or spoilage.
[A01N 25/30] characterised by the surfactants -- A01N 25/30 covers agricultural biocidal formulations in which surfactants are a defining feature of the composition. The subclass includes mixtures where surfactants are used to enhance spreading, wetting or adhesion of the active ingredient on plants, animals, humans or related materials for preservation or protection.
[A01N 25/32] Ingredients for reducing the noxious effect of the active substances to organisms other than pests, e.g. toxicity reducing compositions, self-destructing compositions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Only given as main (first) classification in cases where a compound is used for the first time as safener. When a known safener is used for the first time with a particular active ingredient, A01N25/32 is given as combination-set.
[A01N 25/34] Shaped forms, e.g. sheets, not provided for in any other sub-group of this main group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Tablet formulations and to formulations directed at nanosized particulate matter having a particular form such as nanofibers.
[A01N 27/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing hydrocarbons -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 29/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing halogenated hydrocarbons -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 29/02] Acyclic compounds or compounds containing halogen attached to an aliphatic side-chain of a cycloaliphatic ring system -- A01N 29/02 covers acyclic compounds or those where a halogen is bound to an aliphatic side-chain of a cycloaliphatic ring, used as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators that contain halogenated hydrocarbons. It falls under the agriculture branch of the Human Necessities section, specifically for preserving bodies of humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 29/04] Halogen directly attached to a carbocyclic ring system -- A01N 29/04 covers chemical compounds in which a halogen atom is bonded directly to a carbocyclic ring, used as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators containing halogenated hydrocarbons. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for agriculture-related preservation of human, animal or plant bodies.
[A01N 29/06] Hexachlorocyclohexane -- A01N 29/06 covers hexachlorocyclohexane, a halogenated hydrocarbon employed as a biocide, pest repellent, attractant or plant growth regulator for protecting humans, animals, plants or their parts in agricultural settings.
[A01N 29/08] Halogen directly attached to a polycyclic ring system -- This subclass covers chemical compounds where a halogen atom is directly bonded to a polycyclic ring system, used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants within agriculture.
[A01N 29/10] Halogen attached to an aliphatic side chain of an aromatic ring system -- A01N 29/10 covers chemical compounds in which a halogen atom is bonded to an aliphatic side chain attached to an aromatic ring system, typically used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators. The class falls under agriculture-related preservation of human, animal or plant bodies.
[A01N 29/12] 1,1-Di- or 1,1,1-tri-halo-2-aryl-ethane or -ethene or derivatives thereof, e.g. DDT -- The A01N 29/12 CPC subgroup covers halogenated hydrocarbon biocides such as 1,1-di- or 1,1,1-tri-halo-2-aryl-ethane or -ethene compounds and their derivatives, including agents like DDT used for pest control, disinfection, or plant-growth regulation. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, agriculture and the preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 3/00] Preservation of plants or parts thereof, e.g. inhibiting evaporation, improvement of the appearance of leaves  -- A01N 3/00 covers methods and substances used to keep plants or plant parts in good condition, such as treatments that reduce water loss, protect against wilting, or enhance leaf colour and appearance. It includes coatings, packaging, and other preservation techniques aimed at extending freshness and visual quality of harvested or stored botanical material.
[A01N 3/02] Keeping cut flowers fresh chemically -- A01N 3/02 pertains to methods and chemical treatments for preserving the freshness of cut flowers, focusing on inhibiting decay and maintaining their aesthetic appeal. This classification is part of a broader category that deals with the preservation of plant parts.
[A01N 3/04] Grafting-wax -- A01N 3/04 pertains to grafting-wax, a protective material applied to plant cuts or graft junctions to seal wounds, limit evaporation and guard against infection, thereby preserving plant parts and improving leaf appearance.
[A01N 31/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic oxygen or sulfur compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients containing oxygen or sulfur atoms attached to a carbon atom by a single bond only, for example aliphatic or aromatic alcohols or ethers.
[A01N 31/02] Acyclic compounds -- A01N 31/02 covers acyclic organic compounds containing oxygen or sulfur that are used as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, or as plant-growth regulators in agricultural applications for preserving human, animal or plant bodies.
[A01N 31/04] Oxygen or sulfur attached to an aliphatic side-chain of a carbocyclic ring system -- A01N 31/04 classifies biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compositions in which oxygen or sulfur atoms are bonded to an aliphatic side-chain attached to a carbocyclic ring system. These organic oxygen or sulfur compounds are used as pest repellents, attractants or preservatives for humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 31/06] Oxygen or sulfur directly attached to a cycloaliphatic ring system -- This subclass includes organic compounds in which an oxygen or sulfur atom is directly attached to a cycloaliphatic ring, used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants, or plant growth regulators within agricultural preservation applications.
[A01N 31/08] Oxygen or sulfur directly attached to an aromatic ring system -- A01N 31/08 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions in which an oxygen or sulfur atom is bonded directly to an aromatic ring system. It falls under the agriculture sub-class for preservation of human, animal or plant bodies using organic oxygen or sulfur compounds.
[A01N 31/10] Pentachlorophenol -- A01N 31/10 identifies pentachlorophenol, an organic oxygen- and sulfur-containing compound employed as a biocide, pest repellent or plant growth regulator for preserving human, animal or plant material in agricultural contexts.
[A01N 31/12] Bis-chlorophenols -- A01N 31/12 designates bis-chlorophenols, organic compounds in which two chlorine atoms are attached to a phenol ring. These substances are classified as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain oxygen-based functional groups, fitting within the broader category of preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 31/14] Ethers -- A01N 31/14 designates ethers that function as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, or as plant growth regulators containing organic oxygen or sulfur groups. These compounds are used in agriculture to preserve bodies of humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 31/16] with two or more oxygen or sulfur atoms directly attached to the same aromatic ring system -- A01N 31/16 classifies biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with at least two oxygen or sulfur atoms directly bonded to the same aromatic ring system. These substances are used in agriculture for their specific chemical properties that affect biological processes or pests.
[A01N 33/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic nitrogen compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients containing nitrogen atoms attached to a carbon atom by a single bond, for example amines or quaternary ammonium compounds.
[A01N 33/02] Amines -- A01N 33/02 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system covers amines used as biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators. These compounds contain organic nitrogen and are specifically classified under the broader category of human necessities, agriculture, and preservation of bodies of humans, animals, or plants.
[A01N 33/04] Nitrogen directly attached to aliphatic or cycloaliphatic carbon atoms -- A01N 33/04 covers biocides, pest repellants, attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic nitrogen compounds where the nitrogen is directly attached to an aliphatic or cycloaliphatic carbon atom. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with the preservation of human, animal, or plant bodies or parts.
[A01N 33/06] Nitrogen directly attached to an aromatic ring system -- A01N 33/06 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic nitrogen compounds where the nitrogen is directly attached to an aromatic ring system. This classification is part of a broader category dealing with the preservation of human, animal, and plant bodies or parts.
[A01N 33/08] containing oxygen or sulfur -- A01N 33/08 is a CPC subclass that classifies biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions in which organic nitrogen compounds also contain oxygen or sulfur, intended for preservation of humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 33/10] having at least one oxygen or sulfur atom directly attached to an aromatic ring system -- A01N 33/10 classifies biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic nitrogen compounds with at least one oxygen or sulfur atom directly bonded to an aromatic ring. This specific structural feature is crucial for the activity of these chemical substances in biological control and agricultural applications.
[A01N 33/12] Quaternary ammonium compounds -- A01N 33/12 covers the use of quaternary ammonium compounds as biocidal agents, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators in agricultural applications. The subclass includes formulations and methods that employ these organic nitrogen-based salts to preserve or protect humans, animals, plants, or their parts.
[A01N 33/14] containing nitrogen-to-halogen bonds -- A01N 33/14 classifies agricultural biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain at least one nitrogen-to-halogen bond. The definition targets organic nitrogen compounds in which nitrogen is directly linked to a halogen atom, used to preserve or protect humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 33/16] containing nitrogen-to-oxygen bonds -- A01N 33/16 pertains to agricultural chemical formulations whose active components contain nitrogen-oxygen bonds, such as nitro, nitroso or N-oxide groups, and are used as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, or plant growth regulators.
[A01N 33/18] Nitro compounds -- A01N 33/18 covers nitro compounds employed as biocides, pest repell
[A01N 33/20] containing oxygen or sulfur attached to the carbon skeleton containing the nitro group -- A01N 33/20 covers agricultural chemicals that contain organic nitrogen compounds with a nitro group in which oxygen or sulfur atoms are directly attached to the carbon skeleton. These compositions are used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators, providing specialized activity through the modified nitro functionality.
[A01N 33/22] having at least one oxygen or sulfur atom and at least one nitro group directly attached to the same aromatic ring system -- A01N 33/22 covers organic-nitrogen biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that feature at least one oxygen or sulfur atom and a nitro group attached directly to the same aromatic ring system. The class is used for compounds such as certain herbicides, fungicides or insecticides where the nitro-substituted aromatic core is combined with other hetero-atoms, defining a specific chemical subgroup within agricultural preservative formulations.
[A01N 33/24] only one oxygen atom attached to the nitrogen atom -- A01N 33/24 classifies agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain an organic nitrogen compound in which the nitrogen atom bears only one oxygen atom, such as N-oxides, hydroxylamines or related mono-oxygenated nitrogen derivatives. This fine-grained subgroup narrows the broader A01N 33 field to substances with a single N-O linkage.
[A01N 33/26] containing nitrogen-to-nitrogen bonds, e.g. azides, diazo-amino compounds, diazonium compounds, hydrazine derivatives -- A01N 33/26 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic nitrogen compounds specifically characterized by nitrogen-to-nitrogen bonds, including azides, diazo-amino compounds, diazonium compounds, and hydrazine derivatives.
[A01N 35/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic compounds containing a carbon atom having two bonds to hetero atoms with at the most one bond to halogen, e.g. aldehyde radical -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 35/02] containing aliphatically bound aldehyde or keto groups, or thio analogues thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group also covers biocides whose antimicrobial action derives from aldehydes generated by these compounds (e.g. formaldehyde releasers like imidazolinylurea or hydantoins). Such compounds are also classified in the corresponding subgroup for the aldehyde generating precursor itself (e.g. hydantoin is classified in both A01N35/02 and A01N43/50 ).
[A01N 35/04] containing aldehyde or keto groups, or thio analogues thereof, directly attached to an aromatic ring system, e.g. acetophenone -- A01N 35/04 classifies agricultural or preservation compositions that contain organic biocidal or plant-growth-regulating agents whose aldehyde, keto or thio-analogue groups are bonded directly to an aromatic ring, such as acetophenone. These formulations are used to control pests, diseases or weeds by exploiting the reactive carbonyl-type functionality attached to the aromatic system.
[A01N 35/06] containing keto or thioketo groups as part of a ring, e.g. cyclohexanone, quinone -- A01N 35/06 covers biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compositions in which the active organic compound contains a keto or thioketo group that is part of a ring, such as cyclohexanone-type structures or quinones, used as pest repellents, attractants or preservatives.
[A01N 35/08] at least one of the bonds to hetero atoms is to nitrogen -- This CPC subclass covers biocidal or plant-growth regulating compounds where the organic molecule contains a carbon atom doubly bonded to hetero atoms and at least one of those hetero atoms is nitrogen. It includes pest repellents, attractants, and preservation agents fitting that structural feature.
[A01N 35/10] containing a carbon-to-nitrogen double bond -- A01N 35/10 covers biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to hetero atoms, specifically featuring a carbon-to-nitrogen double bond. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO for patent documentation.
[A01N 37/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic compounds containing a carbon atom having three bonds to hetero atoms with at the most two bonds to halogen, e.g. carboxylic acids -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 37/02] Saturated carboxylic acids or thio analogues thereof -- A01N 37/02 relates to the use of saturated carboxylic acids or their thio analogues as biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating agents. The compounds are organic molecules in which a carbon atom bears three bonds to hetero atoms and at most two bonds to halogen atoms.
[A01N 37/04] polybasic -- This CPC subclass covers polybasic organic compounds such as carboxylic acids that are employed as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators, falling under agricultural preservation of bodies. The compounds are defined by a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms and having no more than two halogen bonds.
[A01N 37/06] Unsaturated carboxylic acids or thio analogues thereof -- A01N 37/06 covers the use of unsaturated carboxylic acids and their thio analogues as active ingredients in biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators. The classification applies to organic compounds whose carbon atom bears three hetero-atom bonds and at most two halogen bonds, falling under agriculture-related preservation of living organisms.
[A01N 37/08] containing carboxylic groups or thio analogues thereof, directly attached by the carbon atom to a cycloaliphatic ring -- A01N 37/08 covers biocidal, pest-repellent or plant-growth-regulating compositions that include organic molecules with a carboxylic or thio-carboxylic group directly bonded to a cycloaliphatic ring. These chemicals are used for preserving humans, animals or plants by controlling pests or disease, and fall within the agriculture sub-class of the Human Necessities section.
[A01N 37/10] Aromatic or araliphatic carboxylic acids, or thio analogues thereof -- A01N 37/10 refers to the use of aromatic or araliphatic carboxylic acids and their thio analogues as active ingredients in biocidal, pest-repellent, pest-attractant or plant-growth-regulating products. These compounds contain a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms and may have up to two halogen bonds.
[A01N 37/12] containing the group -- A01N 37/12 pertains to biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms, specifically those featuring a carboxylic acid group.
[A01N 37/14] containing the group -- A01N 37/14 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic compounds in which a carbon atom is bonded to three hetero-atoms and to no more than two halogen atoms, such as certain carboxylic acids, used for preserving or influencing the growth of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 37/16] containing the group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Peroxycarboxylic acids.
[A01N 37/18] containing the group —CO—N<, e.g. carboxylic acid amides or imides -- A01N 37/18 relates to biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain an organic moiety where a carbon atom is bonded to three hetero-atoms and to at most two halogen atoms, specifically the -CO-N< group such as carboxylic acid amides or imides. The entry covers formulations where these functional groups provide the active pesticidal or growth-regulating effect.
[A01N 37/20] containing the group -- A01N 37/20 pertains to biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms, specifically those featuring a carboxylic acid group.
[A01N 37/22] the nitrogen atom being directly attached to an aromatic ring system, e.g. anilides -- This CPC subclass covers pesticides, pest repellents or plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds in which a nitrogen atom is attached directly to an aromatic ring, such as anilide structures. It is classified under A01N 37/22 in the CPC hierarchy.
[A01N 37/24] containing at least one oxygen or sulfur atom being directly attached to the same aromatic ring system -- A01N 37/24 covers biocidal or pest-repellent compounds that contain organic molecules where a carbon atom is bonded to three hetero atoms and at most two halogen atoms, and that have at least one oxygen or sulfur atom directly attached to the same aromatic ring system.
[A01N 37/26] containing the group -- A01N 37/26 pertains to biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms, specifically those featuring a carboxylic acid group.
[A01N 37/28] containing the group -- A01N 37/28 pertains to biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms, specifically those featuring a carboxylic acid group.
[A01N 37/30] containing the groups —CO—N< and -- This CPC subclass covers agricultural or preservation uses of organic compounds where a carbon atom is bonded to hetero atoms with at most two halogen bonds, specifically containing the -CO-N< functional group such as certain carboxylic acids used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators.
[A01N 37/32] Cyclic imides of polybasic carboxylic acids or thio analogues thereof -- A01N 37/32 covers cyclic imides derived from polybasic carboxylic acids or their thio analogues, used as active ingredients in biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating formulations. These compounds feature a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms and at most two halogen bonds, placing them in the organic chemistry subclass for carbonyl heterocycles.
[A01N 37/34] Nitriles -- A01N 37/34 is a CPC subclass for nitrile compounds employed as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators. It covers organic molecules in which a carbon atom is bound to three hetero atoms and to no more than two halogen atoms, including related carboxylic acids. This entry falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture, and preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 37/36] containing at least one carboxylic group or a thio analogue, or a derivative thereof, and a singly bound oxygen or sulfur atom attached to the same carbon skeleton, this oxygen or sulfur atom not being a member of a carboxylic group or of a thio analogue, or of a derivative thereof, e.g. hydroxy-carboxylic acids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  In this group as well as in A01N37/38 - A01N37/50 , CN-groups are considered as derivatives of carboxylic acid.  Compounds like Bromoxynil hence are not classified in A01N37/34 , but in the relevant lower subgroup (Bromoxynil A01N37/44 ).
[A01N 37/38] having at least one oxygen or sulfur atom attached to an aromatic ring system -- A01N 37/38 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic molecules with a carbon atom bonded to three heteroatoms and that have at least one oxygen or sulfur atom attached directly to an aromatic ring system. This subclass falls under the preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants within the broader agriculture group of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01N 37/40] having at least one carboxylic group or a thio analogue, or a derivative thereof, and one oxygen or sulfur atom attached to the same aromatic ring system -- A01N 37/40 covers organic biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that possess at least one carboxylic (or thio) group and an additional oxygen or sulfur atom attached to the same aromatic ring, typically as part of a carbon skeleton with up to two halogen bonds. The classification is used for compounds where a carbon atom is bonded to three hetero atoms and contains limited halogen substitution.
[A01N 37/42] containing within the same carbon skeleton a carboxylic group or a thio analogue, or a derivative thereof, and a carbon atom having only two bonds to hetero atoms with at the most one bond to halogen, e.g. keto-carboxylic acids -- A01N 37/42 covers agricultural biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compounds that contain, within the same carbon skeleton, a carboxylic (or thio) group together with a carbon atom bonded to at most two hetero atoms and no more than one halogen, exemplified by keto-carboxylic acids. These substances are used as pest repellents, attractants or preservatives for humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 37/44] containing at least one carboxylic group or a thio analogue, or a derivative thereof, and a nitrogen atom attached to the same carbon skeleton by a single or double bond, this nitrogen atom not being a member of a derivative or of a thio analogue of a carboxylic group, e.g. amino-carboxylic acids -- A01N 37/44 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compounds that contain at least one carboxylic (or thio) group together with a nitrogen atom attached to the same carbon skeleton by a single or double bond, the nitrogen not being part of the carboxylic derivative. Typical examples are amino-carboxylic acids used for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 37/46] N-acyl derivatives -- A01N 37/46 covers nitrogen-acyl derivatives, compounds in which an acyl group is bonded to a nitrogen atom, used as biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating agents. These organic molecules feature a carbon atom linked by three bonds to hetero atoms and at most two bonds to halogen, fitting the broader class of chemical pesticides and preservatives for plants, animals or humans.
[A01N 37/48] Nitro-carboxylic acids -- A01N 37/48 covers nitro-substituted carboxylic acids employed as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and as plant-growth regulators. The compounds feature a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms and contain at most two halogen bonds, exemplified by various nitro-carboxylic acids used in agricultural preservation.
[A01N 37/50] the nitrogen atom being doubly bound to the carbon skeleton -- A01N 37/50 classifies organic biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compounds in which a nitrogen atom is double-bonded to the carbon framework, such as nitriles, imines or related heterocycles, used as pest repellents, attractants or growth regulators.
[A01N 37/52] containing -- A01N 37/52 covers biocides, pest repellants or attractants, and plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom bonded to three hetero atoms, such as carboxylic acids.

[A01N 41/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic compounds containing a sulfur atom bound to a hetero atom -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients comprising sulfur compounds such as sulfonic acids, sulfones or polysulfides.

It falls under the CPC hierarchy for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.

These are organic compounds in which a sulfur atom is bound to a hetero atom, used in agricultural contexts to preserve bodies of humans, animals, plants or their parts.

[A01N 41/10] Sulfones -- A01N 41/10 designates sulfone organic compounds that contain a sulfur atom bonded to a hetero atom and are employed as biocidal agents, pest repellents or attractants, or as plant growth
[A01N 41/12] not containing sulfur-to-oxygen bonds, e.g. polysulfides -- A01N 41/12 covers organic biocidal, pest-repellent or plant-growth-regulating compounds that contain a sulfur atom directly bonded to a hetero-atom but do not have sulfur-oxygen (S-O) bonds; typical examples include polysulfides and related sulfur-rich molecules.
[A01N 43/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing heterocyclic compounds -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Containing compounds of the formula 2 groups -> A01N33/00 – A01N41/12 - Containing cyclic anhydrides, cyclic imides -> A01N37/00 - Containing cyclopropane carboxylic acids or derivatives thereof, e.g. esters having heterocyclic rings -> A01N53/00  **Glossary:** - Hetero ring: Ring having at least one halogen, nitrogen, oxygen or sulfur atom as ring member - Bridged: The presence of at least one fusion other than ortho, peri and spiro - Condensed rings: Two rings that at least have one shared ring member, i.e. spiro and bridged are considered condensed - Condensed ring system: A ring system in which all rings are condensed among themselves - Number of rings: The number of rings in a condensed ring system equals the number of scissions necessary to convert the ring system into one acyclic system. The relevant rings in a condensed system are chosen according to the following consecutive criteria: (i) lowest number of ring members; (ii) highest number of heteroatoms as ring members (ring members shared by 2 or more rings are regarded as being a member of each of these rings).
[A01N 43/02] having rings with one or more oxygen or sulfur atoms as the only ring hetero atoms -- A01N 43/02 pertains to biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that incorporate heterocyclic compounds whose rings contain only oxygen or sulfur atoms as hetero atoms, possibly with one or more of these atoms per ring.
[A01N 43/04] with one hetero atom -- A01N 43/04 classifies agricultural chemicals-biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators-whose active heterocyclic compound contains a single hetero atom, covering inventions that use such one-hetero-atom heterocycles for preserving humans, animals or plants.

It is situated within the agriculture field for preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants, and specifies formulations where the active component includes a five-membered heterocycle.

[A01N 47/30] Derivatives containing the group >N—CO—N aryl or >N—CS—N—aryl -- A01N 47/30 covers organic compounds that are not part of a ring and lack carbon-carbon or carbon-hydrogen bonds, specifically derivatives containing the functional groups >N-CO-N aryl or >N-CS-N aryl. These substances are employed as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 47/32] containing >N—CO—N< or >N—CS—N< groups directly attached to a cycloaliphatic ring -- A01N 47/32 covers agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain >N-CO-N< or >N-CS-N< groups directly attached to a cycloaliphatic ring. The compounds are organic, with a carbon atom outside any ring and no bond to another carbon or hydrogen, such as certain carbonic-acid derivatives used for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 47/34] containing the groups -- A01N 47/34 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a specific structure: a carbon atom not part of a ring and not bonded to carbon or hydrogen, such as derivatives of carbonic acid. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A01N 47/36] containing the group >N—CO—N< directly attached to at least one heterocyclic ring -- A01N 47/36 classifies agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain an organic compound with a carbon atom outside a ring and no carbon-hydrogen bond, specifically those where the functional group -N-CO-N- is directly attached to at least one heterocyclic ring. This falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, agriculture and preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 47/38] containing the group >N—CO—N< where at least one nitrogen atom is part of a heterocyclic ring -- A01N 47/38 classifies biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a specific functional group: >N-CO-N<, where at least one nitrogen atom is part of a heterocyclic ring. These substances are used in agriculture for preservation and protection purposes.
[A01N 47/40] the carbon atom having a double or triple bond to nitrogen, e.g. cyanates, cyanamides -- A01N 47/40 covers organic compounds in which a carbon atom is doubly or triply bonded to nitrogen, such as cyanates and cyanamides. These substances are used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators and belong to the subgroup of non-ring carbon compounds that lack carbon-carbon or carbon-hydrogen bonds.
[A01N 47/42] containing —N=CX -- A01N 47/42 classifies biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic compounds with a carbon atom not part of a ring and not bonded to carbon or hydrogen, specifically those derivatives of carbonic acid that include a -N=CX group.
[A01N 47/44] Guanidine -- A01N 47/44 designates guanidine compounds employed as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain a non-ring carbon atom with no bond to carbon or hydrogen, i.e., derivatives of carbonic acid. This subclass belongs to the CPC hierarchy for preservation of human, animal or plant bodies under the broader category of agriculture.
[A01N 47/46] containing —N=C=S groups -- This subclass covers agricultural chemicals such as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators that contain organic molecules bearing an isothioc
[A01N 47/48] containing —S—C≡N groups -- The subclass A01N 47/48 comprises agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain a sulfanyl-cyanide (-S-C≡N) functional group, i.e. compounds in which a carbon atom outside a ring is bonded to sulfur and a cyano group. This entry falls under the CPC hierarchy for non-ring organic carbon compounds used in preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 49/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators, containing compounds containing the group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients containing an acyclic terpenoid (isoprenoid) structure such as methoprene or geraniol.
[A01N 51/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic compounds having the sequences of atoms O—N—S, X—O—S, N—N—S, O—N—N or O-halogen, regardless of the number of bonds each atom has and with no atom of these sequences forming part of a heterocyclic ring -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients comprising nitroguanidine compounds such as clothianidin or imidacloprid.
[A01N 53/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing cyclopropane carboxylic acids or derivatives thereof -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 55/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators, containing organic compounds containing elements other than carbon, hydrogen, halogen, oxygen, nitrogen and sulfur -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 55/02] containing metal atoms -- A01N 55/02 covers agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators that incorporate metal atoms into their organic active compounds, extending beyond the usual elements of carbon, hydrogen, halogen, oxygen, nitrogen and sulfur. These formulations are used for the preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants and fall under the broader category of human necessities in agriculture.
[A01N 55/04] Tin -- A01N 55/04 covers tin-based compounds employed as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators. These chemicals contain tin as the principal non-organic element, distinguishing them from formulations limited to carbon, hydrogen, halogen, oxygen, nitrogen or sulfur. The subclass is used for agricultural preservation of crops, livestock and other biological materials.
[A01N 55/06] Mercury -- A01N 55/06 relates to the use of elemental mercury or mercury compounds as biocidal agents, pest repellents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators that fall outside the usual organic element composition. It is classified under preservation of humans, animals or plants, covering inorganic compounds containing mercury employed for controlling pests or disease.
[A01N 55/08] containing boron -- A01N 55/08 covers agricultural biocidal or pest-control compositions that incorporate boron as an active element. These organic compounds, which may also contain other non-carbon, hydrogen, halogen, oxygen, nitrogen or sulfur elements, are used to preserve plants, animals or human bodies from pests, diseases or unwanted growth.
[A01N 57/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing organic phosphorus compounds -- In this group, C-Sets are used for classification. The detailed information about the C-Sets construction and the associated syntax rules is found in the Definitions of A01N .
[A01N 57/02] having alternatively specified atoms bound to the phosphorus atom and not covered by a single one of groups -- A01N 57/02 covers biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic phosphorus compounds in which the phosphorus atom is bonded to assorted, individually defined atoms that do not fit into a single predefined group. This subclass addresses formulations where the substituents on phosphorus are specified separately rather than grouped together.
[A01N 57/04] containing acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals -- A01N 57/04 covers agricultural biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compositions in which the organic-phosphorus active ingredient contains acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals, defining a subgroup of formulations used for preserving humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 57/06] containing aromatic radicals -- A01N 57/06 covers organic-phosphorus biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators that incorporate aromatic radicals into their molecular structure. It specifies formulations where the aromatic group forms part of the active ingredient or a key component of the compound.
[A01N 57/08] containing heterocyclic radicals -- A01N 57/08 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators that contain organic phosphorus compounds with heterocyclic radicals. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[A01N 57/10] having phosphorus-to-oxygen bonds or phosphorus-to-sulfur bonds -- A01N 57/10 covers agricultural biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain organic phosphorus compounds featuring phosphorus-oxygen or phosphorus-sulfur bonds, used for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants and for controlling pests and diseases.
[A01N 57/12] containing acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals -- The CPC subclass A01N 57/12 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic phosphorus compounds whose phosphorus atom is linked to acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals. It is situated under the hierarchy for preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants within the broader field of agriculture.
[A01N 57/14] containing aromatic radicals -- A01N 57/14 classifies agricultural or preservation compositions that contain organic-phosphorus compounds bearing aromatic radicals. It applies to biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators in which an aromatic group is attached to the phosphorus-based active ingredient.
[A01N 57/16] containing heterocyclic radicals -- A01N 57/16 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic phosphorus compounds bearing heterocyclic radicals. The class is used for formulations where the phosphorus moiety is linked to ring-structured heteroatoms to enhance biological activity.
[A01N 57/18] having phosphorus-to-carbon bonds -- A01N 57/18 defines agricultural chemicals such as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that contain organic phosphorus compounds where phosphorus is directly bonded to carbon. The subclass is used for formulations aimed at preserving humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 57/20] containing acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals -- A01N 57/20 covers agricultural chemical compositions-biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators-where the organic phosphorus active ingredient incorporates acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals. These formulations are used to preserve or protect humans, animals, plants, or their parts. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for human necessities, agriculture, and preservation of biological bodies.
[A01N 57/22] containing aromatic radicals -- A01N 57/22 classifies agricultural chemicals that are biocides, pest repellents or attractants, or plant-growth regulators composed of organic phosphorus compounds which contain aromatic radicals. The subclass targets formulations used to preserve or influence the bodies of humans, animals, plants or their parts through the presence of these aromatic-substituted phosphorus agents.
[A01N 57/24] containing heterocyclic radicals -- This subcategory covers formulations of organic-phosphorus biocides, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators that incorporate heterocyclic radicals, used for preserving or controlling organisms in agricultural contexts.
[A01N 57/26] having phosphorus-to-nitrogen bonds -- A01N 57/26 classifies agricultural or preservation chemicals that contain organic phosphorus compounds linked directly to nitrogen atoms. The subclass is used for biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that feature phosphorus-to-nitrogen bonds.
[A01N 57/28] containing acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals -- A01N 57/28 covers formulations of biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that incorporate organic phosphorus compounds which bear acyclic or cycloaliphatic radicals, such as linear alkyl or cyclohexyl groups. The classification is used for chemical agents applied in agriculture to protect or preserve crops, livestock or plant material.
[A01N 57/30] containing aromatic radicals -- A01N 57/30 covers formulations of organic-phosphorus biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant growth regulators that incorporate aromatic radicals. It is situated within the CPC hierarchy for preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants in agriculture.
[A01N 57/32] containing heterocyclic radicals -- A01N 57/32 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain organic phosphorus compounds and incorporate heterocyclic radicals. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for agriculture-related preservation of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 57/34] having phosphorus-to-halogen bonds -- This subclass covers pesticide, pest-repellent or plant-regulating compositions that contain organic phosphorus molecules linked directly to a halogen atom such as chlorine, bromine or iodine. The presence of a phosphorus-to-halogen bond defines the chemical structure of these biocidal agents.
