[A01N 59/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing elements or inorganic compounds -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Inorganic halogen compounds (in particular hypochlorite or hypobromite) not covered by subgroups A01N59/02 - A01N59/26 , hydrogen peroxide, silica and carbonates. Halogen releasing compounds (e.g. hydantoin derivatives or trichloroisocyanurate) are classified under A01N59/00 in addition to classification under A01N27/00 - A01N57/00 .
[A01N 59/02] Sulfur -- A01N 59/02 covers the use of elemental sulfur in agriculture as a biocide, pest repellent or attractant, and as a plant-growth regulator. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for human necessities, specifically the preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants, and relates to inorganic compounds applied to protect or influence living organisms.
[A01N 59/04] Carbon disulfide -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This subgroup also covers bicarbonates.
[A01N 59/06] Aluminium -- A01N 59/06 concerns aluminium used in biocides, pest-control agents or attractants, and plant-growth regulators. The code applies to inorganic aluminium compounds that serve as active ingredients or carriers in products intended to preserve humans, animals, or plants.
[A01N 59/08] Alkali metal chlorides -- A01N 59/08 covers the use of alkali-metal chlorides such as sodium or potassium chloride as biocidal, pest-repellent or attractant agents, or as plant-growth regulators, typically in compositions or methods for protecting or treating plants, animals, humans or plant parts.
[A01N 59/10] Fluorides -- A01N 59/10 covers fluoride compounds used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators in agricultural settings. These inorganic substances are applied to protect or preserve humans, animals, plants or their parts from pests, disease or decay.

[A01N 59/18] Mercury -- A01N 59/18 classifies the use of elemental mercury or mercury compounds as biocidal agents, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators in agricultural practice. This subclass falls under preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants within the agriculture group of the CPC.
[A01N 61/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing substances of unknown or undetermined composition, e.g. substances characterised only by the mode of action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients which have no specified or well-defined chemical structure, are comprised of complex mixtures or are only defined by their method of preparation. For example mineral oils ( A01N61/02 ), humic acids or tannins are classified in this group.
[A01N 61/02] Mineral oils -- A01N 61/02 covers mineral oils that are employed as biocidal agents, pest repellents or attractants, or as plant-growth regulators when the exact chemical composition is unknown or only defined by their mode of action, within agricultural preservation of bodies.
[A01N 63/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing microorganisms, viruses, microbial fungi, animals or substances produced by, or obtained from, microorganisms, viruses, microbial fungi or animals, e.g. enzymes or fermentates -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Biocides as defined above which cannot be classified more precisely than according to their origin (animal, microbial fungi, bacteria, yeast or viruses).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Compounds of defined constitution -> A01N27/00 - A01N59/00 - Unicellular algae -> A01N65/03

[A01N 63/60] Isolated nucleic acids -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Biocides containing nucleic acids, which are for instance capable of interfering with the transcription machinery.
[A01N 65/00] Biocides, pest repellants or attractants, or plant growth regulators containing material from algae, lichens, bryophyta, multi-cellular fungi or plants, or extracts thereof -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Active ingredients of undetermined or complex constitution which are derived from plant materials such as extracts (including seed extracts), essential oils or fermentates.
[A01N 65/03] Algae -- A01N 65/03 covers the use of algae, including extracts and derived materials, as active ingredients in biocidal formulations, pest repellents or attractants, and plant growth regulators for preserving or protecting humans, animals, plants or their parts. It falls under the agriculture branch of the CPC system.
[A01N 65/04] Pteridophyta [fern allies] -- A01N 65/04 covers the use of material derived from pteridophytes-ferns and their allies-as biocidal or pest-control agents, including repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators, applied for the preservation of humans, animals or plants and their parts.
[A01N 65/06] Coniferophyta [gymnosperms], e.g. cypress -- A01N 65/06 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain extracts or active substances derived from coniferous gymnosperms such as cypress. It is situated under the broader class for biocides and related agents obtained from algae, lichens, bryophytes, fungi or other plants, used in agricultural preservation of human, animal or plant bodies.
[A01N 65/08] Magnoliopsida [dicotyledons] -- A01N 65/08 covers patents for biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain material derived from dicotyledonous (Magnoliopsida) plants, such as extracts, powders or related substances, used for preserving or protecting humans, animals, plants or their parts.
[A01N 65/10] Apiaceae or Umbelliferae [Carrot family], e.g. parsley, caraway, dill, lovage, fennel or snakebed -- A01N 65/10 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain material derived from members of the Apiaceae (Umbelliferae) family, such as parsley, caraway, dill, lovage, fennel or related species. The entry applies to formulations using extracts, powders or other preparations of these plants for agricultural or preservation purposes.
[A01N 65/12] Asteraceae or Compositae [Aster or Sunflower family], e.g. daisy, pyrethrum, artichoke, lettuce, sunflower, wormwood or tarragon -- A01N 65/12 covers agricultural chemicals-biocides, pest repellents, attractants, or plant growth regulators-derived from or containing extracts of plants belonging to the Asteraceae (Compositae) family, such as daisies, pyrethrum, artichoke, lettuce, sunflower, wormwood or tarragon, for use in preserving or protecting crops, animals or other plant material.
[A01N 65/14] Celastraceae [Staff-tree or Bittersweet family], e.g. spindle tree, bittersweet or thunder god vine -- A01N 65/14 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators derived from the Celastraceae family, including species like spindle tree and bittersweet.
[A01N 65/16] Ericaceae [Heath or Blueberry family], e.g. rhododendron, arbutus, pieris, cranberry or bilberry -- A01N 65/16 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain material derived from members of the Ericaceae family, such as rhododendron, arbutus, pieris, cranberry or bilberry. The entry applies to formulations using extracts, whole organisms or parts of these plants for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants, or for controlling pests and diseases.
[A01N 65/18] Euphorbiaceae [Spurge family], e.g. ricinus [castorbean] -- This CPC subclass covers biocidal or pest-control compositions where the active ingredient is derived from plants of the Euphorbiaceae family, such as castor bean. It includes formulations containing extracts, oils or compounds from these species used as pesticides
[A01N 65/20] Fabaceae or Leguminosae [Pea or Legume family], e.g. pea, lentil, soybean, clover, acacia, honey locust, derris or millettia -- A01N 65/20 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, pest-attractive or plant-growth-regulating formulations that contain extracts or other material derived from plants of the Fabaceae (Leguminosae) family, such as peas, lentils, soybeans, clover, acacia, honey-locust, derris or millettia.
[A01N 65/22] Lamiaceae or Labiatae [Mint family], e.g. thyme, rosemary, skullcap, selfheal, lavender, perilla, pennyroyal, peppermint or spearmint -- A01N 65/22 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators derived from the Lamiaceae family, including species like thyme, rosemary, and lavender. These natural compounds are used for their biological activity in agricultural and horticultural applications.
[A01N 65/24] Lauraceae [Laurel family], e.g. laurel, avocado, sassafras, cinnamon or camphor -- A01N 65/24 covers biocidal or plant-growth regulatory compositions that contain extracts from members of the Lauraceae family such as laurel, avocado, sassafras, cinnamon or camphor. It is classified under human necessities → agriculture → preservation of bodies of humans or animals or plants, focusing on biocides, pest repellents or attractants
[A01N 65/26] Meliaceae [Chinaberry or Mahogany family], e.g. mahogany, langsat or neem -- A01N 65/26 pertains to biocides, pest repellants, attractants, or plant growth regulators derived from the Meliaceae family, which includes trees like mahogany and neem, known for their natural compounds effective in agricultural and horticultural protection.
[A01N 65/28] Myrtaceae [Myrtle family], e.g. teatree or clove -- A01N 65/28 designates compositions that incorporate extracts from the Myrtaceae family, such as tea-tree or clove, and are used as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators for preserving plants, animals or humans.
[A01N 65/30] Polygonaceae [Buckwheat family], e.g. red-knees or rhubarb -- This CPC subclass A01N 65/30 covers biocidal or plant-growth-regulator compositions that contain material derived from the Polygonaceae family, such as buckwheat, red-knees or rhubarb. These formulations are used for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 65/32] Ranunculaceae [Buttercup family], e.g. hepatica, hydrastis or goldenseal -- A01N 65/32 concerns biocidal or plant-growth-regulating compositions that use material from members of the Ranunculaceae (Buttercup) family, such as hepatica, hydrastis or goldenseal, whether as whole extracts or isolated constituents. The subclass falls under the broader category of preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants using natural algae, lichens, bryophytes, fungi or plant-derived substances.
[A01N 65/34] Rosaceae [Rose family], e.g. strawberry, hawthorn, plum, cherry, peach, apricot or almond -- A01N 65/34 pertains to chemical or biological agents such as biocides, pest repellents, attractants or plant-growth regulators that are derived from members of the rose family, for example strawberry, hawthorn, plum, cherry, peach, apricot or almond. It falls under the broader category of preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants using material sourced from algae, lichens, fungi or plants.
[A01N 65/36] Rutaceae [Rue family], e.g. lime, orange, lemon, corktree or pricklyash -- A01N 65/36 covers the use of extracts or other material derived from plants of the Rutaceae family-such as lime, orange, lemon, cork-tree or prickly-ash-in biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating formulations. It falls under preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants within the agriculture segment of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A01N 65/38] Solanaceae [Potato family], e.g. nightshade, tomato, tobacco or chilli pepper -- A01N 65/38 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain material derived from members of the Solanaceae family, such as nightshade, tomato, tobacco or chilli pepper. It falls within the CPC hierarchy for agriculture and the preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants, specifically addressing extracts from algae, lichens, bryophytes, fungi or higher plants used in pest control.
[A01N 65/385] Tobacco -- A01N 65/385 classifies tobacco when it is employed as a biocide, pest repellent, attractant or plant growth regulator that contains material derived from algae, lichens, bryophyta, multi-cellular fungi or plants, used in agricultural preservation of human, animal or plant bodies.
[A01N 65/40] Liliopsida [monocotyledons] -- A01N 65/40 covers biocidal or pest-repellent compositions, attractants and plant-growth regulators that contain material derived from algae, lichens, bryophytes, multicellular fungi or higher plants, specifically formulated for use on monocotyledons (Liliopsida). It falls within the agriculture subclass of human necessities, dealing with preservation of bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 65/42] Aloeaceae [Aloe family] or Liliaceae [Lily family], e.g. aloe, veratrum, onion, garlic or chives -- A01N 65/42 relates to biocidal or pest-repellent compositions that incorporate extracts from the Aloeaceae (aloe) or Liliaceae (lily) families, including plants such as veratrum, onion, garlic or chives, for the control of harmful organisms.
[A01N 65/44] Poaceae or Gramineae [Grass family], e.g. bamboo, lemon grass or citronella grass -- A01N 65/44 pertains to biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating formulations that incorporate material or extracts derived from the Poaceae (grass) family, including bamboo, lemongrass and citronella grass. These compositions are used in agricultural preservation of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 65/46] Stemonaceae [Stemona family], e.g. croomia -- A01N 65/46 covers biocidal, pest-repellent, attractant or plant-growth-regulating compositions that contain material derived from algae, lichens, bryophytes, multicellular fungi or plants, specifically extracts of the Stemonaceae (Stemona) family such as Croomia, used for preserving bodies of humans, animals or plants.
[A01N 65/48] Zingiberaceae [Ginger family], e.g. ginger or galangal -- The CPC subclass A01N 65/48 covers compositions that contain extracts or derivatives of plants belonging to the Zingiberaceae family, such as ginger or galangal, when these are employed as biocides, pest repellents or attractants, or as plant-growth regulators. It resides within the broader group of biocidal or pest-control agents derived from algae, lichens, fungi or higher plants.
[A01P] BIOCIDAL, PEST REPELLANT, PEST ATTRACTANT OR PLANT GROWTH REGULATORY ACTIVITY OF CHEMICAL COMPOUNDS OR PREPARATIONS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The use of compounds or preparations thereof for regulating plant growth, for pest control or for biocidal activity.  Biocidal activity and pest control includes the treatment of plants and inanimate surfaces such as materials, equipment and furniture that are not used for direct contact with food or feeding stuffs. Also included is disinfection and pest control for general hygiene purposes of animate surfaces of humans or animals wherein the effect is not intended to be medical or cosmetic, for example the treatment of parasites, lice, or ticks on humans or animals.  Pest control also includes repellants such as those used on animal or human skin.  **Glossary:** - Acaricide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying mites and ticks or making them less harmful. - Arthropodicide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying arthropods, e.g. insects, myriapods, arachnids, crustaceans or making them less harmful. - Biocide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing, destroying, or mitigating any living organism (e.g., plant, animal). Examples of a biocide are: acaricide, arthropodicide, fungicide, insecticide, molluscicide, rodenticide. - Disinfectant: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing, destroying, or mitigating microorganisms. - Fungicide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying moulds and fungi or making them less harmful. - Herbicide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying plant life or making it less harmful. - Insecticide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying insects or making them less harmful. - Molluscicide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying molluscs, e.g. snails, clams or making them less harmful. - Pesticide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying any pest (e.g., insects, rodents) or making it less harmful. - Plant: Any of a kingdom (Plantae) of multicellular eukaryotic mostly photosynthetic organisms typically lacking locomotive movement or obvious nervous or sensory organs and possessing cellulose cell walls. - Plant Growth Regulator: Materials which alter the plant or may affect plant growth through a chemical modification of the plant metabolism, such as auxins. - Rodenticide: Any substance or mixture of substances intended for preventing or destroying rodents, e.g. rats, mice or making them less harmful.
[A01P 1/00] Disinfectants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof as disinfectants, having for instance an antimicrobial activity. Disinfectant activity includes disinfection of inanimate surfaces such as materials, equipment and furniture that are not used for direct contact with food or feeding stuffs. Also included is disinfection of animate surfaces including of plants, and for general hygiene purposes of humans or animals wherein the effect is not intended to be medical or cosmetic.
[A01P 11/00] Rodenticides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against rodents, like mice or rats.
[A01P 13/00] Herbicides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof for controlling unwanted plants or algae.
[A01P 13/02] selective -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof for selectively controlling unwanted plants or algae usually in the presence of desired plants and algae.
[A01P 15/00] Biocides for specific purposes not provided for in groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof for controlling unwanted growth of living organisms (e.g. plant or animal or microorganism) not specifically covered by A01P1/00 - A01P13/00 .
[A01P 17/00] Pest repellants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having a repulsive activity against pests.
[A01P 19/00] Pest attractants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof capable of attracting pests.
[A01P 21/00] Plant growth regulators -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof capable of modifying the growth of plants, including those that chemically modify plant metabolism, such as auxins.
[A01P 23/00] Chemosterilants -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of chemical compounds that cause reproductive sterility in a pest organism.
[A01P 3/00] Fungicides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having anti-fungal or mold destroying activity.
[A01P 5/00] Nematocides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against nematodes.
[A01P 7/00] Arthropodicides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against arthropods, e.g. insects, myriapods, arachnids or crustaceans.
[A01P 7/02] Acaricides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against ticks and mites.
[A01P 7/04] Insecticides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against insects.
[A01P 9/00] Molluscicides -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This group covers the use of compounds or preparations thereof having an activity against molluscs, e.g. snails, slugs, clams.
